markdown:
[Skip to content](https://www.ucdenver.edu/about-cu-denver/our-history#CUMain)

## A History Spanning More Than 50 Years

Located on the downtown Auraria Campus and stretching into Denver’s central business district, the University of Colorado Denver educates 15,000 students in the heart of an emerging global city. Part of the [University of Colorado system](https://www.cu.edu/about-cu-system?_ga=2.198221871.1449492668.1588702111-627714767.1588702111&_gac=1.18150475.1586808742.Cj0KCQjwm9D0BRCMARIsAIfvfIakqGT2NJ3o-yVndLuxSeNevi1VDZvGYzQLUYSBgrmd1ZNgMK4roCEaAhlyEALw_wcB), CU Denver was officially founded in 1973. Throughout our history we have supported students from all walks of life, and we are committed to being a [university that works for all](http://ucdenver.edu/2030).

### Our Origins

CU Denver is located on the traditional territories and ancestral homelands of the Cheyenne, Arapaho, and Ute nations. We acknowledge the painful history of genocide and forced removal from this territory and pay our respects to the diverse Indigenous Peoples still connected to this land. We give thanks to all Tribal Nations and the ancestors of this place and have developed a formal [Land Acknowledgement](https://news.ucdenver.edu/this-land-is-their-land-universities-and-indigenous-acknowledgment-statements/) as one way to honor this past.

Our recent history acknowledges the [Auraria neighborhood—](https://news.ucdenver.edu/honoring-displaced-aurarians-and-their-families-now-and-into-the-future/) composed largely of Hispanic community members—that was dismantled in the 1970s to construct the Auraria Higher Education Campus (AHEC) of which CU Denver is a part. CU Denver is committed to honoring the displaced Aurarians in multiple ways, including through the expanded Displaced Aurarian Scholarship Program and a commitment to [revitalize historic Ninth Street Park](https://ucdenver.edu/about-cu-denver/cudenveronninth).

### Honoring Our Past

CU Denver continues to acknowledge its past and that of Colorado’s — from its ancestral lands and Hispanic heritage to its modern-day operation as a premier minority-serving public research institution making education work for all.

1800s - Early 1900s

### Gold Sparks a New Neighborhood

In 1947, CU Denver was located in the Fraternal Building at 14th and Glenarm.

The history of Auraria is one of the oldest of modern-day Denver. In the years leading up to the 1800s, this land was the home of the Cheyenne, Ute, and Arapaho. Auraria, or aurum, is Latin for “gold,” which was found in the Cherry Creek in 1858, igniting a small gold rush that established the Auraria settlement. Houses, businesses, and places of worship were built in the Auraria neighborhood over the next 50 years, and the neighborhood evolved over time to become largely Hispanic.

The Tivoli Brewery, built in 1870 in the Auraria neighborhood, operated for nearly 100 years before closing in 1966. Restored and opened in 1994 as the Auraria Campus Student Union. Today the Tivoli Student Union houses offices, study areas, the campus bookstore, restaurants, and a revived brewery.

1912 - 1960s

### CU Expands in Denver

In 1977, CU Denver students began taking classes on the Auraria Campus, alongside students from Metropolitan State University of Denver and Community College of Denver.

CU Denver originated in 1912, when the University of Colorado’s Department of Correspondence and Extension was established to meet the needs of Denver’s growing population. Holding classes in buildings across Speer Boulevard from the Auraria neighborhood–including in the Frontier Hotel’s bar–the institution became known as Denver’s “UCLA” (the University of Colorado between Lawrence and Arapahoe Streets).

As course offerings expanded, the Denver Extension Center was renamed the University of Colorado Denver Center in 1965. It was an institution that at its root helped nontraditional, working students pursue degrees, build skills, or simply enrich their lives near where they lived and worked. Demand grew and by 1969, 23 fields of undergraduate study and 11 of graduate study were offered. A Colorado constitutional amendment established CU Denver as an independent institution in 1973.

1970s

### A Neighborhood Displaced

The geographical area is bounded by Colfax, Auraria Parkway, Speer, and I-25.

During the early 1970s, the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development designated the Auraria neighborhood an urban renewal area and Denver voters approved a referendum and bond issue to build the Auraria Campus. In a unique arrangement, three institutions of higher education–CU Denver, Metropolitan State University Denver, and the Community College of Denver–would be housed on one campus.

By 1974, most of the buildings in the Auraria neighborhood were condemned and razed.

A well-established, close-knit, largely Hispanic community of more than 300 households was displaced in the name of urban development. Thirteen cottages and one grocery store were preserved. Today they make up the 9th Street historic park on campus, the oldest restored block of residences in the city.

The university began the [Displaced Aurarian Scholarship program](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/scholarships/displaced-aurarian#:~:text=The%20Displaced%20Aurarian%20Scholarship%20is,to%20descendants%20of%20these%20residents.) in the 1990s to provide tuition and fees for former residents of the Auraria neighborhood and their children and grandchildren. The university expanded the program in 2021 as part of a long-term effort to honor and support the displaced Aurarians and acknowledge the long-term impacts the taking of their homes has had on them, their families, and their livelihoods.

1980s – Today

### Auraria and CU Denver Today

CU Denver is part of the Auraria Higher Education Center, a 150-acre tri-institutional setting that also includes Metropolitan State University of Denver and Community College of Denver.

The establishment of this unique, innovative, tri-institutional campus, while carrying a difficult history, has made it possible for hundreds of thousands of people to improve their own lives through an affordable, high-quality education. Today the Auraria Campus serves more than 40,000 students across all three institutions.

CU Denver has grown, as well. As the most diverse research university in Colorado, CU Denver today draws top students each year and offers over [110 undergraduate and graduate degree programs](https://www.ucdenver.edu/academics) across eight schools and colleges, as well as more than [30 online degree programs and numerous certificate and non-degree programs.](https://online.cu.edu/)

Home to more than 40 research centers and institutes, the campus receives sponsored research awards annually to generate knowledge and create solutions to society’s most complex problems. CU Denver fills a singular niche as a vital contributor to the civic, cultural, and economic success of the city, the state, and beyond.

In more recent years:

- Downtown Denver has flourished, and CU Denver’s geographic footprint has expanded
- We opened the [the Lola and Rob Salazar Student Wellness Center](https://news.ucdenver.edu/lola-rob-salazar-student-wellness-center-opens-july-11/)
- We introduced club and intramural sports
- We built our first freshmen dorm in [City Heights and a companion Learning Commons](https://news.ucdenver.edu/new-city-heights-residence-hall-and-learning-commons-opens/) facility for teaching and learning
- We launched an ambitious [2030 Strategic Plan](https://news.ucdenver.edu/work-for-all-how-cu-denver-will-become-a-leading-public-urban-research-university-by-2030/) that aims to make education work for all
- We announced the planned revitalization of [historic Ninth Street Park](https://www.denverpost.com/2022/03/31/auraria-campus-ninth-street-historic-district-renovations/) and the construction of a brand new [engineering, design, and computing building](https://news.ucdenver.edu/new-engineering-design-and-computing-building-will-serve-as-anchor-for-innovation-district-outlined-in-2030-strategic-plan/), the anchor of our forthcoming innovation district

## Toward a Brighter, More Equitable Future. Together.

CU Denver has created an ambitious [2030 Strategic Plan](http://ucdenver.edu/2030) to be a public urban research university that works for all: learners of all kinds and at all stages of life, industries and employers that need talent ready to hit the ground running, and communities in need of new solutions and discoveries.

# Our Impact

CU Denver positively affects the economy of the Denver metro area and the state of Colorado. Our education, research, and public service activities benefit students and alumni, as well as nonprofit, government, and private companies.

Economic Impact

$665 Million

Through research, teaching, operations, construction, and student spending

## Significant Financial Force

CU Denver boasts a large percentage of minority and first-generation college students (defined as students of parent/s with no bachelor’s degree), successfully narrowing the college achievement gap. # Visit CU Denver

Are you ready to see where university learning connects with city experience? Explore the University
of Colorado Denver's campus virtually or in-person, schedule a personalized academic visit, or
attend one of our admissions events. We're proud of our campus and its place in the Mile High City.
We're excited to show you everything that's possible here.

To visit CU Denver, choose from the options below. If you have any questions, call 303-315-2601
and speak with our helpful staff.

## CU Denver Tours & Virtual Sessions

There's no better way to experience our dynamic campus than visiting it for yourself. Explore CU Denver's campus in person or virtually to learn more about the admissions process, our position in the city, our programs, and a chance to learn first-hand why students choose to live and learn here.

* * *

### Transfer Thursday Sessions

If you’ve taken college classes, learn about transferring your credits.

[Transfer Thursday Sessions](https://application.admissions.ucdenver.edu/portal/transferthursday)

### K-12 Group Visits

We welcome your students for a fun and informative visit.

[K-12 Group Visits](https://application.admissions.ucdenver.edu/portal/schedule-group-visit)

## Admissions Events & Other Experiences

Experience the vibrant heartbeat of CU Denver's campus by joining one of our engaging events. These carefully curated experiences offer an exceptional opportunity for students and groups to immerse themselves in the unique spirit of the University of Colorado Denver. Discover our campus, connect with fellow future Lynx, and delve into what makes our community thrive. Don't miss out—register for an upcoming event!

# Location & Maps

CU Denver is located in the heart of downtown Denver—Colorado's capital and biggest city. From our campus, you can see both the Rocky Mountains and the city skyline. For more information about our place in the Mile High City, please visit [Life in Denver](https://www.ucdenver.edu/life/living-on-around-campus/life-in-denver).

## Auraria Campus

In addition to our presence in downtown Denver, CU Denver shares the Auraria Campus with two other separate and distinct institutions of higher learning. For more information about this tri-institutional academic environment, please visit the [Auraria Campus](https://www.ucdenver.edu/life/living-on-around-campus/the-auraria-campus) page.

# Summer Camps

![Children sitting at summer camp](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/default-source/university-website-images/cu-denver-summer-camp-hero.jpg?sfvrsn=7ccaefb4_0)

## Looking for an opportunity to get ahead this summer?

With industry-specific topics and real-world experience, our summer camps are far from ordinary. We offer students of all ages a chance to expand their learning with fun, engaging opportunities for exploration. With science, tech, engineering, art, and math (STEAM) camps, we’ve got a complete spectrum of learning programs for K-12 students. Check out this year’s camps and find more details below!

## School & College Camps

[Learn More at the College of Architecture and Planning site](https://architectureandplanning.ucdenver.edu/academics/high-school-programs/architecture-in-the-city)

![pen resting on architectural drawing](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/default-source/offices-ucomm-images/architecture-camp-400-250.png?sfvrsn=8897edb4_0)

### Architecture in the City

The Architecture in the City Virtual Summer Camp is a 3-day virtual program for high school students interested in exploring architecture and design. Students will receive an introduction to the field of architecture through a series of interactive architectural and design lessons from CU Denver’s College of Architecture and Planning faculty. Students will apply these design ideas and methods to create their own models at home.

[Learn More](https://architectureandplanning.ucdenver.edu/academics/high-school-programs/architecture-in-the-city)

[Learn More at the College of Engineering site](https://engineering.ucdenver.edu/bioengineering/bolt-camp)

![decorative image of tools](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/default-source/offices-ucomm-images/engineering-camp-400-250.png?sfvrsn=3c97edb4_0)

### BOLT

BOLT is a camp for high school students that teaches about biomedical engineering and some of the exciting career options available for STEM-focused students. Though the experience will be different than the in-person camp the Department of Bioengineering typically hosts at CU Anschutz, virtual BOLT will still be filled with interesting topics and activities designed to introduce high school students to biomedical engineering and leadership topics.

[View Camp Information](https://engineering.ucdenver.edu/bioengineering/bolt-camp)

[Lean More about the GenCyber Camp](https://www.ucdenver.edu/gencyber)

![decorative image of code](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/default-source/offices-ucomm-images/business-camp-400-250.png?sfvrsn=a9e8edb4_0)

### GenCyber Camp

The GenCyber Summer Camp program at the CU Denver Business School provides exciting, hands-on cybersecurity experiences for students at the K-12 level. Students will explore various aspects of cybersecurity with hands-on exercises and games, including ethical hacking, social engineering, computer and digital forensics, networking fundamentals, cyber threats, cybersecurity careers, ethics in cybersecurity, and online safety.

[Learn More](https://www.ucdenver.edu/gencyber)

![Kids playing instruments on stage](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/default-source/offices-ucomm-images/lynxcamps2-345.jpeg?sfvrsn=42e8edb4_0)

### LYNX Arts Camps

The LYNX National Arts and Media Camps are summer immersion programs for high school students interested in music and the arts hosted CU Denver and sponsored by the CU Denver College of Arts & Media. The camps are designed to provide students with an educational preview of the college programs offered in the College of Arts & Media while also providing students with an inspiring and memorable summer camp experience.

[LYNX Arts Camps Sessions](https://artsandmedia.ucdenver.edu/prospective-students/lynx-camps)

In This Section

# Transportation & Parking

It's important to know where you're going. CU Denver is located in downtown Denver and on the tri-institutional [Auraria Campus](https://www.ucdenver.edu/life/living-on-around-campus/the-auraria-campus). Visitors who drive to campus can rely on metered street parking or any of the covered parking garages or uncovered parking lots. With buildings on both sides of Speer Boulevard, it's helpful to look at parking options in advance to find the spot that's closest to your destination.

For a full list of options and parking locations, please visit the Auraria Higher Education Center’s [parking website](https://www.ahec.edu/services-departments/parking/parking-lot-options). Flat rate and hourly fee options are available. Fees vary slightly depending on location.

The Auraria Campus [Parking Map](https://aurariacampus.edu/wp-content/uploads/Parking-Map.pdf) can help you find the most convenient parking location for your destination.

## Directions to Campus

From I-25, exit Speer Boulevard south. Our campus is bounded by Speer Boulevard, Auraria Parkway, and Colfax Avenue. Tours start at Undergraduate Admissions, located in the Student Commons Building (across the hall from Qdoba) at 1201 Larimer
Street, Suite 1005, Denver, CO 80204.

If traveling by light rail, CU Denver is accessible from the Auraria West Campus Station.

## Campus Tour Parking

Please park in the Spruce Parking Lot, located at 800 Walnut Street, Denver, CO 80204. You will receive a parking validation card when you check in for your tour.

![RTD Lightrail at Denver Union Station](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/default-source/sjwe-images/t-and-p---student-parkingv2.png?sfvrsn=28e719b9_4)

## Discover and Design

In This Section

# Academics

Discover the benefits of CU Denver. We are the only public urban research university in Colorado. Our campus in downtown Denver enables our faculty and students to work on groundbreaking research, often focused on solving urban issues. Our academic programs
bring together current research, creative work, and civic engagement in classes taught by dedicated professors.

Design your educational goals. Choose from academic degrees and programs at all levels. Our courses serve students who want to earn [bachelor’s](https://www.ucdenver.edu/academics/degrees-programs/undergraduate-degrees),
[master's](https://www.ucdenver.edu/academics/degrees-programs/graduate-degrees), and [doctoral degrees](https://www.ucdenver.edu/academics/degrees-programs/graduate-degrees) who
want to learn something new for personal or professional development. Additionally, we offer certificate programs to help you advance your career.

18:1

Less than 20 students in over 1/3 of undergraduate classes

## [This is Our Place](https://www.ucdenver.edu/theplace)

# Creating the Next Generation of Tech Talent

Computing-related skills are the future of everything, not just STEM. Making higher education work for all starts with giving students the computing skills they need to succeed.

## Cracking the Code to Social Mobility While Meeting Workforce Needs

Computing skills are increasingly important, not just for landing a job but for building a sustainable and rewarding career. Over the next 10 years, Colorado will add 5.7M new jobs — most of which will require computing skills. With a diverse student
population and home in one of America’s hotbeds of innovation, CU Denver is uniquely positioned to capitalize on the opportunity to meet a growing workforce need.

By infusing computing into its curriculum, not just in STEM-related programs but in arts and media, business, and healthcare, [CU Denver can further promote social mobility](https://news.ucdenver.edu/cracking-the-code-to-social-mobility-by-infusing-tech-skills-into-cu-denvers-curriculum/) — and become known as a university for life.

## Changing the Face of Computing

* * *

In March, the Interdisciplinary Computing Task Force published a report – “Computing Across the Curriculum” – that includes recommendations for how the university can make computing skills a core part of the university’s
academic programs.

Guided by the task force’s report, the university will work to deliver computing skills to more students by:

- Closing the gap in internet and device access for students of color
- Increasing access to computing-related apprenticeships and internships
- Integrating computing and data ethics into existing and new courses
- Creating collaboration spaces for students that offer technological support
- Forming partnerships to gain better insights into the capabilities of incoming students and future workforce needs

To position CU Denver at the cutting edge of computing education, the university will pursue six “high-value projects” over the next three years. The recommendations include developing:

- A virtual hub of computing and technology strategy
- Sets of computational competencies related to in-demand workforce skills
- Innovative academic programming to expand upon these competencies
- Comprehensive support and experiential learning opportunities for students

CU Denver’s faculty, staff, and graduate students will play a direct role in helping the university realize its computing ambitions, as well as in the university’s technology infrastructure.

## Computing Skills Where You Might Not Expect to Find Them

At CU Denver, computing is core to many of our courses and programs, including many you might not expect. Below is a sample of computing-related offerings within our schools and colleges.

### College of Arts and Media

- [Animation Foundations](https://catalog.ucdenver.edu/cu-denver/undergraduate/schools-colleges-departments/college-arts-media/visual-arts/3d-graphics-animation-bfa/)
- [Intro. to Digital Photography](https://artsandmedia.ucdenver.edu/areas-of-study/visual-arts/photography)
- [Mastering & Advanced Digital Audio](https://artsandmedia.ucdenver.edu/areas-of-study/music-entertainment-industry/recording-arts/recording-arts-ms)

### School of Business

- [Business Intelligence and Financial Modeling](https://business.ucdenver.edu/ms/business-analytics)
- [Designing for the Web](https://business.ucdenver.edu/bsba/information-systems#curriculum-871)
- [Intro. to Python](https://business.ucdenver.edu/bsba/information-systems)

### Engineering, Design & Computing

- [3D Modeling for Bioengineers](https://engineering.ucdenver.edu/bioengineering/3d-printing-services)
- [Transportation Engineering](https://engineering.ucdenver.edu/civil-engineering/research-specialty-areas/transportation-engineering)
- [Engineering Design Innovation](https://catalog.ucdenver.edu/cu-denver/undergraduate/schools-colleges-departments/college-engineering-design-computing/inworks/human-centered-design-innovation-undergraduate-certificate/#text)

### College of Liberal Arts and Sciences

- [AI in Chemistry and Biochemistry](https://clas.ucdenver.edu/chemistry/)
- [Economic Forecasting](https://clas.ucdenver.edu/economics/programs/master-arts-economics)
- [Research Methods in Psychology](https://clas.ucdenver.edu/psychology/undergraduate-studies/bachelor-arts-and-bachelor-sciences/bachelor-arts-psychology)

### School of Public Affairs

- [Public Administration](https://catalog.ucdenver.edu/cu-denver/graduate/courses-a-z/puad/)
- [Statistics for Criminal Justice](https://publicaffairs.ucdenver.edu/programs/criminal-justice-programs)

* * *

## Cultivating Tech at Our New Engineering, Design and Computing Building

We’re moving forward with plans for a [new engineering, design and computing building](https://www.ucdenver.edu/about-cu-denver/our-campus-community/anchor-of-engineering) that will serve as home to the College of Engineering, Design
and Computing and interdisciplinary computing initiatives, and which will anchor the university’s open innovation district.

* * *

Learn more about how computing can help us make higher education work for all.

# Certificates & Continuing Education

Professionals who want to advance their careers or learn a new skill can earn a certificate from CU Denver. Our certificate programs provide specialized information delivered by knowledgeable professors. Additionally, our continuing education classes
help you stay current with developments in your field.

Certificate programs are offered by the [College of Liberal Arts and Sciences](https://clas.ucdenver.edu/certificates), the [School of Education & Human Development](https://education.ucdenver.edu/continuing-education/certificates),
the [School of Public Affairs](https://publicaffairs.ucdenver.edu/programs/professional-development), and the [College of Engineering, Design and Computing](https://engineering.ucdenver.edu/academics/certificate-programs).
Ranging from Construction Project Management to Spanish for International Business, CU Denver provides a diverse range of certificate program topics.

> We provide decision makers with information on the impact of disaster as quickly and efficiently as possible to help them respond with staff and supplies. We’re using cutting-edge technology to help people.-Jesse Rozelle,BA, Certificate in Geographic Information, Science Risk Analyst/GIS Coordinator, FEMA

In This Section

# Non-Degree Programs

CU Denver knows there are people who want to learn so they can enrich their lives. This is why we offer non-degree programs. Students in non-degree programs take the same classes as degree-seeking students, taught by our knowledgeable professors. Non-degree
programs work for people who want to take a course for professional development, personal interest, or simple curiosity. It is also a great way for students enrolled at other institutions to earn extra credit by taking summer classes at CU Denver.

Non-degree programs are also a good way to discover if CU Denver is right for you. Students who first attend non-degree programs may ultimately decide to apply for traditional degree-granting undergraduate or graduate admission.

## About Classes

Once admitted to CU Denver, non-degree students can take many classes listed in the [Course Catalog](http://catalog.ucdenver.edu/). There are also specific non-degree programs at various CU Denver schools.

### Graduate Programs

CU Denver has several certificate programs on a non-degree basis.

In This Section

# Schools & Colleges

CU Denver is organized into seven schools and colleges based on their areas of study and research. Each provides students unique opportunities to develop and learn alongside exceptional faculty. Together, CU Denver’s seven schools and colleges
offer 112 different academic degrees and hundreds of certificate and specialty programs.

But depth of learning matters just as much as breadth of choice. Noteworthy examples include several nationally ranked programs in our School of Public Affairs, as well as the pivotal investigations conducted by the National Center
for Media Forensics within our College of Arts & Media. Our College of Architecture and Planning is the only school in the state that offers accredited programs in architecture, landscape architecture, and urban planning.

### College of Architecture and Planning

For students looking to earn accredited degrees in architecture, landscape architecture, and urban planning

[College of Architecture and Planning](https://architectureandplanning.ucdenver.edu/)

### College of Arts & Media

For students who want to focus on the arts, with degrees in film and television, media forensics, music, and visual arts

[College of Arts & Media](https://artsandmedia.ucdenver.edu/)

### Business School

For students who want to focus on all aspects of business, including accounting, finance, human resources, information systems, and health administration

[Business School](https://business.ucdenver.edu/)

### School of Education & Human Development

For students who want to pursue teaching, educational leadership, and mental health careers in counseling and school psychology

[School of Education & Human Development](https://education.ucdenver.edu/)

### College of Engineering, Design and Computing

For students interested in the fields of bioengineering, computer science, as well as civil, electrical, or mechanical engineering

[College of Engineering, Design and Computing](https://engineering.ucdenver.edu/)

### College of Liberal Arts and Sciences

For students seeking degrees in the arts or sciences, from English and psychology to chemistry and public health

[College of Liberal Arts and Sciences](https://clas.ucdenver.edu/)

## [Campus Accessibility](https://www.ucdenver.edu/accessibility)

# CU Denver Campus Accessibility

We strive to ensure that people with disabilities have equal, available and reliable access to the organization’s services, digital content, and technologies. Our promise encompasses our offerings made available through both technology and our people.

* * *

## Digital Accessibility Statement

* * *

The University of Colorado Denver is committed to ongoing accessibility initiatives aligned with the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) version 2.1, levels A and AA criteria. These efforts aim to enhance accessibility for users with sensory, cognitive, and mobility disabilities, fostering an inclusive online environment for all individuals. The university's commitment to equal access to IT has been more formally stated through the publication of an [IT Digital Accessibility Policy](https://www.ucdenver.edu/docs/librariesprovider284/default-document-library/5000-information-technology/5012.pdf?sfvrsn=1474bbb9_3).

## Guidelines & Resources

* * *

### Digital Accessibility Guidelines & Resources

CU Denver fully supports Colorado law, which emphasizes digital accessibility for all individuals. Resources and information regarding digital accessibility — including websites, applications and documents — are available to ensure that everyone can access and use university digital content and services.

[Learn More About Digital Accessibility](https://www.ucdenver.edu/accessibility/digital-accessibility)

## Report a Barrier

* * *

### Web Accessibility

If you experience a barrier that affects your ability to access information or edit parts of the website, please send the web team a detailed report.

[Report an Inaccessible Website](mailto:websitehelp@ucdenver.edu)

### Online Course Content & Technology

Accessible technology includes online course content, electronic documents, websites, videos, software applications, and hardware devices that can be used effectively by everyone, including people with disabilities.

[Report Inaccessible Technology](mailto:oit-servicedesk@ucdenver.edu)

### Physical Barrier

If you encounter physical ADA barriers on campus, please report them; accessible parking is available in nearly all Auraria Campus lots for vehicles with a state-issued or campus-issued hangtag.

[Report a Physical ADA Issue](mailto:DC_Facilities_Dispatch@ucdenver.edu)

### Discrimination or Harassment

The Office of Equity (OE) provides resources and support, as appropriate, to ensure all involved parties have access to a safe and inclusive working and academic environment.

[Report Discrimination or Harassment](https://cm.maxient.com/reportingform.php?UnivofColoradoDenver&layout_id=2)

## ADA Accommodations

* * *

### Accommodations for Students

CU Denver encourages students with disabilities to talk with the Office of Disability Resources & Services to identify any needs and accommodations.

[Office of Disability Resources & Services](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/disability-resources-and-services/cu-denver-disability-resources-and-services-home)

### Accommodations for Employees

The university actively promotes the use of appropriate accommodations for faculty, staff, and campus visitors with disabilities. These accommodations are tailored to individual needs and provided on a case-by-case basis.

[Learn More About ADA Compliance](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/human-resources/employee-relations-performance/ada-compliance)

### Parking & Transportation

Accessible parking for students, staff and visitors is available in nearly all Auraria Campus parking lots. Parking spaces are marked and reserved for vehicles that have a state-issued or campus-issued hangtag.

[Accessible Parking on the Auraria Campus](https://aurariacampus.edu/services-departments/parking/accessible-parking)

### Including Students and Faculty Who Are Disabled

Information for faculty on better including and accommodating students in your courses, as well as information on campus-related disability resources for faculty who are disabled.

[Learn How to Manage Student Accommodations](https://www.ucdenver.edu/faculty-staff/faculty-assembly/denver-campus/denver-campus-committees/faculty-assembly-committees/disabilities-committee/managing-drs-accomodations)

### Accommodations for Students

CU Denver encourages students with disabilities to talk with the Office of Disability Resources & Services to identify any needs and accommodations.

[Office of Disability Resources & Services](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/disability-resources-and-services/cu-denver-disability-resources-and-services-home)

### Accommodations for Employees

The university actively promotes the use of appropriate accommodations for faculty, staff, and campus visitors with disabilities. These accommodations are tailored to individual needs and provided on a case-by-case basis.

[Learn More About ADA Compliance](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/human-resources/employee-relations-performance/ada-compliance)

### Parking & Transportation

Accessible parking for students, staff and visitors is available in nearly all Auraria Campus parking lots. Parking spaces are marked and reserved for vehicles that have a state-issued or campus-issued hangtag.

[Accessible Parking on the Auraria Campus](https://aurariacampus.edu/services-departments/parking/accessible-parking)

### Resources for the Inclusivity of Disabled Faculty and Students

Information for faculty on better including and accommodating disabled students in your courses, as well as information for disabled faculty and campus-related disabled resources.

[Learn How to Manage Student Accommodations](https://www.ucdenver.edu/faculty-staff/faculty-assembly/denver-campus/denver-campus-committees/faculty-assembly-committees/disabilities-committee/managing-drs-accomodations)

## Accessibility Trainings & Events

Learn more about making your digital assets more accessible with training events. You can also access popular [on-demand training videos](https://www.ucdenver.edu/tips/KeyInitiatives/digitalaccessibility#video) tailored specifically to the CU Denver community.

* * *

- [**Addressing Digital Accessibility LIVE! Adding Alt Text in Word/PowerPoint** ![](https://calendar.ucdenver.edu/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/DIgital-Accessibility.png)February 04, 202611:00 AM\\
\\
In this training you will:\\
\\
Learn the fundamentals of alt text (alternative text).\\
Add alt text to functional images in PowerPoint & Word. \\

Come to this open-forum workshop with all your digital accessibility questions and work one-on-one with a TIPS team member to remediate content in your courses.\\

\\
This training will help you:\\
\\

Learn how screen readers interpret and navigate tables.\\
Implement one best practice strategy for structuring accessible tables.\\
\\
Bring a friend and your digital accessibility questions to work one-on-one with a TIPS team member to remediate content in your courses.\\
\\
\\

\\

- [**Enhancing the Digital Accessibility of Your Course With Anthology Ally** ![](https://calendar.ucdenver.edu/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/2025-05-01_08-58-36.png)March 18, 202611:00 AM\\
\\
This training will help you:\\
\\
Understand when and how to use tables appropriately.\\
Learn how screen readers interpret and navigate tables.\\
Implement one best practice strategy for structuring accessible tables.\\
\\
Bring a friend and your digital accessibility questions to work one-on-one with a TIPS team member to remediate content in your courses.\\
\\
\\
* * *\\
\\
Learn More](https://calendar.ucdenver.edu/event/enhancing-the-digital-accessibility-of-your-course-with-anthology-ally-4)

## [Toward Securing a Stronger Financial Future](https://www.ucdenver.edu/achieving2030)

# Strategic Enrollment and Budget Initiative

![Students at CU Denver](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/default-source/offices-ucomm-images/cudenver-students-class.jpg?sfvrsn=162559bb_0)

In November 2022, CU Denver embarked on a holistic strategic enrollment and budget initiative with a goal of increasing our financial health and enhancing our ability to deliver on our student mission with greater effectiveness. Through the community’s collaborative work in phases 1 and 2 of our budget realignment initiative, we achieved a balanced budget through the end of fiscal year 2024-25.

CU Denver’s budget is directly linked to our recruitment, retention, and graduation success, and continuing to focus on student success is critical to how we will achieve greater outcomes for our students and greater financial strength to achieve our ambitious [2030 Strategic Plan goals](https://www.ucdenver.edu/strategic-plan/goals-for-2030). Beginning in November 2024, we will be working together in a multi-year budget planning process with the goal of positioning us for long-term financial health.

**Engage in the Multi-Year Budget Planning Process:**

## Apply Your Potential

Due to data center move, our office will be closing early this Friday, October 6th, at 12 p.m., and we'll be back to our regular business hours on Monday, October 9th. If you need any assistance during this time, please feel free to send us an email at [admissions@ucdenver.edu](mailto:admissions@ucdenver.edu)
and we will get back to you on Monday. \*\*\* We will still host our 2 p.m. campus tour.

In This Section

# Admissions

We believe you’ve got potential. The right degree can turn that potential into so much more—gain knowledge and educational experiences while preparing for your future career.

CU Denver offers challenging classes and supportive professors in a vibrant city setting. Our downtown Denver location makes us different from other colleges. It connects us with local businesses and cultural organizations. It is also convenient for students
who commute to school or work nearby. Additionally, our location enables us to collaborate with Colorado companies headquartered downtown. These alliances often benefit our graduates entering the workforce.

We have an inclusive campus community that attracts students of all ages and backgrounds. With 49% first-generation college students and 42% students of color making up our student body, we are the most diverse research university in Colorado.

Explore our departments and majors to find your degree. Come visit our unique campus and meet our dedicated students. Talk to our talented professors and helpful staff. We want you to choose the best college—for you.

## Visit CU Denver and Schedule a Tour

Find out how CU Denver and our students help make Denver the best destination in the state.

## Key Dates & Figures

### Deadlines for Undergraduate Applications

* * *

Summer 2025: **Friday,****May 23, 2025**

Fall 2025: **Thursday,****Jul. 31, 2025**

Spring 2026: **Friday,****Jan. 2, 2026**

### Average Undergraduate Admission Scores

* * *

Average ACT: **23.2 Composite**

Average SAT: **554 Math, 559 Verbal**

Average GPA: **3.5**

## Additional Information

Get admissions requirements, application instructions, and deadline checklists by clicking on one of the categories below.

### Undergraduate Admissions

For first-time, transfer, and returning students

[Undergraduate Admissions](https://www.ucdenver.edu/admissions/undergraduate-admissions)

### Transfer Admissions

For students who have taken college classes at another institution, including students who have earned an associate’s degree and returning students

[Transfer Admissions](https://www.ucdenver.edu/transfer)

### Online Admissions

For students who want to pursue a degree online or enroll in remote classes exclusively

[Online Admissions](https://online.cu.edu/admissions)

### Graduate Admissions

For students who have already earned an undergraduate degree and want to enroll in a master’s or PhD program

[Graduate Admissions](https://www.ucdenver.edu/graduate-programs/admissions)

### International Admissions

For students applying from countries outside the United States

[International Admissions](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions)

### Non-Degree Admissions

For high school students, professionals looking for CEUs, lifelong learners, ESL students, and anyone who is interested in taking college classes but does not need a college degree

[Non-Degree Admissions](https://www.ucdenver.edu/admissions/non-degree-admissions)

### Certificates & Continuing Education

For professionals who want to advance their careers or learn a new skill

[Certificates & Continuing Education](https://www.ucdenver.edu/academics/degrees-programs/certificates-continuing-education)

_The University does not condone high pressure recruitment tactics for the recruiting of any students, specifically those receiving federal funds or military/Service members. The University will not engage in harassing, multiple unsolicited contacts by phone, e-mail, or in person for purposes of recruitment. The University trains its admissions counselors to not engage in high pressure recruitment tactics._

In This Section

# Apply Now

To apply to CU Denver, select the appropriate application below.

If you have questions or need help submitting your application, our admissions teams are happy to help, email us at:

- **Undergraduate Admissions:** [Admissions@ucdenver.edu](mailto:Admissions@ucdenver.edu "mailto:admissions@ucdenver.edu")
- **Graduate Admissions:** [graduateadmissions@ucdenver.edu](mailto:graduateadmissions@ucdenver.edu "mailto:graduateadmissions@ucdenver.edu")
- **International Admissions:** [intladmissions@ucdenver.edu](mailto:intladmissions@ucdenver.edu "mailto:intladmissions@ucdenver.edu")

## Undergraduates

### CU Denver Application - First Year, Transfer, and Returning

The easiest way to apply to CU Denver if you are applying as a First-Time Freshman, Transfer, Online, Returning, International student, or if you want to apply to the Post Bac Certificate in Allied & Professional Health Sciences.

### Common App - First Year

Already have a Common App account? First-Time Freshmen can apply to CU Denver through the Common App.

### Common App - Transfer

Already have a Common App account? Transfer Students can apply to CU Denver through the Common App.

### Undergraduate Non-Degree Applicant

High school students interested in our Pre-Collegiate program, CU Denver Scholars program, or CU Pre-Health Scholars program, or undergraduate students looking to complete a course for professional development or transfer credits to another institution can enroll in CU Denver courses as a undergraduate non-degree student.

Students with a bachelor's degree applying to pursue undergraduate non-degree coursework need to submit a Graduate Non-Degree application, excluding undergraduate certificates.

### Undergraduate Continuing and Professional Education Applicant

If you are applying for English as a Second Language Academy, Non-Credit, or Continuing Education Units

## Graduates

### Graduate Degree Applicant

If you are applying as a master’s or doctoral degree seeking, returning, or international student

### Graduate Non-Degree Applicant

If you are applying as a Post-Baccalaureate student with a bachelor’s degree, this includes those seeking to take undergraduate non-degree coursework (excluding undergraduate certificate programs).

### Graduate Continuing and Professional Education Applicant

If you are applying for English as a Second Language Academy, Non-Credit, or Continuing Education Units

In This Section

# After Admission

Congratulations on your acceptance to CU Denver. Take a moment to celebrate. You should be proud of this accomplishment. We are excited to welcome you to the Lynx community.

Whether you are enrolling as a college student for the first time, coming back to school after a break, transferring from another institution, or traveling from another country to attend university here, we are happy you chose CU Denver for your undergraduate education.

## Next Steps

There are a few things that undergraduate students should do after they receive their acceptance letter. Follow the steps below to be prepared for your first day of classes at CU Denver.


1


### Log In


Log in to your Admission Status Page.



[Log In to Your CU Denver Account](https://passport.ucdenver.edu/login.php)


2


### Accept Offer


You must accept your offer of admission.




3


### Schedule New Student Orientation


Click on the orientation link and select your session. During orientation, you will meet with an academic advisor who will help you find classes and register.



[How to Sign Up for New Student Orientation](https://www.ucdenver.edu/orientation/home)

markdown:
[Skip to content](https://www.ucdenver.edu/admissions/cu-denver-direct-admissions#CUMain)

## [CU Denver Direct Admissions](https://www.ucdenver.edu/admissions/cu-denver-direct-admissions)

### [Admissions](https://ucdenver.edu/admissions)

# Guaranteed Admissions. Guaranteed Opportunities. Guaranteed Support.

If you're a junior or senior with a 3.0 GPA at a CU Denver partner high school, you're automatically admitted.

[See If Your School Qualifies](https://www.ucdenver.edu/admissions/cu-denver-direct-admissions#partner-schools)

## _Your Future Starts Here—CU Denver Direct_

At CU Denver, we believe that education should work for all. But, we also know that opportunities play an important role in achieving that goal. In an effort to open doors for as many students as possible, CU Denver has partnered with several local school districts to offer guaranteed admissions to qualified high school students.

- **Guaranteed Admission:** Your 3.0 GPA earns you a place at CU Denver—no surprises, no extra steps.
- **Guaranteed Opportunities:** Access world-class programs, real-world learning, and pathways to every career dream.
- **Guaranteed Support:** From day one, CU Denver provides guidance, financial aid resources, and one-on-one help to ensure your success.

![Lakewood highschool students with CU Denver Chancellor and Milo at CU Denver Direct Admission event](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/default-source/sjwe-images/cu-denver---jeffco-schools.jpg?sfvrsn=97cec3b4_0)

[I'm a High School Student](https://www.ucdenver.edu/admissions/cu-denver-direct-admissions#for-students)

[I'm a Parent or Guardian](https://www.ucdenver.edu/admissions/cu-denver-direct-admissions#for-parents-families)

[I'm a Counselor](https://www.ucdenver.edu/admissions/cu-denver-direct-admissions#for-counselors)

## _Partner Schools_

Our partner schools share our commitment to expanding access and opportunity—together, we’re creating a direct pathway from high school to college for students who are ready to thrive at CU Denver.

![Denver Public Schools logo in English and Spanish](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/default-source/cu-denver-direct-admissions-logos/dps-logo.jpg?sfvrsn=4d9c3b4_0)

![Aurora Public Schools Logo](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/default-source/cu-denver-direct-admissions-logos/aps-logo.jpg?sfvrsn=75d9c3b4_2)

![Jefferson County Public Schools logo](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/default-source/cu-denver-direct-admissions-logos/jeffco-logo.jpg?sfvrsn=24d9c3b4_1)

* * *

## _How Direct Admission Works_

1. School Partnership

Participating schools and districts share the names of students with a 3.0 GPA or higher directly with CU Denver at the beginning of the semester.


2. Automatic Outreach

CU Denver emails eligible students, inviting them to complete their application — with no fee required.


3. Simple Application

If you submit your application and transcripts by November 1, you'll get your official decision letter and next steps by mid-November.


4. Financial Aid Preview

Students who complete their application and transcript submission by March 1 will receive a sample financial aid package by April 15 to help plan ahead.



* * *

## _For High School Students_

**You’ve Earned It — Your Future at CU Denver Starts Now**

You’ve worked hard. You’ve challenged yourself. And now, your dedication has paid off — you’re guaranteed admission to CU Denver through our Direct Admissions program.

If your GPA is 3.0 or higher and you attend one of our partner high schools, your name has been shared directly with CU Denver by your school district. That means you’re already on your way to becoming a CU Denver Lynx.

### _Here’s what happens next:_

1. Watch Your Inbox

Participating schools and districts share the names of students with a 3.0 GPA or higher directly with CU Denver at the beginning of the semester.


2. Apply and Submit Your Transcripts

Complete your CU Denver application for free and upload your transcripts by November 1 to receive your official acceptance packet with your next steps in mid-to-late November.


3. See Your Financial Aid Estimate Early

If you submit your application and transcripts by March 1, you’ll get a sample financial aid package by April 15 so you can see how affordable CU Denver can be.



This is your moment to take the next step toward your goals — in the heart of the city, surrounded by opportunity, and supported every step of the way.

_Resources_

- CU Denver Academics – [Degrees & programs](https://www.ucdenver.edu/academics/degrees-programs), [Center for Undergraduate Exploration & Advising](https://www.ucdenver.edu/center-for-undergraduate-exploration-and-advising)
- Explore CU Denver Life –  [Housing](https://www.ucdenver.edu/housing-and-dining), [Student Life](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-life), [Orientation](https://www.ucdenver.edu/orientation)
- Support Resources – [Advising, Coaching, Financial Aid](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student)

* * *

## _For Parents & Families_

**A Simple Path to Your Student’s Future at CU Denver**

At CU Denver, we believe every student deserves a clear, confident path to college — one built on their hard work and potential. Through our Direct Admissions partnerships with select high schools and districts, your student can be automatically admitted to CU Denver if they have a 3.0 GPA or higher.

**Here's how it works:**

1. We Receive Student Lists Directly from Schools

Each semester, participating schools share a list of eligible students who meet the 3.0 GPA threshold. There’s no separate form or nomination process for you to worry about.


2. CU Denver Reaches Out to Eligible Students

Students on the list will receive an email from CU Denver letting them know they qualify for guaranteed admission and that their application fee is waived.


3. Your Student Applies and Submits Transcripts

As long as your student completes their CU Denver application and submits transcripts by November 1, they’ll receive their official acceptance packet—with personalized next steps—in mid to late November.


4. Planning Ahead with Financial Aid

Students who submit their application and transcripts by March 1 will receive a sample financial aid package by April 15, giving families an early look at potential costs and aid options.



Your student’s journey to CU Denver is designed to be simple, supportive, and stress-free. Our goal is to remove barriers so you can focus on what really matters — helping your student take the next step toward a bright, successful future.

CU Denver Direct: Guaranteed Admission. Guaranteed Opportunities. Guaranteed Support.

_Resources_

- Affordability & Financial Aid – [Scholarships and FAFSA guidance](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/apply), [Billing & Payments](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/billing-payments)
- Health & Wellbeing – [Student Wellness](https://www.ucdenver.edu/wellness), [Student Transitions & family engagement](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/sess/academic-achievement/STFE)
- Academic Achievement – [Advising](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student/advising/undergraduate), [Learning Resources](https://www.ucdenver.edu/learning-resources-center)
- Campus Experience – [Housing](https://www.ucdenver.edu/housing-and-dining), [Student Life](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-life)

* * *

## _For Counselors_

**Empowering Your Students with Guaranteed Admission to CU Denver**

Through the CU Denver Direct Admissions partnership, your students can gain guaranteed admission to CU Denver based on their academic achievement—no extra forms or barriers required.

Each semester, participating high schools and districts share a list of students who meet the 3.0 GPA or higher threshold. From there, the CU Denver Office of Admissions takes the lead:

1. Outreach to Eligible Students

CU Denver sends an official email to each eligible student letting them know they qualify for guaranteed admission and that their application fee is waived.


2. Application Submission

Students simply need to submit their CU Denver application for free and official transcripts by November 1 to receive their acceptance packet — including next steps for enrollment—by mid-to-late November.


3. Financial Aid Information

Students who complete their application and transcript submission by March 1 will receive a sample financial-aid package by April 15, helping them and their families plan ahead.


As a counselor, your partnership ensures students stay on track with deadlines and take advantage of this opportunity. We encourage you to remind eligible students to check their email, submit materials early, and explore all that CU Denver has to offer — Guaranteed Admission, Guaranteed Opportunities, Guaranteed Support.

* * *

## _Contact_

- [Students & Families](https://www.ucdenver.edu/admissions/cu-denver-direct-admissions#tabBody013c66c62-5519-45b7-b3cb-c867c85f34e3)
- [High Schools or District Partners](https://www.ucdenver.edu/admissions/cu-denver-direct-admissions#tabBody113c66c62-5519-45b7-b3cb-c867c85f34e3)

### Students & Families

**Do you have any questions about your guaranteed admission, application, or next steps?**

Our admissions team is ready to help you navigate the process and connect you with the right resources.

**Contact the Office of Admissions**

📧 Email: [admissions@ucdenver.edu](mailto:admissions@ucdenver.edu)

📞 Phone: 303-315-2601

### High Schools or District Partners

**Interested in bringing CU Denver Direct Admissions to your school or district? Or are you already a partner who has questions?**

We’d love to collaborate to help your students access guaranteed admission, early financial planning, and seamless transition support.

**Contact the CU Denver Direct Admissions Partnership Team**

📧 Email: Deneshia Hearon, [Deneshia.Hearon@ucdenver.edu](mailto:Deneshia.Hearon@ucdenver.edu)

📞 Phone: 303-315-2619

# How to Apply

Applying to college can be a complex process, but CU Denver is here to help. The application you need depends on your specific situation. For example, if you are a current high school student, you will follow the link to [Undergraduate Admissions](https://www.ucdenver.edu/undergraduate-admissions/home).

Take a look at the different student types explained below. Find the type of student you are and click on the link to get to your application. If you have questions at any point, you can contact the [Admissions Office](https://www.ucdenver.edu/undergraduate-admissions/first-year/help-and-resources).

## Undergraduate Students

If you are a current high school student or a high school graduate who has not attended college previously:

If you are transferring from another college or university, coming back to college after a break, or returning to college as a military or veteran student who has not previously earned a bachelor’s degree:

### Undergraduate Student Key Dates

### Spring

### Jan. 6, 2026

Spring Application Deadline

### Jan. 20, 2026

Spring Semester Begins

### Summer

### May 22, 2026

Summer Application Deadline

### June 8, 2026

Summer Semester Begins

### Fall

### July 31, 2026

Fall Application Deadline

### Aug. 17, 2026

Fall Semester Begins

## Graduate Students

Students who have previously earned a bachelor’s degree and would like to work toward a master’s or doctoral degree should apply for graduate admission. If you want to take graduate-level classes for continuing education credits or professional
development, you should also apply for graduate admission. You must contact the individual program for exact deadlines. See a [list of graduate programs](https://www.ucdenver.edu/redirects/graduate-programs/homepage).

### Find Graduate Student Information by School & College

## Veteran & Military Students

Ranked among the [Best Colleges for Veterans in 2024 by **Military Times**](https://bestforvets.militarytimes.com/rankings/colleges/y2024/), CU Denver is committed to your success as a student veteran. We offer a range of support services, including education benefits and our **Boots to Suits** program, which provides both academic and career resources to help you thrive.

## International Students

CU Denver has hosted students from more than 130 countries. You are an international applicant if you currently have or will require a temporary, non-immigrant visa to study in the United States. Please see below for details.

## Non-Degree Students

- If you are a professional looking to learn new skills that will improve your career options, go to [Certificate Programs](https://www.ucdenver.edu/academics/degrees-programs/certificates-continuing-education).
- Area residents 60+ can attend CU Denver on a non-credit, non-tuition basis through our [Lifelong Learners Program](https://www.ucdenver.edu/lifelong-learners)
- If you are looking for licensures or continuing education credits, go to [Continuing & Professional Education](https://www.ucdenver.edu/academics/degrees-programs/certificates-continuing-education).

In This Section

# Transfer Credits to CU Denver

If you are thinking about transferring to CU Denver from another college or university, you're in good company. Each year, we welcome many students who have already begun their undergraduate education elsewhere. As part of the application review process,
the Office of Admissions evaluates how transferred credits will apply toward specific degree requirements.

## Transfer Requirements

Learn more about transferring to CU Denver. Specific requirements vary by school and college, and this information can help you better understand your next steps.

[View Transfer Requirements](https://www.ucdenver.edu/transfer/requirements)

## International Students

International students should contact the Office of International Affairs for additional help and resources to complete the application.

[View International Admissions](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions)

# Merit Scholarships

At CU Denver, we’re proud to honor students who go above and beyond. Merit scholarships recognize your achievements—academic excellence, leadership, and dedication to your goals. These scholarships are awarded based on your accomplishments, not financial need, and are our way of investing in your potential and helping you take the next step toward your future with confidence.

To be eligible, students must be enrolled full time (at least 12 credits) and maintain Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP).

In This Section

# Non-Degree Admissions

Do you want to explore the University of Colorado Denver before applying for formal admission? Would you like to complete a course for professional development or transfer credits to another institution? If so, non-degree admission may be right for you.

Some courses and programs have additional admission requirements. We recommend you contact the academic
department for more information.

Please note: Students with a bachelor's degree applying to pursue undergraduate non-degree coursework need to submit a Graduate Non-Degree application, excluding undergraduate certificates. To pursue undergraduate non-degree coursework, students need to have graduated from high school or earned a GED at the time of enrollment.

### Undergraduate Non-Degree Application Deadlines:

#### Spring 2025:

- Jan. 2, 2025

#### Summer 2025:

- May 1, 2025

#### Fall 2025:

- Jul. 31, 2025

### Undergraduate Non-Degree Admission Requirements:

Even students who are trying out classes at CU Denver can do so with a purpose. Learn more about our non-degree admissions requirements.

- Application Fee: You must pay a $50 non-refundable application fee via credit card by [logging back in to your application](https://www.ucdenver.edu/admissions/-apply-now).


## Non-Degree Information

Non-degree students take the same classes as degree-seeking students, but there are some important differences that apply to non-degree admission.

### Non-Degree Financial Information

Taking classes on a non-degree basis may affect your costs. For example, non-degree students are not eligible for financial aid, student loans, most scholarships, or tuition assistance. Additionally, tuition rates are based on your admission classification.

## Financial Questions?

Please call the Bursar’s Office with questions about non-degree costs at **303-315-1800.**

### Switching From Non-Degree to Degree Admissions

If you are admitted as a non-degree student but you want to change to degree-seeking status for the same semester, you must reapply as a degree-seeking student and complete the application requirements.

If you are under the age of 22, an ACT or SAT test score and official high school transcript will be required for admission to a degree program, regardless of how many credits were completed at the non-degree level.​

Undergraduate and graduate degree
programs have varying policies on the number of credits that may be applied from non-degree status toward your degree requirement. Please contact your program for more information.

### Continuing and Professional Education

If you want to enroll in courses identified as [Continuing and Professional Education](https://www.ucdenver.edu/academics/degrees-programs/certificates-continuing-education), do not apply for non-degree admission. See your program for application information.

### Lifelong Learners Program

Area residents age 60+ can enroll in CU Denver’s Lifelong Learners Program to take classes on a non-credit, non-tuition basis. Keep your mind active by learning something new or revisiting subjects you enjoy. The Lifelong Learners Program will be active for the Fall 2024 Semester. For more information about enrolling in courses, please visit our [Lifelong Learners Website](https://www.ucdenver.edu/lifelong-learners).

# Welcome to @Web

### Resources to help you create great Sitefinity content

## Report a bug

* * *

### Something is wrong. Widgets aren't working correctly. The site is acting funky. There's a problem.

**Let us know so we can fix it!**

[**Report a bug**](https://www.ucdenver.edu/website-buildout-feedback)

## Request a new feature

* * *

### The current CMS just doesn't do it for you. You have a specific need that would improve Sitefinity for all users.

**Let us know so we can build it!**

[**Request a new feature**](https://www.ucdenver.edu/website-buildout-feedback)

## Ask a question

* * *

### You need help. You're unsure how to do something. You're confused. You have a specific, detailed question.

**Let us know so we can answer you!**

[**Ask a question**](https://www.ucdenver.edu/atweb/features-bugs-help/get-help-2/general-website-questions)

All sessions are fully remote using Microsoft Teams. Just select a service below to get started.

markdown:
[Skip to content](https://www.ucdenver.edu/atweb/using-sitefinity/website-basics#CUMain)

## [@Web](https://www.ucdenver.edu/atweb)

# Getting Started

## What is a CMS?

* * *

A CMS (content management system) is software that allows you to easily manage content and create a website. The CMS we are using is Sitefinity.

There's a lot to love about the Sitefinity content management system (CMS). Topping the list: it is easy to use for all skill levels, portrays a unified [university brand](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/ucomm/brand) and
leads to positive experiences for our site users. Some more good qualities of Sitefinity is the simplicity, accessibility, sophisticated look and feel and security. [Learn more about Sitefinity.](https://www.ucdenver.edu/atweb/using-sitefinity/website-basics/introducing-sitefinity)

## Components of a Sitefinity website

* * *

### [Pages](https://www.ucdenver.edu/atweb/using-sitefinity/building-your-site/pages)

This first thing to note is your full website is made up of pages. Each page helps create the structure of your site. [Learn how to create pages](https://www.ucdenver.edu/atweb/using-sitefinity/building-your-site/pages/setting-up-pages).

### Navigation

The navigation allows visitors to easily flow from one page to another. It is a key factor to consider on your website; a great navigation will help users easily find the content they need. Every page you create will have navigation menus so
that site visitors can find your content. [Learn more about navigation and best practices.](https://www.ucdenver.edu/atweb/using-sitefinity/building-your-site/pages/navigation)

### Design

There are many facets to designing a website. In Sitefinity, we do a lot of the work for you by creating the themes and widgets that dictate the general look and feel of your website. But, never fear, you can still have a unique website. Your individual
design is dependent upon the layouts you decide to use for your pages and the widgets you pick to display your content. [Learn about how to design your webpages](https://www.ucdenver.edu/atweb/using-sitefinity/building-your-site/pages/website-design)

### Content

Speaking of content, this is really important. This is the "meat" of your site. In other words, this is the information your site visitors want. Make it simple to find and understand. [Learn about content and how to add it to your pages](https://www.ucdenver.edu/atweb/using-sitefinity/building-your-site).

### Media

Images and videos are forms of media in Sitefinity, images being the most common. They bring visual interest to a page and if used correctly, engage your audience. When using media on your webpages, be sure to follow [accessibility guidelines](https://www.ucdenver.edu/atweb/web-accessibility/accessible-images) for people with disabilities and also [optimize your images](https://www.ucdenver.edu/atweb/using-sitefinity/after-launch/image-optimization) to look good and load fast.

### Accessibility

Keep accessibility in mind when you are building your website. It's really important because it allows people with disabilities to access your content but these practices will make your site more usable to everyone. You can get [a full rundown of accessibility here](https://www.ucdenver.edu/atweb/web-accessibility).

### Search engine optimization

This is also known as SEO and it's essentially how people find your website, via search engines like Google or Bing. When creating pages in Sitefinity, include descriptions and keywords to optimize your search ranking.

### Google Analytics

This is the service we use to track and report your website traffic. It supplies information about site visits, page views, page clicks and more. In order to track this information, we need you to include the proper Google tag manager ID in your page
properties.

## Sitefinity access

* * *

Sitefinity tools and features are available by permissions, based on your training attendance. When your site is ready to be created, OIT staff will meet with your team to establish the initial permission structure for your site. In order to
get access to a website, you have to be approved by your department to attend training. [Learn more about training.](https://www.ucdenver.edu/atweb/trainings/training)

## Training

* * *

Current Sitefinity training is only for individuals currently in the formal CMS Transition process or those who have already completed the transition process led by the CMS Transition team and have been approved by their department's CMS Transition Project Manager for training.

During the transition process, your department will be guided through relaunching your site step by step and notified when it’s time to complete Sitefinity training. If you believe it's time for training, please reach out to your department's CMS Transition project manager for more information.

## Logging in

* * *

1. Open a browser and navigate to your website.
2. Click the CMS Login button at the bottom of the page.
3. Use your university credentials to log in.

![CMS Login button for Sitefinity](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/default-source/atweb-images/widget-screen-shots/accesssitefinity.png?sfvrsn=c70abcb8_0)

## The dashboard

* * *

![Logged-in pencil icon](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/default-source/atweb-images/usage-guide-images-db/screen-shot-2018-02-22-at-11-35-22-am.png?sfvrsn=4184bcb8_0)The **dashboard** is the first place you should start when building your site. The dashboard includes the navigation structure of your entire university website. You can access all of your pages and content here. It provides information on new projects and releases, gives you access to recent content, you can utilize resources and additional workshops and more. Once you're logged into the [CMS](https://ucdenver.edu/atweb/sitefinity-knowledge-base/terminology-glossary#cms), choose the pencil icon in the upper right corner and select Access Dashboard. Be sure that you have the proper multisite selected in the top right hand corner.

![Pencil Menu - no inline editing](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/default-source/atweb-images/screen-shot-2020-08-26-at-7-32-33-am.png?sfvrsn=fbc6dcb9_0)

**Pages** are the building blocks of your website. Choose Pages at the top of the screen to find the area of your site you want to work on.

- To create a page, choose Pages from the top of the dashboard page and select "Create a page". When creating a page, you'll enter a name and select a template. After creating a page, you can choose widgets to display different types of content on your page. Learn how to [create a page](https://www.ucdenver.edu/atweb/using-sitefinity/building-your-site/pages/setting-up-pages).

**Content:** Anything you add here becomes part of your content library and can be used on different pages of your website, making this content reusable. You will draw upon this when displaying your content with widgets.

- To add reusable content, choose [content](https://ucdenver.edu/atweb/sitefinity-knowledge-base/terminology-glossary#content) from the top of the dashboard page, select your [content type](https://ucdenver.edu/atweb/sitefinity-knowledge-base/terminology-glossary#content-type) and upload files from your computer or complete the form to create the content. Learn how to [create content](https://www.ucdenver.edu/atweb/using-sitefinity/building-your-site/content/content-types).

## Contact us

* * *

Don't have the right permissions? Have an issue or concern? To get assistance, please submit a Sitefinity help request to [ucd-oit-helpdesk@cuanschutz.edu](mailto:ucd-oit-helpdesk@cuanschutz.edu).

## [Center for Undergraduate Exploration & Advising](https://www.ucdenver.edu/center-for-undergraduate-exploration-and-advising)

## Undecided or thinking about changing your major?

[Explore Majors](https://www.ucdenver.edu/center-for-undergraduate-exploration-and-advising/major-exploration)

# Center for Undergraduate Exploration & Advising (CUE&A)

The Center for Undergraduate Exploration & Advising (CUE&A) is a resource for students who are looking to explore major and career pathways at CU Denver. We welcome students who are still deciding on a major, planning to change their major, and/or looking to supplement their current major with a minor or certificate program. In addition to being a general source for exploration, we serve as primary academic advisors for several programs such as Undeclared students in the College of Liberal Arts & Sciences (CLAS), First Year College of Arts & Media (CAM) students, and undergraduate Non-Degree seeking students.

## Schedule an Appointment

Success Advisors in the Center for Undergraduate Exploration and Advising are here to help. Schedule an appointment for individualized guidance.

[Schedule Appointment with Success Advisor](https://ucdenver.navigate.eab.com/)

## Programs We Advise

### Non-Degree Students

Find information about being an Undergraduate Non-Degree seeking student and the requirements for admission.

[Learn About Non-Degree Advising](https://www.ucdenver.edu/center-for-undergraduate-exploration-and-advising/pre-architecture)

## About CUE&A Advising

### Who's my Advisor?

Students can meet with any advisor in CUE&A.

[Select Your Advisor](https://www.ucdenver.edu/center-for-undergraduate-exploration-and-advising/advisors)

### Policies

Be sure to stay in good academic standing.

[Review Academic Policies](https://www.ucdenver.edu/center-for-undergraduate-exploration-and-advising/policies)

### Frequently Asked Questions

Get to know who we are.

[Understand How CUE&A Works](https://www.ucdenver.edu/center-for-undergraduate-exploration-and-advising/faq)

## Additional Resources and Information

#### New Students

### Orientation

New Student Orientation sessions are held each semester. Detailed information about orientation is mailed to new students.

[Please visit the orientation website](https://www.ucdenver.edu/orientation/home-page), for more information and specific dates.

### Advising

New students must meet with their advisor before registration. That initial advising meeting will occur during an orientation event.

### Testing

CU Denver places the utmost importance on high-quality education and student success. For those reasons, we have implemented the ALEKS Placement, Preparation, and Learning Assessment for all students requiring a STEM math pathway course. The STEM math pathway consists of four possible course levels:

- College Algebra
- Trigonometry
- Pre-calculus
- Calculus

This assessment is designed to help you take the highest level math course for which you are prepared to succeed.

ALEKS is designed to help you recall previously learned material and build your math skills. After you complete the assessment, a set of modules will be created for you based on your areas for mathematical growth. You can work through those modules and retake the assessment up to four more times, and the highest score you earn is the one that will count.

Depending on the math requirements for your degree, making use of ALEKS could save you a semester of math, and possibly valuable tuition dollars. It is a study tool designed to help you succeed in getting where you want to go most efficiently.

#### Prospective Students

### Still deciding a major?

If you're considering attending CU Denver but haven't yet decided on a major, we invite you to speak with a CUE&A Success Advisor regarding your options.

During orientation and registration season, we may not be available on a drop-in basis so it is best to call our office at 303-315-1940 to make an appointment in advance. We are also happy to offer phone and Zoom appointments for out-of-state students.

### Know your intended major?

If you know the major you're interested in and would like to learn more about that major, please contact the appropriate college or school:

- [Business School](https://business.ucdenver.edu/)
- [College of Architecture and Planning](https://architectureandplanning.ucdenver.edu/)
- [College of Arts & Media](https://artsandmedia.ucdenver.edu/)
- [College of Engineering, Design & Computing](https://engineering.ucdenver.edu/)
- [College of Liberal Arts & Sciences](https://clas.ucdenver.edu/)

- [School of Education and Human Development​](https://education.ucdenver.edu/)
- [School of Public Affairs](https://www.ucdenver.edu/center-for-undergraduate-exploration-and-advising)

### Prospective Transfer Students

Prospective transfer students are highly encouraged to visit CU Denver’s campus for a Transfer Thursday session prior to applying or in the process of applying. These sessions, held every Thursday, with the exception of university holidays, breaks, or school closure, are designed to provide future transfer students information on admissions, academic programs/majors, transferring credit, financial aid, and other transfer related topics.​

#### Advisor Resources

These online resources are intended for CU Denver academic advisors.

- [Navigate360](https://ucdenver.navigate.eab.com/)
- [UCDAcces​s student portal](https://portal.prod.cu.edu/UCDAccessFedAuthLogin.html)
- [University Catalog​](http://catalog.ucdenver.edu/)

## [Office of the Chancellor](https://www.ucdenver.edu/chancellor)

# Office of the Chancellor

# Chancellor Kenneth T. Christensen, PhD

[Meet Chancellor Christensen](https://www.ucdenver.edu/chancellor/biography)

# _Ken Christensen_  Chancellor of University of Colorado Denver

![Chancellor Christensen posing with students in with props in front of wall with CU Denver Lynx logo](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/officeofthechancellorlibraries/default-album/chancellor-christensen-posing-with-students-in-with-props-in-front-of-wall-with-cu-denver-lynx-logo.jpg?sfvrsn=20d33ab4_0)

I am honored to serve as chancellor of the University of Colorado Denver. As the state’s premier public urban research institution, we lead by meeting our public service mission: to make education work for all.

To deliver on that promise, we invest in our students’ future at every stage, from traditional students to adult learners. CU Denver [ranks first in Colorado](https://www.usnews.com/best-colleges/co?schoolType=national-universities&ranking=social-mobility&_sort=rank&_sortDirection=asc) for improving students’ social mobility, a distinction we’ve earned by pairing classroom fundamentals with real-world experience. We are bold about our intentions. Our drive to maximize student outcomes fuels everything we do.

As we evolve, we will deepen our partnerships with our industry and community partners to ensure that our experiential learning opportunities and degrees expand opportunity, develop an engaged citizenry, and ensure workforce relevance.

Among Colorado’s public institutions, CU Denver stands alone as the only university that is [highly accessible](https://carnegieclassifications.acenet.edu/carnegie-classification/classification-methodology/2025-student-access-and-earnings-classification/) as well as producing graduates who average more than [$2 million in lifetime earnings](https://cew.georgetown.edu/cew-reports/roi2025/#data). With this unique combination, inclusive of our standing as a [research leader](https://news.ucdenver.edu/eighteen-cu-denver-faculty-among-the-most-cited-researchers-in-the-world/), we are well positioned to help shape the future of our city and region as we work to earn the public’s trust as Denver’s anchor institution of higher education.

The value of a CU Denver degree is clear. We fuel economic mobility for our learners and their communities, strengthen talent pipelines, and generate innovative research and ideas in our classrooms, labs, and creative spaces.

If you’re already a member of our community, thank you for helping us empower every student to meet their moment. If you’re considering joining us as a student, faculty, staff member, or community partner, I invite you to [connect](https://www.ucdenver.edu/about-cu-denver) with us and discover how we are expanding access and driving innovation.

[Read Full Bio](https://www.ucdenver.edu/chancellor/biography)
