## [CU Denver Honors & Leadership](https://www.ucdenver.edu/honors)

## You Belong in Honors

Find the right program for you!

## You Belong in Honors

Find the right program for you!

# CU Denver Honors & Leadership

**With two pathways to honors, you can choose the program which best fits your educational journey.**

Both the [Honors Scholars Program (HSP)](https://www.ucdenver.edu/honors/scholars) and the [University Honors and Leadership (UHL) Program](https://www.ucdenver.edu/honors/uhl) provide honors-level education and have unique benefits. As part of the [UHL](https://www.ucdenver.edu/honors/uhl) program, you will join a _supportive cohort_ with other honors students and complete
_dedicated coursework together_, while [HSP](https://www.ucdenver.edu/honors/scholars) allows for _flexible learning_ options that you design around your passions and goals. Each program is aimed to help you achieve your educational goals by developing your talents
and feeding your intellectual curiosity.

![CU Denver UHL Student focuse on a presentator at the front of the room](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider186/default-album/cud_students_uhl_004362ebdde6302864d9a5bfff0a001ce385.jpg?sfvrsn=adc3b9ba_4)

### Honors Scholars Program

This flexible certificate program allows students to set their own honors goals and design their own plans. Students combine challenging coursework and co-curricular and extra-curricular activities that help them work toward their goals to earn a certificate in honors. Students work with an advisor to create this personalized certificate.

[Learn more about HSP](https://www.ucdenver.edu/honors/scholars)

![CU Denver honors students posing together for a photo outdoors](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider186/default-album/certificate.jpeg?sfvrsn=9f9db8ba_4)

### University Honors and Leadership Program

Complete coursework in small discussion-based seminars with other honors students that explore a range of special topics from a multidisciplinary perspective. Students in this highly selective program enter as part of a cohort and receive scholarship support, have access to priority class registration, and complete community service with their UHL peers.

[Learn More About UHL](https://www.ucdenver.edu/honors/uhl)

# **Honors & Leadership Faculty and Staff**

#### Dr. Jennifer Reich, Program Director

Dr. Reich’s research examines how individuals and families weigh information and strategize their interactions with the state and service providers in the context of public policy, particularly as they relate to healthcare and welfare. She is author of two award-winning books, Fixing Families: Parents, Power, and the Child Welfare System and Calling the Shots: Why Parents Reject Vaccines, is editor of the books, Reproduction and Society and State of Families, and has written more than 35 articles and book chapters that explore vaccination, reproductive health, welfare, multiracial families, public assistance, and recovery after disaster. Her work has been featured in many news outlets including the New York Times, the Washington Post, NPR, and Newsweek, and on the Netflix show, Bill Nye Saves the World.

**Education**

Post-Doctoral Fellowship, Institute for Health Policy Studies, University of California, San Francisco (funded by the Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality), 2002-2004

Certificate of Training in Clinical Research, Department of Epidemiology and Biostatistics, University of California, San Francisco, 2003

Ph.D., Sociology, University of California, Davis (Designated Emphasis: Feminist Theory and Methods from the Department of Women and Gender Studies), 2002

M.A., Sociology, University of California, Davis,1997

B.A., Sociology, University of California, Santa Barbara (highest honors), 1993

**Contact** via email:  [jennifer.reich@ucdenver.edu](mailto:jennifer.reich@ucdenver.edu) or request an appointment at [https://calendly.com/jennifer-reich](https://calendly.com/jennifer-reich)

#### Dr. Sarah Fields, HSP Faculty Coordinator

Dr. Sarah K. Fields (JD Washington University; PhD University of Iowa) is a professor in Communication. She has previously taught at Washington University in St. Louis, the University of Georgia, and Ohio State University. Her research and teaching focuses largely on the intersection of sport and American culture, specifically examining issues of gender, injury, and law. She is the author of Game Faces: Sport Celebrity and the Laws of Reputation (University of Illinois Press, 2016) and Female Gladiators: Gender, Law, and Contact Sport in America (University of Illinois Press, 2005). Dr. Fields is a recreational athlete who also enjoys outdoor activities with her dog and wife as well as watching television with her cats.

**Education**

PhD, American Studies (2000) University of Iowa, Iowa City, Iowa

MA, American Studies (1996) Washington State University, Pullman, Washington

JD, (1994) Washington University in St. Louis, St. Louis, Missouri

BA, Psychology (1990) Yale University, New Haven, Connecticut

Appointments are available most weekdays as well as several evening.

**Contact**

Use the calendly link to set up an appointment. [https://calendly.com/sarah-fields](https://calendly.com/sarah-fields)

Also via e-mail at [sarah.fields@ucdenver.edu](mailto:sarah.fields@ucdenver.edu) any time, expect a response within 24 hours Monday-Fridays (holidays and weekends may take a little longer).  Please include your student ID and your question in the email.

#### Elisabeth Hawksbee, MS, Senior Program Coordinator



Elisabeth joined the UHL program in 2015 and has been a part of the Auraria campus since 1991. She received her undergraduate degree in organizational communication and anthropology from Metropolitan State University of Denver and her master's degree in business management and organization from CU Denver. She recruits and advises students within UHL and works to ensure UHL students have the resources necessary for success.

Elisabeth’s favorite thing about UHL is the feeling of community and atmosphere of support within the program. Elisabeth is an adoptive mother, wife, and parent to five rescue pets. When she's not at UHL or chasing her extremely active pets and daughter, you can find her gardening, scuba diving, floral arranging, learning a new craft, or traveling.


#### Jane Lickteig, HSP Coordinator

Jane joined the UHL and HSP programs in 2024. She received her undergraduate degree in integrated marketing communication from Wichita State University. She recruits, advises and creates promotional materials for the Honors programs and serves as a resource for UHL and HSP students. Jane has a passion for helping students achieve their goals while accessing their full, authentic potential. Her main objective in this role is to offer a safe space for students to feel heard, seen, valued and understood.

Jane's favorite part of the Honors Programs is the energ, and light students bring to campus. I believe the students make CU Denver a lively, energetic and inclusive place to be. Jane is a Wichita, Kansas native and moved to Denver to 2023 to dive deeper into her interests, hobbies and passions. Jane is currently a graduate student in CU Denver's school counseling program. In her free time, she loves doing yoga, biking, writing, watercoloring, cooking, playing billiards, pickleball and any outdoor activity. She also loves Taylor Swift, cats and self-help books!

#### Affe Nilsson, Student Assistant

Affe Nilsson is a sophomore at the University of Colorado Denver, majoring in Music Performance. He was born and raised in Eskilstuna, Sweden. Between 2016 and 2020, Affe went on a world tour working as a band leader and stage technician, performing shows, staying with host families, and doing volunteer work. Before moving to the United States, Affe studied music and entrepreneurship in Sweden, Jazz guitar in the Netherlands. During his free time, he loves hanging out with friends and working on music with his band.

#### Angel Ruiz Gomez, Student Assistant



Angel Ruiz Gomez is a junior at the University of Colorado Denver, majoring in Mechanical Engineering. He was born and raised in Denver, Colorado. He loves learning about his Mexican culture and is very passionate about it. During his free time, he enjoys playing video games, listening to music, and spending quality time with his friends and family.


#### Aniya Brown, Student Assistant



Aniya Brown is a senior at the University of Colorado Denver, majoring in accounting and minoring in psychology. She was born in Illinois, but raised in Aurora, Colorado. She volunteers at an assisted living facility as often as she can. During her free time, she loves to draw, paint, and binge watch 90's shows.


#### Cody Barkman, Student Assistant



 -


#### Hannah Prutch, Student Assistant

Hannah Prutch is a freshman at the University of Colorado Denver, majoring in Psychology, Bachelor of Science. She was born and raised in Colorado and frequently moved throughout the state. During her free time, Hannah loves all things art, including jewelry-making, learning new instruments, drawing, and a variety of crafts. She also spends a lot of time exploring the outdoors through hiking and random adventures.

#### Gabi Morse, Student Assistant

Gabi is a freshman at the University of Colorado Denver, majoring in Secondary Math Education with a minor in French, and is part of the University Honors and Leadership program. They were born in California and grew up in Las Vegas, Nevada, before recently moving to Denver for school. In their free time, Gabi enjoys reading fantasy series, writing fanfiction and Dungeons & Dragons campaigns, listening to ska-punk and dad rock music, and spending time with the people they love.

#### Melissa Yos, Student Assistant

Melissa Yos is a sophomore at the University of Colorado Denver, majoring in biochemistry and is in the Honors Scholar Program. She was born and raised in Aurora, Colorado to Cambodian immigrants. In her spare time, she likes to read books, spend time with her family and friends, and completing diamond art kits.

## [Housing & Dining](https://www.ucdenver.edu/housing-and-dining)

# CU Denver Housing and Dining

Although CU Denver is located in the heart of the city, we've always operated with the needs of college students in mind. Whether you're looking for on-campus housing and dining close to classrooms or wish to explore other off-campus options in Denver, we've got the resources to help you find the perfect environment.

###### Welcome home!

![Spring 2026 only contracts available now!](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider184/2021-interior-images/2026-2027-ay-contract-marketing-mood-board.png?sfvrsn=c488f0b4_0)

Our Contracting Process for Spring 2026 ONLY is available for students in the [housing portal](https://cudenver.starrezhousing.com/starrezportal) on **NOW**!

Secure your Spring 2026 Housing TODAY!

If you have a 2025–2026 Academic Year housing contract, and are **currently** living on campus for the Fall 2025 semester, you do _**not**_ need to complete a Spring 2026 ONLY contract.

Students who have _completed and signed_ their **Spring 2026 ONLY** **contracts** will receive their placements _beginning_ the week of **December 8th, 2025**.

![2026-2027 Academic Year contracts available beginning Monday, January 5th, 2025 at 1:00PM Mountain Standard Time](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider184/2021-interior-images/2026-2027-ay-contract-marketing-mood-board-(1).png?sfvrsn=7982f0b4_0)

Our Contracting Process for 2026 - 2027 Academic Year Housing & Dining will be available for students in the [housing portal](https://cudenver.starrezhousing.com/starrezportal) at 12:00pm MST on Monday, January 5th, 2026!

Space in on-campus housing is limited, and spaces are first-come first-served. Signing a contract early helps your chances of getting your preferred floor plan & maintaining your Lynx Loyalty status!

## Prepare for the 2026-2027 Academic Year Housing Contract

#### Complete your 2026-2027 FAFSA

Completing your [FAFSA](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/apply) is an important step in getting ready for a new academic year. Need more financial support? Reach out to your institution's Financial Aid Office!

[CU Denver Financial Aid & Scholarships](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid)

[MSU Denver Office of Financial Aid and Scholarships](https://www.msudenver.edu/financial-aid/)

[Community College of Denver Financial Aid and Scholarships](https://www.ccd.edu/financial-aid-scholarships)

#### Under 18? Get a Parent or Guardian!



Future residents who are under the age of 18 at the time of signing a 2026-2027 Academic Year housing contract will need a parent or guardian to successfully secure their 2026-2027 Academic Year housing.


#### Set up Accommodations in Advance

If you need housing accommodations for a disability or medical condition, please reach out to your institution’s Disability Resource Center to begin the accommodations process. Setting up your accommodations in advance helps us set you up for success!

[CU Denver Disability Resources and Services](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/disability-resources-and-services)

[MSU Denver Access Center](https://www.msudenver.edu/access/)

[Community College of Denver Accessibility Center](https://www.ccd.edu/accessibility-center)

#### Planning to Bring an Assistance Animal? Submit your Request Early!

To request an assistance animal, please go through your institution's Disability Resources office.

[CU Denver Disability Resources and Services](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/disability-resources-and-services)

[MSU Denver Access Center](https://www.msudenver.edu/access/)

[Community College of Denver Accessibility Center](https://www.ccd.edu/accessibility-center)

Read more about our policies on animals in the [Resident Handbook](https://www.ucdenver.edu/housing-and-dining/faq/resident-handbook).

#### Make sure you have access to your University email account!

To access the 2026-2027 Academic Year Housing Contract, you will need access to your University email account!

Need help getting access to your university email? Contract your institution's Office of Information Technology!

[CU Denver Office of Information Technology](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/office-of-information-technology)

[MSU Denver Information Technology Services](https://www.msudenver.edu/technology/)

[Community College of Denver Information Technology Services](https://www.ccd.edu/information-technology-services)

#### Mark your Calendar!

Spaces in on-campus housing are limited, and assignments are made based upon availability.

Mark your calendar, and familiarize yourself with the general timeline for the 2026-2027 Academic Year contracting process to ensure that you're up to date with your housing!

**Monday, January 5th, 2026 - The Monday after New Years**

**Contracting Process Opens**: At 12:00PM Mountain Standard Time, the 2026 - 2027 Academic Year Housing Contracting Process will be available for all students.

**Saturday, February 14th, 2026 - Valentine's Day**

**Last Day of the Returning Student Priority Period**: the Returning Student priority period will end at 11:59PM Mountain Standard Time on Valentine's Day.

**Tuesday, March 31st, 2026 - The Last Day of March**

**Last Day of the First Year Priority Period:** At 11:59PM Mountain Standard Time on the last day of March, the First Year Priority Period will end.

**Monday, April 6th, 2026 - The Second Week of April**

**Room Assignments:** Beginning in the second week of April, Housing will start releasing room assignments in the Housing Portal for First Year students who opted out of Roommate Matching, and Non-Priority Returning Students.

**Thursday April 16th, 2026 - The Third Thursday of April**

**The Lynx Learning Community (LLC) Room Selector Closes**: LLC _Priority_ Students will have access to the Lynx Learning Community Room Selector until 11:59PM on Thursday April 16th, 2026.

**Tuesday, June 30th, 2026 - The Last Day of June**

**Roommate Matching Closes**: The last day of June is also the last day for Roommate Matching! June 30th is the last day for students to utilize the roommate matching feature and the last day to finalize their groups. _Please note that Roommate Matching is a feature exclusively for First Year & Transfer Students._

#### Be 100% Confident!

To secure your 2026 - 2027 Academic Year housing, you must complete and sign a 2026 - 2027 Academic Year Housing _Contract._ This contract is legally binding, and as such, we recommend students are 100% confident in the following areas prior to signing a 2026 - 2027 Academic Year Housing Contract:

- Be **100% confident** you will attend an Auraria Campus Institution and be enrolled in 6 credit hours or more for both the Fall 2026 and Spring 2027 semesters.
- Be **100% confident** in your plan to pay for tuition and fees and housing & dining for the full 2026 - 2027 Academic Year. Your financial wellness is important! Not feeling 100%? Reach out to your institution's Financial Aid Office.
- Be **100% confident** that you will live on campus for the full 2026 - 2027 Academic Year, meaning both the Fall 2026 and Spring 2027 semesters.
- Be **100% confident** you fully understand the 2026 - 2027 Academic Year contract _before_ signing it. We want you to fully understand what you're signing, so read it carefully! If you're under 18, read it carefully _with_ a parent or guardian! If you need help understanding any part of the contract, do not hesitate to reach out to the Office of Housing & Dining for clarification by emailing housing@ucdenver.edu.

## 2026–2027 Academic Year Priority Deadlines

![Returning Students](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider184/2021-interior-images/returningstudents2.png?sfvrsn=2aeff0b4_0)

#### To qualify for Priority Status as a Returning Student you must:

- Sign and complete your 2026 -2027 Academic Year housing contract _**before**_ **11:59PM MST on Valentine's Day - Saturday, February 14th, 2026.**

#### **Returning Students with Priority Status Get:**

- Qualifying Returning Students who have priority status get Lynx Loyalty! See our [FAQ](https://www.ucdenver.edu/housing-and-dining/faq) page for more information about Lynx Loyalty, and how it's changed this year! _The Lynx Loyalty offer will expire on February 15, 2026._
- _Subject to Availability_, Returning Students get to hand select their floor plan and room if they sign and complete their 2026 - 2027 Academic Year contract before 11:59PM on Saturday, February 14th, 2026.
- _Subject to Availability_, Returning Students get to secure their parking for the 2026-2027 Academic Year as part of the 2026 - 2027 Academic Year Contracting Process.

Remember Returning Students, this is all subject to availability, and everything is first-come, first-served. If you have something specific in mind for your 2026 -2027 Academic Year housing, get ready to sign your contract as soon as it launches!

![Milo The Lynx - Excited to CU on Campus!](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider184/2021-interior-images/2026-2027-ay-contract-marketing-mood-board-(5).png?sfvrsn=ae0f0b4_0)

![First Year students and transfer students](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider184/2021-interior-images/2026-2027-ay-contract-marketing-mood-board-(4).png?sfvrsn=9ae0f0b4_0)

#### To qualify for Priority Status as a First Year Student or Transfer Student you must:

- Sign and complete your 2026 -2027 Academic Year housing contract _**before**_ **11:59PM MST on Tuesday, March 31st, 2026.**
- Complete a Financial Planning Meeting **_before_ 11:59PM MST on Tuesday, March 31st, 2026.**

#### **First Year and Transfer Students with Priority Status Get:**

- _Subject to Availability_, First Year and Transfer Students get to secure their parking for the 2026-2027 Academic Year as part of the 2026 - 2027 Academic Year Contracting Process.
- CU Denver First Year Students and eligible CU Denver Transfer students who apply for a Lynx Learning Community and are accepted into a Lynx Learning Community get access to the Lynx Learning Community Room Selector to pick which of the available rooms in their Lynx Learning Community they would like to live in for the 2026-2027 Academic Year!
- First in line for high demand floor plans such as first year eligible floor plans at Lynx Crossing _(Subject to Availability & Eligibility)._
- First to join the Opt-In Roommate Matching program, providing more time for you to find your ideal roommate!

![Meet your moment](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider184/2021-interior-images/meetyourmoment_lockup-bg-5556x868-52acbdb-(1).jpg?sfvrsn=a5f6f0b4_0)

# Explore Housing

At CU Denver, you have two exceptional housing options designed to enhance your university experience. Explore these unique housing options to find the perfect fit for your CU Denver experience!

![CityHeights-1217-Bsmall](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider184/default-album/cityheights-1217-bsmall.png?sfvrsn=c9afa6ba_0)

### City Heights Housing

Home to over 500 residents, City Heights Residence Hall ​serves as a home away from home for students at CU Denver. Located in the heart of campus, City Heights features community-style rooms and the City Heights Dining Hall.

[Discover City Heights](https://www.ucdenver.edu/housing-and-dining/city-heights)

![Lynx Crossing courtyard](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider184/default-album/lynxcrossing-card-imageb5e070e5302864d9a5bfff0a001ce385.jpg?sfvrsn=61c814b9_0)

### Lynx Crossing Housing

Live close to your classes and all the excitement that takes place on campus! Choose from six great floor plans with utilities included. With everything from lodging essentials to entertainment and recreation, you'll love it here.

[Check Out Lynx Crossing](https://www.ucdenver.edu/housing-and-dining/lynx-crossing)

# Dining

![Club-sandwich-679060 copy](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider184/default-album/club-sandwich-679060-copy.bmp?sfvrsn=9647aeba_0)

### Residence Hall Dining

Lynx Dining is proud to partner with Sodexo to offer our residents best-in-class dining services presenting an end to dreaded "dorm food."​​​

[Explore Dining Choices](https://lynxdining.sodexomyway.com/en-us/)

![Tivoli Food Court](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider184/default-album/tivoli-foodcourt.jpg?sfvrsn=ef2b15b9_0)

### Auraria Campus Dining

In addition to residence hall dining, students have access to a variety of restaurants, cafes, food trucks, and vendors.

[Visit Campus Food Vendors](https://www.ahec.edu/services-departments/tivoli/merchants-vendors)

![Milo Lynx Mascot looking at food](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider184/default-album/milo-at-themarket.jpg?sfvrsn=20cf14b9_0)

### Discounted Dining in Denver

Looking to save some money and try new restaurants? Check out the Student Discounts page for several opportunities to save across the city.

[Find Discounts](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/discounts-deals)

## Transportation and Parking

View parking and commuting options, campus maps, and additional transportation information.

[View Student Transportation and Parking Options](https://www.ucdenver.edu/life/living-on-around-campus/student-transportation-parking)

## [Housing & Dining](https://www.ucdenver.edu/housing-and-dining)

# City Heights Residence Hall

Gathering areas, dining options, and student access to academic support, as well as a variety of co-curricular success services, are designed to support all students and help you build new connections with each other, faculty, and staff.

An affordable housing option that doubles as a central hub for academic enrichment opportunities and peer-to-peer connections, along with a dining hall for all campus community members, City Heights is the hottest spot on campus. Students will live in a central location within walking distance of some of the most-visited student and academic support services, as well fun and exciting experiences the city of Denver has to offer, including popular restaurants, unique retail spaces, state-of-the-art entertainment, and professional opportunities.

## Heart of the Neighborhood

> Located in the heart of the CU Denver neighborhood and connected to the new Learning Commons, students are closer than ever to services, amenities, and classrooms. Welcome to your new hub for student residential life.

## Live, Laugh, Learn

> Built to bring our entire campus community together, and designed to support the growth and development of both students and faculty. The Learning Commons is the place to be on campus. Gather, study, learn, and socialize.

![5O5A0099](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider184/2021-interior-images/2021-interior-images/5o5a0099.jpeg?sfvrsn=932fb6ba_2)

### Live and Learn

City Heights is home to students and the Learning Commons. Imagine study spaces, tutoring rooms, and some of the most visited academic support and student success services all in one location.

![5O5A0102](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider184/2021-interior-images/2021-interior-images/5o5a0102.jpeg?sfvrsn=6920b6ba_2)

### Home Sweet Home

Finding housing in Denver can be tough and expensive! The new residence hall aims to take that stress off of students by offering a modern, safe, and affordable space.

![5O5A0117](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider184/2021-interior-images/2021-interior-images/5o5a0117.jpeg?sfvrsn=9b20b6ba_2)

### All You Care to Eat

City Heights Dining Hall is our brand-new, all-you-care-to-eat dining facility in the City Heights Resident Hall. Enjoy a variety of fresh dishes for breakfast, lunch and dinner.

# Residence Hall Information

Improving student retention and success is a key element of CU Denver’s Strategic Priorities. So, when students expressed a desire for a residential college experience, university leadership listened. Now officially open, the new residence hall for students offers a completely new feel to the CU Denver neighborhood.

### New Connections to Campus

Studies have shown that students who live on campus stay in school, matriculate faster, perform better and feel more connected to their faculty and fellow students, tying them to the university in powerful and life-changing ways. Paired with the  [Learning Commons](https://www.ucdenver.edu/life/services/housing/new/Pages/learning-commons.aspx "Learning Commons")[Learning Commons](https://www.ucdenver.edu/learning-resources-center/learning-resources-center), along with the Student Commons Building and the Lola & Rob Salazar Student Wellness Center, the residence hall will create a whole new community and hub for services in the [CU Denver neighborhood](https://connections.cu.edu/stories/auraria-master-plan-expanding-opportunities-cu-denver-urban-communities).

### Modern, Safe, and Affordable

With over 500 beds available, this housing, dining, and learning complex helps to combat the rising rents in Denver and place students in the heart of campus, seconds away from classrooms, support services, amenities, and downtown Denver. Accessible housing for students is an attractive option for domestic [Colorado and non-Colorado resident](https://www.ucdenver.edu/registrar/residency) students as well as [international students](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions/home), helping to ease their transition to a new culture.

### Heart of Campus

The new residence hall sits at the intersection of significant pedestrian, bike and bus routes. It features two dormitory wings, one six stories and the other seven stories, and includes double and triple rooms. Along with the Learning Commons wing, it's a highly visible and prominent addition to the CU Denver neighborhood offering a central gathering place that connects and interacts with the university and the greater [Auraria Campus](https://www.ahec.edu/).

![Picture of city heights](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider184/2021-interior-images/housing-library-2024/cityheights-1452-e1674234693354.jpg?sfvrsn=dc33e4bb_0)

## [Housing & Dining](https://www.ucdenver.edu/housing-and-dining)

Lynx Crossing is conveniently located On-Campus and in the heart of the city, so you're never far away from your classes or fun. Lynx Crossing offers a wide range of floor plans and other features you can choose from as a student. Welcome home.

## Future Residents

## Current Residents

## Resources

# Amenities

Lynx Crossing offers a number of amenities to residents, including common rooms equipped with full kitchens, ping pong, billiards, a quiet study lounge, an active lounge, laundry free to use for residents, and a newly renovated gym.

## Lynx Crossing Fitness Center

The Lynx Crossing Fitness Center is newly renovated with a variety of exercise machines to meet the needs of our residents.

## Eat Market

The new Eat Food Market at Lynx Crossing offers a variety of exciting grab-and-go dining options. Enjoy convenient food and beverage options while on the run!

## Active Lounge

Our newly renovated Active Lounge is the location of many of our events, spacious, with furniture for a variety of needs, and a large flat screen TV for community use, it's the perfect place for studying with friends or watching a movie!

## [Housing & Dining](https://www.ucdenver.edu/housing-and-dining)

# Lynx Crossing Floor Plans

Lynx Crossing provides expertly designed floor plans that fit your on-campus lifestyle. Each housing unit comes furnished and includes a newly renovated kitchen with appliances including: sink, cabinets, microwave, refrigerator, and modern living room furniture. Have the accommodation of your dreams, just steps away from class! Choose your new housing unit below.

## **Rates for Fall 2026 - Spring 2027**

Below are the floor plan rates (per semester) for the 2026 - 2027 Academic Year. All available floor plans include Wi-Fi, utilities, and free laundry.

\*All 2026-2027 Housing Rates are Subject to Change.

![lc_Studio_1.27.22](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider184/default-album/lc_studio_1-27-22.png?sfvrsn=50c2a9ba_1)

### Studio (Single)

**Per Person Semester Rate:** $8,230.00

**Occupancy:** Full suite is occupied by one resident.

**Bedroom:** Full size bed, two dressers, and a desk and chair


**Bathrooms:** Private bathroom with bathtub

## Welcome to International Admissions

The University of Colorado Denver is a fully accredited institution that offers academic excellence and unparalleled career outcomes in one of America's greatest cities. International students from 130 countries benefit from opportunities for [freshman scholarships](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions/costs-financial-aid/undergraduate), on-campus employment, and [57 STEM-OPT eligible programs](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions/after-admission/optional-practical-training-(opt)). Whether you’re interested in 3-D graphics and animation, urban design, business, engineering, pre-med, pharmacy, or a degree you’ve customized from one of the [university’s 150+ undergraduate or graduate degree choices](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions/about-cu-denver/programs-of-study), your educational experience will be supported through outstanding student services.

As a collaborative partner with the Office of International Admissions, the university’s [ESL (English as a Second Language) Academy​](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions/about-cu-denver/learn-english-at-cu-denver) not only prepares you in English language fluency, it also supports your success as a University of Colorado Denver student. Choices of [on-campus or off-campus housing](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions/after-admission/arrival-housing) are available, all easily within walking or public transportation distance.

Students at the university are serious about their studies, and when it's time for recreation, Colorado has more than 300 days of sunshine per year, 200 parks, and the "best trail system" in the nation. Set against the backdrop of the dramatic Rocky Mountains, [Denver](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions/about-cu-denver/about-denver-co) is a thriving city centrally located in the United States, is home to one of the nation’s best international airports, one of its strongest city economies, and is a great place to gain an exceptional education.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

Learn More about International Admissions at the University Of Colorado Denver

# Learn More about International Admissions at the University Of Colorado Denver

Learn More about International Admissions at the University Of Colorado Denver

From 130 countries around the world, international students arrived in the Rocky Mountains this year ready to start classes at CU Denver. The international student population at the university increases annually, and each student brings his or her own unique perspective to the campus.

"This was a totally new place—I had no clue—and I had to start from scratch. But the professors and students were so helpful," said Medhi Bandali, a biology major on a pre-med track.

Bandali was teamed with a "study buddy", an American student who helped him with the language and with finding his way around in an unfamiliar city. He describes himself as ethnically Indian and nationally Kenyan; his family is from Zanzibar, an island off the coast of Tanzania, and he went to high school in London. Having no pre-conceived ideas about CU Denver and the city, he's now enthusiastic about both the university and the state. Enrolled in the Undergraduate Pre-Health Program (UPP), part of his educational experience includes working at Kaiser Permanente, and he hopes to continue his studies at Anschutz Medical Campus.

"My advice to other international students who are considering coming here is: be willing to take the chance," said Bandali.

Another advantage of going to school at CU Denver is its proximity to downtown Denver businesses and organizations, according to international student Karim Al-Hassoun.

"I can go to my classes, then easily go to my internship job," said Al-Hassoun, an undergraduate in the College of Engineering and Applied Science who is planning to enroll in the MBA program upon graduation. "Denver is a city that's just the right size for students. And there are many international students and student groups here on campus."

## **ESL Academy**

With five (5) levels of instruction from High-beginner to Advanced, you can become proficient in English in as little as eight (8) weeks.

title: Apply for Admission

markdown:
[Skip to content](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions/apply-for-admission/undergraduate/transfer-admission#CUMain) ## [International Admissions](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions) ### [Office of International Affairs](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/international-affairs) # Undergraduate Transfer Admission [**Undergraduates Receive a Scholarship Automatically! \*Merit-based yearly awards**\\ \\ * * *\\ \\ **$5,000 - $10,000/year**\\ \\ * * *\\ \\ **View Details**](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions/costs-financial-aid/undergraduate "Automatic Merit-based Scholarships") ### Before You Begin ##### Read below carefully: You may need to complete your application with another admissions department. If you fall into one of the following categories, you would be considered a domestic student and should work with [Domestic Undergraduate Admissions](https://www.ucdenver.edu/undergraduate-admissions): - U.S. permanent resident status - Undocumented immigrants (DACA, etc.) - Asylee/Refugee { **I understand the above information** ( [need help?](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions/contact-us)) _Note: check boxes are for informational purposes only and are not an indicator of actual application progress. To check the status of your application, please visit the_ [Application Status](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions/statusapp) _page._ ### Application Deadlines | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Term | Priority Scholarship Consideration | Deadline if outside the US | Deadline if Inside the US | | Fall (August) | March 1 | July 15 | August 8 | | Spring (January) | October 1 | December 1 | January 4 | | Summer (June) | N/A | May 1 | May 23 | CU Denver uses rolling admission processing for undergraduate applications. You will receive your admission decision within ~10 business days of submitting a complete application. ## How to Apply - [Apply Online](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions/apply-for-admission/undergraduate/transfer-admission#apply-online) - [Transcripts & Test Scores](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions/apply-for-admission/undergraduate/transfer-admission#transcripts-test-scores) - [English Language Proficiency](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions/apply-for-admission/undergraduate/transfer-admission#english-language-proficiency) - [Additional Major Requirements](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions/apply-for-admission/undergraduate/transfer-admission#additional-major-requirements) Apply Online #### Don't have ANY post-secondary/college/university coursework? [**Apply as a First-Time Student**](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions/apply-for-admission/undergraduate/first-year) 1 ### Submit Online Application { Be sure to write down the email that you use for your application as well as your password. You can submit the application before you have provided your test scores and documents. [Start Your Application](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions/ugradapp) 2 ### Pay Application Fee { After you submit your application, you will pay a non-refundable $75 application fee by credit card. You can provide a fee waiver code, or [request a fee waiver](mailto:application@ucdenver.edu), before submitting your application. [Pay Your Application Fee](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions/statusapp) Transcripts & Test Scores 1 ### Submit Post-Secondary School Transcripts #### Document Requirements ##### Secondary/High School Transcripts { **You are _required_ to submit complete transcripts from ALL post-secondary/universities/colleges you have attended**– **even if you did not graduate from the school.** **Read everything under " [Important Information](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions/apply-for-admission/undergraduate/transfer-admission#importantinfo)" below** _Notice: International Admissions reserves the right to deny an application or rescind an application decision where the applicant fails to disclose educational work performed. Reasonable attempts to obtain missing academic records must be documented and verified for a conditional admission decision to proceed. View our [frequently asked questions](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions/questions-resources/frequently-asked-questions) or [contact us](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions/contact-us) for more information. We are here to help!_ #### Important Information #### Transcript Translation **If your transcript was issued in a language other than English, you must also provide a certified English translation of the transcript.** CU Denver does not have a preferred translation provider; we recommend that you use an [ATA member company](https://www.atanet.org/). #### External Evaluations **You do not need to provide an external evaluation of your transcript.** International Admissions will evaluate your transcript during the application process. You only need to provide a translation. #### How to Submit Documents **Electronically:** Electronic submission is the preferred method of document submission. Documents can be submitted through the following methods: - Upload directly to application portal while completing online application (preferred method for unofficial documents) - Electronic delivery services such as Parchment, Naviance, eScrip-Safe or SPEEDE/National Student Clearing House (preferred method for official US transcripts) - Email - International Admissions has a special inbox for application documentation: [intldocuments@ucdenver.edu](mailto:intldocuments@ucdenver.edu) Please do not submit unnecessary documents to [intldocuments@ucdenver.edu](mailto:intldocuments@ucdenver.edu). These will slow application processing. All prospect, applicant, and admitted student inquiries should otherwise be directed to the [intladmissions@ucdenver.edu](mailto:intladmissions@ucdenver.edu) email address. **Guidelines for Sending Documents to International Admissions** | Send | Do Not Send | | --- | --- | | - Academic documents (transcripts/marksheets, certificates of completion/diplomas)
- Translations of academic documents if not in English
- Academic and English examination results (WAEC scratch cards, IELTS & TOEFL Exam reports, etc.) | - Financial Documentation for immigration/I-20 purposes. These documents should be submitted with the Immigration Clearance Form (ICF) after admission
- Passport copies
- Academic documents from lower secondary and elementary school
- Duolingo English Test Reports (Must be sent officially through the Duolingo website to the University of Colorado Denver.)
- Professional certifications
- Extracurricular, athletic, and work experience documentation\
- Letters of Recommendation
- Resumes/CVs | ##### Mail: | | | | --- | --- | | Regular Mail | Courier Service (DHL, FedEx, etc.) | | University of Colorado Denver
International Admissions
Campus Box 141
PO Box 173364
Denver, CO 80217-3364, USA | University of Colorado Denver
International Admissions
1201 Larimer Street, Suite 1119
Denver, CO 80204, USA | #### Transfer Credit Coursework from other colleges and universities may be transferable. A transfer credit evaluation will be performed once you are admitted to the university. #### Document Retention Policy All submitted to CU Denver as part of an application or after admission. **We will NOT return any academic documents** credentials submitted as application materials become the property of the University of Colorado Denver. If you are unable to comply with this policy, or if this policy causes undue hardship, please contact International Admissions at [intldocuments@ucdenver.edu](mailto:intldocuments@ucdenver.edu "mailto:intldocuments@ucdenver.edu")**before** submitting any documents to CU Denver. [Learn More about Degree Audit & Transfer Credit](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student/registration-planning/plan-your-degree#WideColumn_C075_Col01) 2 ### Submit Secondary School Transcripts #### If you have completed LESS THAN 24 US credits or one year of post-secondary/university study { ##### You are _required_ to submit transcripts from ALL secondary/high schools you have attended – **even if you did not graduate from the school.** If you completed secondary/high school school **OUTSIDE** the United States, please search for country-specific documentation requirements below: Search by country name... clear results | Country | Requirement | | --- | --- | | Afghanistan | Transcripts for final three years of secondary school issued by the Ministry of Education and copy of the Baccalauria | | Albania | Transcripts for final three years of secondary school and copy of Diplomë e Maturës Shtetërore (Diploma of State Maturity) | | Algeria | Relevé de notes/Bulletin de notes for final three years of secondary school and copy of Baccalauréat | | Angola | Transcripts for final three years of secondary school and copy of Ensino Geral Certificado de Habilitações (General Education Certificate of Qualifications) | | Anguilla | 1. # UNIVERSITY OF COLORADO

# Disclaimer and Terms of Use

* * *

**​​1 – Acceptable Use**

Your access and use of the official University of Colorado Denver \| Anschutz Medical Campus (“CU Denver \| Anschutz”) official campus websites (the “Site(s)”), and any page or function contained herein, is subject to the following Terms of Use and all applicable laws. Please read the following information carefully before accessing this Site. If you do not agree to be bound by these terms and conditions, promptly exit this Site. Your continued use of this Site indicates your agreement and acceptance to the following terms and conditions.

The information resources made available on this Site are provided to facilitate the operations of the CU Denver \| Anschutz, and its members, including students, faculty, and staff. Use of the information presented in these web pages is for the personal use of you, the individual reading these pages. Use of information from this Site for commercial purposes, other than those expressly approved by CU Denver \| Anschutz, is not permitted. Use of data, algorithms, and conceptual information contained in research web pages without permission from both the authors and CU Denver \| Anschutz is forbidden. Use of U.S. mail addresses, e-mail addresses or telephone numbers for the purpose of contacting individuals at CU Denver \| Anschutz for any purpose other than the conduct of business of CU Denver \| Anschutz is not permitted.

**2 – Copyright**

All material on this Site, and the files associated with it, are copyrighted by CU Denver \| Anschutz as owner and operator of CU Denver \| Anschutz, or by the original creator of that material. It is protected by the copyright laws of the United States of America and under treaties with other nations. You may not download, copy, reproduce, redistribute, republish, publicly display, post, or transmit material from this Site unless expressly permitted, and then only the designated material shall be included within the parameters of the permission granted. CU Denver \| Anschutz in no way represents or warrants that your use of materials displayed on this Site does not infringe the rights of third parties.

CU Denver \| Anschutz has designated an agent to receive notifications of alleged copyright infringement. If you believe your copyrighted work is being infringed on one of the Sites, please notify our copyright agent. The agent designated under the Digital Millennium Copyright Act to receive and act upon notifications of claimed copyright infringement is:

Dan Jones

Assistant Vice President and Chief Information Security Officer

University of Colorado

1800 Grant Street, Suite 745

Denver, CO 80203

Phone: 303.735.6637

Fax: 303.735.6637

**4 – Linking to CU Denver \| Anschutz Websites and Third Party Links**

Please note that this Site is linked to other websites and mobile applications that may have different terms of use and privacy policies. Please refer to those websites and mobile applications for the appropriate information, including terms of use and privacy policies. Please understand that CU Denver \| Anschutz has no control over the content of these third party websites. In addition, a hyperlink to a non-CU Denver \| Anschutz website does not mean that CU Denver \| Anschutz endorses or accepts any responsibility for the content, or the use, of the linked website. If you decide to access any of the third-party websites linked to this Site, you do so entirely at your own risk.

**5 – Accuracy and Integrity of Information**

CU Denver \| Anschutz makes no representation or warranty as to the accuracy, reliability, or completeness of the information contained on this Site. It is possible that this Site could include typographical errors, inaccuracies or other errors, and that unauthorized additions, deletions and alterations could be made to this Site by third parties. In the event that an inaccuracy arises, please inform the webmaster at [webmaster@ucdenver.edu](mailto:webmaster@ucdenver.edu) so that it may be corrected.

The information contained on the CU Denver \| Anschutz web pages is not intended to be nor is it a substitute for professional medical advice. Nothing contained in these pages is intended to be for medical diagnosis or treatment. Use of these web pages is not intended to nor does it create a physician-patient or health care provider-patient relationship between CU Denver \| Anschutz and the user. Always seek the advice of your physician or other qualified health care provider prior to starting any new treatment or with any question you may have regarding a medical condition.

**6 – Blogs, Individuals Opinions, and Other Communications**

Views expressed via the Site are those of the author or other attributed individual and do not necessarily represent the official opinion of CU Denver \| Anschutz unless explicitly stated formally and with the authority to do so.

**8 – User Submitted Content**

You hereby grant CU Denver \| Anschutz a perpetual, royalty-free, non-exclusive license in any copyrightable material that you transmit or post to our Site. Any such material may be used by CU Denver \| Anschutz for any purpose, including, but not limited to, reproduction, disclosure, transmission, publication, broadcast, and posting. Furthermore, CU Denver \| Anschutz is free to use any ideas, concepts, know-how, or techniques in any communication that you send to the Site, for any purpose whatsoever, including, but not limited to, developing, manufacturing, and marketing products. # UNIVERSITY OF COLORADO PRIVACY POLICY

A commitment to confidentiality

* * *

Your privacy is important to us, and we to go great lengths to protect it. Please find the [University Notice of Privacy Practices (PDF) here](https://cuanschutz.edu/docs/default-source/legal/2019-som-cu-medicine-notice-of-privacy-practices---english-(004).pdf?sfvrsn=2fa29eb9_2).

**Effective Date:** April 1, 2019

**Commitment to Individual Privacy:**

The University of Colorado Denver \| Anschutz Medical Campus (“CU Denver \| Anschutz” or “we” or “University”) supports the protection of individual privacy and is committed to ensuring the confidentiality of personal information provided by its employees, students and other members of the University community.  This privacy policy provides a general description of the ways we collect information about individuals, how we may use or disclose this information, and how we safeguard this information. This policy covers the business practices of the CU Denver \| Anschutz Campus.

**Online Privacy Notice:**

​CU Denver \| Anschutz supports the protection of the privacy of individuals who visit official campus websites (the “Site(s)”). It recognizes the numerous laws that govern the collection, storage, transfer, use and access to personal information. This statement extends to the collection of all personal information, regardless of source or medium; it does not govern or apply to the collection of data collected by a CU Denver \| Anschutz covered component, as defined under the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996, as amended.

This statement does not apply to websites that are created and maintained for purposes other than conducting official University business by individual faculty, staff or students using resources made available within a University domain.

**​Collection of Information:**

It is the practice of CU Denver \| Anschutz to collect the least amount of personal information required to fulfill its duties and responsibilities, to complete a particular transaction, or as required by law. Personal information includes, but is not limited to, your name, email address, postal address, telephone number, username and password, and other information specific to you.

You may choose whether or not to provide personal information to CU Denver \| Anschutz via the Internet. If you choose not to provide personal information, you can still visit most CU Denver \| Anschutz Site(s), but you may be unable to access certain options, offers and services.

For website administration purposes, non-personal information is collected for analysis (“Usage Information”). Usage Information includes, but is not limited to, number of visitors, number of page views, IP address, website pages visited prior to visiting the Site(s), browser information, technical information about your device, system, software, and peripherals.

Usage Information is used to help diagnose problems, assess what information is of most interest, determine technical design specifications, cookies may be enabled to track your age, gender and interests, and identify system performance and/or problem areas, and other administrative functions. CU Denver \| Anschutz may collect some information to evaluate the performance of ads and use Usage Information to facilitate the provision of software updates, product support and other services to you (if any).

**Sharing and Disclosing Your Information:​**

CU Denver \| Anschutz may, for example, provide your personal information to third parties in the following instances: (i) to the extent necessary to comply with applicable law and our legal obligations, such as to comply with a subpoena or similar legal process; (ii) when CU Denver \| Anschutz believes in good faith that disclosure is necessary to protect its rights, your safety or the safety of others; (iii) with third party vendors, consultants and other service providers who work for us and need access to your information to do that work; (iv) to investigate fraud, or respond to a government request; (v) to comply with an appropriate request under the Colorado Open Records Act (C.R.S. § 24-72-201 et. seq.); (vi) if you request that someone from CU Denver \| Anschutz share your information; and (vii) in connection with or during negotiation of any merger, financing, acquisition, or dissolution, transaction or proceeding involving sale, transfer, divestiture, or disclosure of all or a portion of our business or assets to another company.

​Colorado Open Records Act (CORA): As a public institution of the state of Colorado, the University must comply with CORA. Information we handle, including your personal information, may be available for request under CORA if it is not protected under any other law or specifically exempt from disclosure under CORA. We will not disclose information protected by law in response to a public records request.

All above categories exclude text messaging originator opt-in data and consent; this information will not be shared with any third parties.

**Protecting Your Information:**

CU Denver \| Anschutz has security policies and standards regarding the protection of personal information. These standards include both administrative and technical requirements to appropriately protect personal information. CU Denver \| Anschutz has an information security officer and team who assist departments in providing appropriate protection for personal information. This includes active monitoring and assessments to detect potential security issues.

When third party vendors, consultants, and other service providers who work for CU Denver \| Anschutz handle your information, CU Denver \| Anschutz relies on a combination of contractual requirements, third-party audits, security reviews and assessments and as assurances that personal information is appropriately protected.

**Third Party Tracking and Advertising:**

CU Denver \| Anschutz is not responsible for the content or privacy statements of those links or the content of any third-party websites. If you are asked to supply personal information to those websites, we suggest you read carefully through the privacy statements of those websites or mobile applications.

CU Denver \| Anschutz may include information on the Site including social media features. These social media features may collect your IP address, which page you are visiting on our Site, and may set a cookie to enable the social media feature to function properly. Social media features may be hosted by a third party or hosted directly on the CU Denver \| Anschutz Sites. Your interactions with these social media features are governed by the privacy statement of the company providing them.

CU Denver \| Anschutz may use your information to re-target or re-market to you via advertisements on the Site. CU Denver \| Anschutz may partner with a third party ad network to manage our advertising on other sites. Our ad network partner may use cookies and Web beacons to collect non-personally identifiable information about your activities on the Site and to then provide you with CU Denver \| Anschutz advertising on other websites. We may also place a persistent third-party cookie on your hard drive if you sign on to ucdenver.edu or cuanschutz.edu. If you wish to not have this information used for the purpose of serving you targeted ads, you may opt-out by utilizing the [Google Ads Preferences Manager](https://www.google.com/settings/ads/onweb#display_optout) or the [Network Advertising Initiative](https://www.networkadvertising.org/managing/opt_out.asp) opt-out tool. Please note this does not opt you out of being served advertising. You may continue to receive generic non-targeted ads featuring CU Denver \| Anschutz.

**Cookies, Web Beacons and Trackers:**

CU Denver \| Anschutz uses certain technologies to help us provide you with personalized service, and to help make our websites, applications and services perform better for you.

Cookies are small text files sent from the Site and stored on your device. Use of cookies are a necessary and widely accepted technology utilized by the Site for functionality, performance, user tracking, and personalization.  Our Site cookies may be either session or persistent cookies or a combination or both:

- **Session Cookies** are temporary cookies that identify and track users within our Site, applications or services which are deleted when you close your browser or leave your session in the application or service.
- **Persistent Cookies** are cookies which enable our websites, applications or services to “remember” who you are and to remember your preferences within our Site, applications or services and which will stay on your computer or device after you close your browser or leave your session in the application or service.

CU Denver \| Anschutz also uses another similar website technology known as web beacons to count users who have visited our Site after clicking through from one of our advertisements on another website or in emails and to collect details of any website services utilized. These web beacons collect limited information which does not identify particular individuals. While it is not possible for you to restrict the use of web beacons, because they are used in conjunction with cookies, you can effectively disable them by setting your browser to restrict or block cookies.

**Children’s Privacy:**

Any child under the age of 13 should not register to use any portion of the CU Denver \| Anschutz website, or provide any personal information. It is not CU Denver \| Anschutz’s desire or intent through its Site to collect and store any personal information from any person CU Denver \| Anschutz actually knows to be under the age of 13.

**Family Education Rights and Privacy Act (20 U.S.C. § 1232g):**

In general, the Family Education Rights and Privacy Act (“FERPA”) prohibits the release of students’ educational records.  However, the University of Colorado may release student directory information or student educational records if allowed or required by FERPA.  For more information about FERPA contact your campus registrar’s office.

**What You are Consenting to:**

By using the Site(s), you are consenting to the use and processing of your information as set forth in this Privacy Notice, almost all of which activities will take place in the United States. If you reside outside the U.S., your information will be transferred to the U.S., and processed and stored there under U.S. privacy standards. By using the Site and providing information to us, you consent to such transfer to, and processing in, the U.S.

If CU Denver \| Anschutz collects information based on your consent, CU Denver \| Anschutz will conform to the processes outlined in any applicable consent form. If you provide information based on consent, you may generally withdraw your consent at any time. However, withdrawal will not affect the processing based on such consent prior to withdrawal.

**My Rights Regarding my Personal Information:**

The rights you have regarding the use and disclosure of your personal information depend on the nature of your relationship to CU Denver \| Anschutz, applicable law, and the type of the information the University holds about you.  Please be aware that personal information rights under international, United States, and Colorado information and privacy laws may differ. Depending on the applicable law(s), you may have the right to request:

- A list of the types of the personal information CU Denver \| Anschutz has about you;
- That CU Denver \| Anschutz correct inaccurate information about you (For example, updates to your name, contact information, etc.);
- That CU Denver \| Anschutz delete information about you. (Depending on your role at CU Denver \| Anschutz, the information CU Denver \| Anschutz stores about you, applicable law, and the University of Colorado’s record retention requirements, CU Denver \| Anschutz may not delete your information); and/or
- To have CU Denver \| Anschutz stop processing your information. ​

You should direct all requests to exercise these rights to the contact information provided in the Comments and Feedback section.

**Comments and Feedback:**

Send comments or questions to [Privacy@CUAnschutz.edu](mailto:Privacy@CUAnschutz.edu). If you send comments, please include a mailing address. Do not send attachments with the message. (Due to concern about computer viruses and the damage they cause, email messages containing attachments will be deleted without opening them.)

**Disclaimer:**

The information provided in this Privacy Notice should not be construed as giving business, legal or other advice, or as guaranteeing the security of information provided through CU Denver \| Anschutz’s websites.

**Updates:**

This Privacy Notice may be modified or updated at any time by CU Denver \| Anschutz.

# STUDENT RIGHT TO KNOW & CAMPUS SECURITY ACT

* * *

In compliance with the Federal Crime Awareness and Campus Security Act (Clery Act) of 1990 and the Higher Education Amendments of 1992 and 1998, we have collected and published information related to campus crime.  [Please click here to review the latest report.](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/institutional-research-and-effectiveness) [.](https://www.ucdenver.edu/about/departments/InstitutionalResearch/Pages/CleryCrimeReport_OldTabs.aspx)

Members of the university community are encouraged to report any incident of threatening or harmful behavior to the administrator closest to the situation and/or the Auraria Campus Police 303-724-4444.  Other resources include the Ombuds Office at 303-556-4493 and the Office of Human Resources at 303-315-2700.​

# OFFICE OF UNIVERSITY COUNSEL

## Mission Statement

* * *

The University of Colorado Denver, as an institution, is the client of the Office of University Counsel. The Office of University Counsel provides comprehensive legal services to university constituents at the Anschutz Medical Campus and Denver Campus to support their campus missions.

The legal issues confronting the campuses are complex and diverse and are dictated by the dynamic environment of an academic health sciences center and an urban comprehensive undergraduate and graduate research university.

For more information on the Office of University Counsel, please visit the [University of Colorado System website](https://www.cu.edu/universitycounsel).

## See Yourself Here

In This Section

# Life at CU Denver

Life as a CU Denver Lynx is about seeing the possibilities—and being brave enough to follow your instincts. In the heart of the city, opportunities surround you. For employment and enjoyment, Denver offers more to do than anywhere else in the state.

Concerts at the Pepsi Center. Shows at the Denver Center for the Performing Arts. Games at Mile High Stadium. Trips down the 16th Street Mall. Many of the best things to see and do in the city are only steps (or a light rail stop) away from our campus.

To get the most out of studying and living downtown, eligible students can choose to live in [student housing at Lynx Crossing](https://www.ucdenver.edu/housing-and-dining/lynx-crossing) or in our new [student housing at City Heights](https://www.ucdenver.edu/housing-and-dining/city-heights).

Whether you live on campus or off, you can easily connect with other students. Whatever your interests are, we want you to do what you love. We will help you discover new interests, too. There are more than 120 [student groups and\\
clubs](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-life/student-organizations) to join, and dozens of [club sports and intramural teams](https://www.ucdenver.edu/wellness/programs/sports). All CU Denver students can use our [Student Wellness Center](https://www.ucdenver.edu/wellness) and its services to stay happy and healthy.

## Living On & Around Campus

Find out more about the Auraria Campus, life in Denver, on-campus housing and dining options, and student health and transportation services.

## Campus Resources

Explore on-campus support services. Join a study group. Find jobs and internships. Get English for Speakers of Other Languages (ESOL) assistance.

## Campus Events

See what is happening on campus and around Denver. Find things to do and register to attend.

## CU Denver News

Learn about student life, research, and campus happenings at _CU Denver News_.

In This Section

# Living On & Around Campus

With views of the Rocky Mountains to the west and the Denver skyline to the east, life downtown and on the [Auraria Campus](https://www.ucdenver.edu/life/living-on-around-campus/the-auraria-campus) feels
pretty grand. Both sights remind us that big things are possible. No matter what direction you want to go, CU Denver offers everything you need to achieve your goals.

For nearly 50 years, CU Denver’s location in downtown has been home to students from Colorado and all over the world. They come here for a global education in one of the best places to live. From Larimer Square and the 16th Street Mall
to Mile High Stadium and the Pepsi Center, many of Denver’s best destinations are our campus’ closest neighbors.

With access to everything the city has to offer, it’s no wonder that two-thirds of our graduates decide to stay in Colorado.

## Campus Life

[Online](https://online.cu.edu/) or in person, you're part of the Lynx community. On campus and off, CU Denver is your home. We offer many reliable resources to better empower our students to focus on their studies. From meal plans to parking permits, the tools below can help you.

### Student Parking & Transit

Find out how to get to class on time.

[Find Transportation](https://www.ucdenver.edu/life/living-on-around-campus/student-transportation-parking)

### Housing & Dining

Choose the plan that’s right for you.

[View Housing & Dining](https://www.ucdenver.edu/housing-and-dining)

### The Auraria Campus

Learn the history and culture of our campus.

[About Our Campus](https://www.ucdenver.edu/life/living-on-around-campus/the-auraria-campus)

* * *

If you're new to Colorado–or even born and raised here–there's something for you to discover in Denver. Explore everything the Mile High City offers.

# Health & Well-Being

At CU Denver, we focus on the educational success of our students, as well as their success in other aspects of life. This is why we prioritize health and well-being. Our goal is for students to enjoy a fulfilling life on and off campus. Using the [7 Dimensions of Wellness](https://www.ucdenver.edu/wellness/about/dimensions-of-wellness), we strive to provide a safe, inclusive, welcoming, and accessible environment. Our Health & Well-Being programs support students throughout their time at CU Denver—from the first class through graduation.

## 7 Dimensions of Wellness

Emotional \| Spiritual \| Physical \| Social \| Environmental \| Financial \| Creative

[Discover Interconnected Wellness](https://www.ucdenver.edu/wellness/about)

## Health & Well-Being Programs

We want to address the many aspects that can improve your individual health and personal fulfillment. Take a look at our services, departments, and trainings.

## Start with YOU

In This Section

# Life in Denver – The Mile High City

Students who grew up in the front range area and those who come from other cities, states, and countries may not know all the perks of living in Denver. From sunny weather to winter sports, beautiful architecture to extensive parks, high-tech startups to farm-to-table restaurants, the city offers something for everyone. It offers great music too, including concerts at the famed Red Rocks Amphitheater, an outdoor venue built of rock. This explains why it is one of the fastest growing cities in the country.

Since CU Denver is located in downtown Denver, the campus has a very urban feel. University buildings are within walking distance to retail shops, restaurants, coffee shops, art galleries, and performing arts venues. The CU Denver neighborhood includes historic brick Victorian storefronts alongside gleaming high-rise buildings. It’s a bustling, diverse, walkable environment.

## Denver Quick Facts

Sunshine

Denver boasts 300 days of sunshine a year. That’s more annual sunny weather than San Diego or Miami Beach.

Altitude

Denver is exactly 5,280 feet above sea level. The high altitude makes the air thinner and the sky bluer.

Sports

Denver has seven professional sports teams. These include baseball, football, hockey, soccer, basketball, rugby, and lacrosse.

Sustainability

Denver is emerging as a model for sustainable cities—with LEED-certified buildings, renewable energy companies, and citywide composting.

Food

Denver grows fresh produce throughout the city, including at the Colorado Convention Center’s Blue Bear Farm.

Mountains

Denver is located at the foothills of the Rocky Mountains. The city enjoys a panoramic view of the mountains, which include 53 fourteeners (peaks exceeding 14,000 feet).

Transportation

Denver has convenient public transportation, including bus, rail, and light rail. It is a top-five cycling city, with more than 196 on-street bike lanes. Denver also offers car, bike, and electric scooter rentals.

Arts

From large museums like the Denver Art Museum to small galleries and creative districts, Denver supports the arts. There is also a thriving music scene with a notable summer concert season.

Parks

Denver has more than 200 parks within the city, including nearby City Park, home to 330 acres of green spaces and the Denver Zoo. There are also more than 20,000 acres of parks in the nearby mountains.

In This Section

# Student Transportation & Parking

A degree should help you get somewhere, and how you get to campus to earn that degree is an important thing to consider. CU Denver students have several options for traveling to and from campus safely and efficiently.

CU Denver is conveniently located downtown near multiple light rail stations and bus stops. Bicycle commuting is also a popular option, given Denver's sunny weather, active culture, and miles of protected bike lanes. If you prefer to drive, there are
parking spots available, too.

## RTD CollegePass

The CU Denver RTD CollegePass provides students access to buses, light rail, Call-n-Ride, and Skyride. RTD CollegePass helps get you to and from campus and across the city. The cost for the pass is not included in student fees.

## Student Parking Pass

The [Auraria Campus](https://www.ahec.edu/services-departments/parking) includes several parking lots with hourly and daily fees. Students can purchase discounted parking permits for the semester. Rates depend on specific lots and frequency of parking.

## Bicycle Commuting

Downtown Denver has many protected bike lanes that students enjoy. CU Denver also offers bike racks and a bicycle repair station.

In This Section

# The Auraria Campus

CU Denver is located on the Auraria Campus in downtown Denver. Our campus includes buildings on both sides of Speer Boulevard. Students get the best of both worlds—classroom buildings and open green spaces on the west and the vibrant metropolitan charm of historic LoDo (lower downtown) on the east.

Three beautiful buildings are central to our student community: Student Commons, [Student Wellness Center](https://www.ucdenver.edu/wellness), and [City Heights, our new first-year student housing building](https://www.ucdenver.edu/housing-and-dining/city-heights). Students get together at Student Commons to study and eat. It’s also home to [Lynx Central](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student), a help desk for prospective and current students that provides resources, referrals, and solutions. The Student Wellness Center houses a state-of-the-art sports and recreation facility, a Wellness Suite that includes the CU Denver Food Pantry, study rooms, a game lounge, and a two-story climbing wall. And City Heights houses our students in the center of campus life.

On the east side of Speer Boulevard, you will find our three buildings located in the heart of downtown Denver: Lawrence Street Center, CU Denver Building, and the [Business School](https://business.ucdenver.edu/). The three buildings are within walking distance of many restaurants, cafes, and shops, as well as the 16th Street Mall.

For students who want a beautiful place to study, the Ninth Street Historic Park showcases one of the oldest restored residential blocks in Denver. CU Denver’s [Emmanuel Art Gallery](http://www.emmanuelgallery.org/) is also notable, as it is housed in Denver’s oldest surviving church and synagogue. The Auraria Campus is a lively setting with unique structures around every corner.

## Tivoli

Founded as a brewery in 1866, the [Tivoli Student Union](https://www.ahec.edu/services-departments/tivoli) is a grand red brick building with a seven-story tower. It houses a bookstore, food court, brewery, conference rooms,
student lounges, and student activities offices. It also includes CU Denver's Veteran and Military Student Services, Counseling Center, Dean of Students office, and LynxConnect. The
Tivoli, as students call it, was named #9 of the 25 most amazing student unions by [Best College Reviews](https://www.bestcollegereviews.org/features/most-amazing-campus-student-unions/).

## Auraria Library

The [Auraria Library,](https://library.auraria.edu/) housed in a modern building designed by internationally recognized architect Helmut Jahn, was remodeled in 2017. It includes study and group meeting rooms, media spaces, and
a coffee shop. The library also includes a Digital Media Studio for photo, video, and audio production and an Innovation Garage with 3D printing, large-format printing, and creative technology software and support.

## Auraria Higher Education Center

CU Denver is part of the Auraria Higher Education Center, a 150-acre tri-institutional setting that also includes Metropolitan State University of Denver and Community College of Denver. With a collective student population about 42,000 and approximately 5,000 faculty and staff, our unique campus provides a diverse and enriching academic environment. The campus includes classroom, research, and performing arts buildings, as well as open green spaces and coffee shops.

Many students wonder where the name Auraria comes from. Some Denver locals even confuse Auraria with Aurora, a city located six miles east of Denver. Auraria was the original name for a gold mining settlement founded in 1858 and incorporated into Denver in 1860. The miners named their camp after Auraria, Georgia, another Gold Rush town.

## [Lifelong Learners](https://www.ucdenver.edu/lifelong-learners)

# Lifelong Learners

Coloradans 60+ may attend CU Denver on a non-credit, non-tuition basis.

[Email Lynx Central with Questions](mailto:Lynx.Central@ucdenver.edu)

# Lifelong Learners Program

The Lifelong Learners program allows Coloradans who are sixty (60) years of age or older to audit up to two (2) eligible undergraduate in-person courses at the University of Colorado Denver (Denver Campus) on a non-credit/non-tuition basis. The only expense that Lifelong Learners must personally cover is a Campus ID. However, this is subject to change as we are exploring providing ID cards at no charge.

Read the Lifelong Learners Program packet to learn more about the program. The Participation Request Form must be submitted after you have completed an online [Continuing and Professional Education (CPE) application](https://login-slate.ucdenver.edu/slate?sr=76df60c2-a62e-4f56-808a-567f1452b2a7&_gl=1*12624v3*_ga*MTYwMTQ1MDg1OC4xNzI0OTQ5NTk4*_ga_DS36BR332D*MTcyNTU0NzQ5MS41LjEuMTcyNTU1MTg2OS41OC4wLjA) and obtained instructor permission.

For any additional information, please contact [Lynx.Central@ucdenver.edu](mailto:Lynx.Central@ucdenver.edu), 303-315-5969, or stop by Lynx Central, Student Commons Building, 1201 Larimer Street, Suite 1107.

### Fall

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Task | Completion Date of Tasks |
| Complete the FREE Non-Credit Undergraduate Continuing and Professional Education application. | Recommended date: August 1st |
| Reach out to instructor for permission to join a course for Fall. | Recommended date: August 5th |
| Submit Fall Participation Request Form to Lynx Central | Deadline: Second Friday after start of semester. Ask Lynx Central for the exact date. |

### Spring

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Task | Completion Date of Tasks |
| Complete the FREE Non-Credit Undergraduate Continuing and Professional Education application. | Recommended date: January 1st |
| Reach out to instructor for permission to join a course for Spring. | Recommended date: January 5th |
| Submit Spring Participation Request Form to Lynx Central | Deadline: Second Friday after start of semester. Ask Lynx Central for the exact date. |
