## [Lifelong Learners](https://www.ucdenver.edu/lifelong-learners)

# Campus Services

Lifelong Learners are not eligible for most services offered to enrolled students on the CU Denver campus including the Health Center at Auraria. However, Alumni services may be available if applicable.

### Lynx Central

Lynx Central is equipped with a program coordinator who supports Lifelong Learners. Support includes helping with checklist items, navigating campus, and other general topics. You may schedule a one-on one meeting with the coordinator by emailing Lynx Central.

Student Commons Building

1201 Larimer Street #1107, Denver, CO 80204

303-315-5969

[Lynx.Central@ucdenver.edu](mailto:Lynx.Central@ucdenver.edu)

### Campus ID Station

The Campus ID Station provides campus IDs for Lifelong Learners at a cost of $24. Lifelong Learners must obtain a campus ID and have it encoded to access buildings.

Tivoli Student Union

900 Auraria Pkwy #269, Denver, CO 80204

303-556-8352

[IDStation@AHEC.edu](mailto:IDStation@AHEC.edu)

### Auraria Library

On-campus library that also provides academic sources online. Watch [this video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Yk7UioFbMb0&ab_channel=AurariaLibraryInstruction) if you need help accessing online resources off-campus.

1100 Lawrence Street, Denver, CO 80204

303-315-7700

[https://library.auraria.edu/](https://library.auraria.edu/)

### Tivoli Station Bookstore

Campus store with a large selection of textbooks, course materials, snacks, spirit gear, and gifts.

Tivoli Student Union

900 Auraria Parkway, #205, Denver, CO

80204

303-556-4286

### Office of Information Technology (OIT)

OIT provides technical support for Lifelong Learners experiencing issues online. OIT can assist with topics like username/password, email, and Canvas. The computer lab at Student Commons provides walk-in OIT assistance.

Student Commons Building

1201 Larimer Street #2017, Denver, CO 80204

303-723-4357

[OIT-ServiceDesk@ucdenver.edu](mailto:OIT-ServiceDesk@ucdenver.edu)

### Parking & Transportation Services

Lifelong Learners can contact this office for any questions about parking.

Feel free to [register your vehicle](https://www.ahec.edu/services-%20departments/parking/register-your-vehicle) using your ID number and vehicle information to avoid paying the Nights and Weekends parking rate. You may register more than one vehicle. Once you register your vehicle(s), please note that it may take at least 24 hours or more for your information to be updated in the system.

777 Lawrence Way, 1st Floor, Denver, CO 80204

303-556-2003

[ahec\_parking@ahec.edu](mailto:ahec_parking@ahec.edu)

### Auraria Campus Police Department (ACPD)

Full-service police department active on campus. The campus police respond faster than 911 to on- campus emergencies.

Administration Building

1201 5th Street, Suite 110, Denver, CO 80217

Emergency Dispatch: 303-556-5000

Text-a-Tip: 720-593-8477

Directions to Campus: From I-25, exit Speer Boulevard south. Our campus is bounded by Speer Boulevard, Auraria Parkway, and Colfax Avenue. ## [Lifelong Learners](https://www.ucdenver.edu/lifelong-learners)

# How to Audit a Class

## Checklist for Lifelong Learners

To join the program, all steps in the Checklist for Lifelong Learners must be followed:

- Complete a FREE admissions application for the Non-Credit Undergraduate Continuing and Professional Education program. [Apply online](https://www.ucdenver.edu/admissions/-apply-now) for each semester you wish to audit classes. For assistance applying, visit Lynx Central. If you are being asked to make a payment, you have started the incorrect application type. Please note that applying does not guarantee participation in the Lifelong Learners program.
- Check out the [Undergraduate Courses A-Z Catalog](https://catalog.ucdenver.edu/cu-denver/undergraduate/courses-a-z/) to learn more about eligible undergraduate courses for audit at CU Denver.
- To find out if a class is offered during the term you plan on attending, conduct a [Class Search](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student/registration/class-search). Not all courses in the Catalog will be offered during the term you audit. Use Class Search to confirm that a course is available.

Here is an example of what Class Search looks like fully filled out:

![Filled out Class Search ](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider277/default-album/class-search-example.png?sfvrsn=9ff0c8bb_1)

Once the fields are filled out, click “Search” at the very bottom of the page.

![Class Search Criteria. Browse course subjects by clicking on “Select Subject.”](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider277/default-album/class-search-criteria.png?sfvrsn=71f1c8bb_1)

You can also browse course subjects by clicking on “Select Subject.” Then, you can select a letter to find a subject.

![Class Search Course Subject Lookup](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider277/default-album/class-search-course-subject-lookup.png?sfvrsn=19f1c8bb_1)

To view all courses offered within a subject, leave the course number blank and fill out the other fields.

- Now that you know which course(s) you want to audit, contact the instructor(s) of the course(s) to request permission to attend through the Lifelong Learners Program. Instructors' emails are typically FirstName.LastName@ucdenver.edu, or you can call their academic department for their email.
- Submit the Participation Request Form (last page of this packet) to Lynx Central. Ink signatures or emailed permission from the instructor to [Lynx.Central@ucdenver.edu](mailto:Lynx.Central@ucdenver.edu) are accepted. It is recommended to submit forms via email to [Lynx.Central@ucdenver.edu](mailto:Lynx.Central@ucdenver.edu) so processing can immediately occur. However, forms can also be submitted in-person to Lynx Central (Front desk on first floor of Student Commons Building).

- Confirm your Participation Request Form has been approved by emailing [Lynx.Central@ucdenver.edu](mailto:Lynx.Central@ucdenver.edu).
- Go to the ID Station in-person (Tivoli Suite #269) to purchase a Campus ID card and request it be encoded for building access.
- Attend class(es) in-person. If needed, ask your instructor/s) or classmates) if they would be willing to email you class materials while you wait for access to [Canvas](https://ucdenver.instructure.com/). Canvas is an application that most instructors use to publish course materials online.

## [Lifelong Learners](https://www.ucdenver.edu/lifelong-learners)

# Lifelong Learners

Coloradans 60+ may attend CU Denver on a non-credit, non-tuition basis.

[Email Lynx Central with Questions](mailto:Lynx.Central@ucdenver.edu)

# Lifelong Learners Program

The Lifelong Learners program allows Coloradans who are sixty (60) years of age or older to audit up to two (2) eligible undergraduate in-person courses at the University of Colorado Denver (Denver Campus) on a non-credit/non-tuition basis. The only expense that Lifelong Learners must personally cover is a Campus ID. However, this is subject to change as we are exploring providing ID cards at no charge.

Read the Lifelong Learners Program packet to learn more about the program. The Participation Request Form must be submitted after you have completed an online [Continuing and Professional Education (CPE) application](https://login-slate.ucdenver.edu/slate?sr=76df60c2-a62e-4f56-808a-567f1452b2a7&_gl=1*12624v3*_ga*MTYwMTQ1MDg1OC4xNzI0OTQ5NTk4*_ga_DS36BR332D*MTcyNTU0NzQ5MS41LjEuMTcyNTU1MTg2OS41OC4wLjA) and obtained instructor permission.

For any additional information, please contact [Lynx.Central@ucdenver.edu](mailto:Lynx.Central@ucdenver.edu), 303-315-5969, or stop by Lynx Central, Student Commons Building, 1201 Larimer Street, Suite 1107.

### Fall

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Task | Completion Date of Tasks |
| Complete the FREE Non-Credit Undergraduate Continuing and Professional Education application. | Recommended date: August 1st |
| Reach out to instructor for permission to join a course for Fall. | Recommended date: August 5th |
| Submit Fall Participation Request Form to Lynx Central | Deadline: Second Friday after start of semester. Ask Lynx Central for the exact date. |

### Spring

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Task | Completion Date of Tasks |
| Complete the FREE Non-Credit Undergraduate Continuing and Professional Education application. | Recommended date: January 1st |
| Reach out to instructor for permission to join a course for Spring. | Recommended date: January 5th |
| Submit Spring Participation Request Form to Lynx Central | Deadline: Second Friday after start of semester. Ask Lynx Central for the exact date. |

## [LynxConnect](https://www.ucdenver.edu/lynxconnect)

### [Internships](https://www.ucdenver.edu/lynxconnect/internships)

# The Experiential Learning Center at LynxConnect

## **Internships - Start your career BEFORE you graduate!**

**DID YOU KNOW?**

Many Employers require new graduates to already have 1-3 years of experience in their field BEFORE applying to entry-level professional jobs.

**HOW DO YOU GET THAT EXPERIENCE?**

Internships!

**FUN FACT:**

- Around 60% of CU Denver students completing internships get an offer from their employer to:
- Continue the internship, or
- Part-time job while finishing school, or
- Full-time job to start their career

**GET CREDIT:**

- Internships can earn academic credit towards degree or certificate completion
- Non-credit options available

The Experiential Learning Center (ELC) is here to help students pursue their educational goals by providing internships and professional development opportunities. The ELC is eager to assist students, academic departments, and employers in reaching their goals.

## Resources and Information

## Find Us

LynxConnect is in Tivoli Annex #439, above the Comcast Media Center. The easiest way to our front door is through the exterior courtyard outside of iPIE; just south of the Tivoli Parking Garage through the walkway between the buildings to the courtyard.

![Map of LynxConnect location within Tivoli Student Union](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider41/default-album/tivoli-map-png137905e8302864d9a5bfff0a001ce385.png?sfvrsn=dc5161b4_0)

## Read Stories About Internships and the ELC

[![Veteran silhouette in front of Tivoli Annex](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider122/counseling-center/elc-hero-banner.png?sfvrsn=30867bb4_1)\\
**Fresh Starts at CU Denver: Adjusting as a Veteran Student** \\
\\
Army veteran Carlin Page shares how transferring to CU Denver and having resources such as the Boots to Suits program helped him to secure a post-grad Internship Advisor position with our very own Experiential Learning Center.](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student/stories/library/beyond-the-classroom/fresh-starts-at-cu-denver-adjusting-as-a-veteran-student)

[![Graphic of person climbing up mountain](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider122/counseling-center/lcovercominghero.png?sfvrsn=4a9bd1bb_1)\\
**Overcoming Uncertainty: My Internship Journey and the Pursuit of Passion** \\
\\
Whether you know your major from day one, change majors along the way, or even take a break, there is a something gained from each experience. Internship Advisor Chloe Dechiro shares her own story as a CU Denver student.](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student/stories/library/beyond-the-classroom/overcoming-uncertainty-my-internship-journey-and-the-pursuit-of-passion)

[![Graphic of open briefcase with dollar signs coming out of it](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider122/default-album/internshipscholarshiphero.png?sfvrsn=6a840ebb_0)\\
**How to do an Unpaid Internship and Still Get Paid** \\
\\
Although unpaid internships can help you gain experience (and credits!), it may be difficult to ignore financial burdens. Luckily, the Experiential Learning Center (ELC) offers an Unpaid Internship Support Scholarship to students each semester.](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student/stories/library/beyond-the-classroom/how-to-do-an-unpaid-internship-and-still-get-paid)

## [Office of Academic Program Evaluation and Reporting](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/academic-program-evaluation-and-reporting)

![Downtown Campus](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider24/default-album/downtown-campus-lrg.jpg?sfvrsn=5e6185b8_3)

# Academic Program Evaluation and Reporting

* * *

The Office of Academic Program Evaluation and Reporting (APER) uses data analysis, program evaluation, and financial reporting tools to support an efficient allocation of campus resources. Our office conducts financial analysis of proposed campus academic programs, centers, or institutes. Our office also develops reporting tools to review existing programs. The office serves as the campus liaison for legislative proposals and identifies the fiscal impact of proposals upon CU and the University of Colorado Denver \| Anschutz Medical Campus.  The office also takes a lead role in authoring publications such as the University economic impact reports.

### The goals of the Office of Academic Program Evaluation and Reporting are to support:

- The development of new academic programs and centers with a financial review
- Informed decision making by developing business intelligence reports and tools
- Financial strategies and reports in response to evolving educational policies
- Balanced and impartial program evaluation and return-on-investment analysis

* * *

![](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider24/default-album/gavel-lrg.jpg?sfvrsn=8c6385b8_3)

### [Public Policy and Budget](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/academic-program-evaluation-and-reporting/public-policy-and-budget)

Track state and federal policy issues impacting the Denver Campus, the Anschutz Medical Campus, the University of Colorado, and higher education.

![](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider24/default-album/diploma-lrg824be1e4302864d9a5bfff0a001ce385.jpg?sfvrsn=4d6385b8_3)

### [Degree Proposals, Centers, & Institutes](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/academic-program-evaluation-and-reporting/degree-proposals-centers-institutes)

Are you proposing a new degree, academic program, research center or institute? This section provides budgetary guidance for developing new programs.

![](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider24/default-album/eyeglasses-1.jpg?sfvrsn=947685b8_3)

### [Economic Impact](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/academic-program-evaluation-and-reporting/economic-impact)

View reports of the University of Colorado Denver \| Anschutz Medical Campus economic

impact on the state of Colorado and the Denver metropolitan area.

## [Budget Office](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/budget)

# BUDGET OFFICE

## Supporting an Efficient Allocation of Resources

* * *

The CU Denver Budget Office is committed to providing the support necessary for our campus community to make informed budgetary decisions and maintain oversight of their departmental budgets. We are dedicated to delivering quality customer service, sustaining a friendly and professional work environment, and continuing to advance communication with the campus community.

### The Budget Office responsibilities include:

- Budget development and implementation for unrestricted and auxiliary funds
- Analyzing tuition revenues
- Annual compensation allocation process
- Long and short-term fiscal planning and data analysis
- Developing working models for allocation of resources
- Providing budgetary representation on campus and university committees
- Generating budget reports for departments
- Processing and approving budget journals​​

## What is the Air Quality Index and Why Should I Care?

Wildfire smoke, smog, and shifting weather patterns can affect Colorado’s air more than we realize—and knowing how to read the Air Quality Index (AQI) can help you make safer day-to-day decisions. CU Denver Assistant Professor Priyanka deSouza, PhD, breaks down what the AQI measures, why different countries report it differently, and how poor air quality impacts health. Her team’s work, from satellite data to real-world monitoring across Denver, offers practical insight for anyone who spends time outdoors.

## [Office of Equity](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/equity)

The Office of Equity (OE) is dedicated to advancing an inclusive environment that supports the missions of the University of Colorado Denver and Anschutz Medical Campus.

The OE strives to foster a community where all identities are respected and every individual has equitable access to educational and employment opportunities. The OE achieves this through transparent case resolutions, impartial investigations, support and safety measures, and comprehensive prevention education.

# Welcome to Human Resources

CU Denver created its dedicated Human Resources team after the university adopted its Strategic Plan in 2021. One of the university’s goals is to become a people-centered best place to work and that started with putting together a team that focuses exclusively on our employees. The first hire to lead the CU Denver Human Resources team was made in February 2022, and the most recent hire for the campus office was made in February 2023.

The goal of the team is to serve all of our employees by providing one place for all human resources-related needs. From assisting with recruiting for an open position in your department and training opportunities for career growth or questions about timecards and Family Leave, our team is here for you. The team also serves as a conduit to the CU System office, which is charged with Health Benefits administration. Training opportunities are offered by this team as well as the CU Anschutz Medical Campus, giving employees several choices.

We invite you to reach out to us with questions and check this website often for updates about our programs and resources.

## Human Resources Updates

### [Comprehensive Compensation Collaborative](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/human-resources/compensation-collaborative)

### [Time and Labor Project](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/human-resources/time-and-labor-project)

## Human Resources Units

### [Talent Acquisition & Compensation](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/human-resources/talent-acquisition-compensation)

Compensation policy, salary analysis, staff selection, Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA), and human capital management (HCM) use and security access.

### [Employee Relations & Performance](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/human-resources/employee-relations-performance)

Policy interpretation/application, performance management, organizational consulting, ADA compliance, leave information, and crisis/emergency resources

### [Learning & Development](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/human-resources/learning-development)

**New Employee Orientation & Required Training**

Designs and delivers professional development programs on topics including interpersonal skills, manager competencies, and university specific topics.

### [HR Operations](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/human-resources/hr-operations)

**Background checks & I-9 resources**

Open records requests, record retention policy, Public Service Loan Forgiveness (PLSF), and award recognition programs

## CU Denver Human Resources Team

### Meet Your CU Denver Human Resources Team

We are here to help you. If you need support or have a question, don’t hesitate to reach out to one of our team members. If you are in a college, school, or department, we recommend you start with your college HR representative if you have one. If you don’t see your area listed or are unsure who to contact, please email [**human.resources@ucdenver.edu**](mailto:workplaceengagement@ucdenver.edu) with your request/question, and one of our team members will get back to you.

## Human Resources Confidentiality Statement

> The University of Colorado Denver Human Resources will treat all information with appropriate discretion, given the nature of the information and the circumstances regarding its use. We care about our employees and their privacy and, whenever possible, will keep their information and inquiries confidential. State and federal law requires that certain information be kept confidential. In contrast, some information is considered an open record under the law and must be disclosed upon proper request.  In cases where University policies or the law require disclosure, the safety of our faculty, staff, students or others is compromised, or there is a compelling business reason, Human Resources will also use appropriate discretion in disclosing employee information to those who have a legitimate need to know. Employees with additional questions about confidentiality are encouraged to ask us for clarification on a case-by-case basis and to use the confidential services of the campus Ombuds Office.

### Benefits and Wellness

### Colorado State Employees Assistance Program (C-SEAP)

C-SEAP offers cost-free professional assessment, referral, and short-term counseling services on work-related or personal concerns. C-SEAP also provides resources for supervisors and managers seeking individual managerial consultation, work-group organizational development, and assistance with conflict resolution.

### Child Care and Elder Care

### University of Colorado Employee Tuition Assistance Benefit

The University of Colorado provides qualified employees and dependents with tuition assistance for up to 9 credit hours per year. For more information, please visit the tuition assistance benefit section of the Employee Services website.

### Public Service Loan Forgiveness (PSLF)

As a CU employee, you may qualify for Public Service Loan Forgiveness—a program that rewards college graduates who work in the public sector.

### Learning and Development

### Leave Information & Consulting

### Performance Management

### CU Denver Campus Wellness and Recreation Services

### Be Colorado

University of Colorado Health Plan Participants can participate in the Be Colorado Programs.

### Omada

The Omada program, provided to employees enrolled in CU Health Plan, will open its application period from July 30-Aug. 27. Omada is an online, lifestyle-intervention program that helps members reduce their risk for obesity-related chronic diseases such as type-2 diabetes, hypertension and heart disease. It can also help those with have type 2 diabetes who are not currently taking insulin.

### Omada Flyer: Shift Your Mindset, Change Your Health

### Omada Flyer: Exercise That Doesn't Feel Like Exercise

### Omada Flyer: Watermelon Chiller Recipe

### UniverCity Key Program

The UniverCity Key is a discount program designed to connect the staff, faculty, and students of the University of Colorado Denver\|Anschutz Medical Campus to surrounding activities and services in downtown Denver.

### State of Colorado’s Work-Life Discount Program

The State of Colorado's BenefitHub is available to all state employees which include all University of Colorado employees. Discounts and rewards on travel, tickets, autos, electronics and more.

### University of Colorado Faculty and Staff Season Tickets

University of Colorado Faculty and Staff Season Tickets​ for football, volleyball and basketball. As an employee you must have a 50% or greater appointment with the University of Colorado (any campus).

### Immediate threat to physical safety or actual harm conducted​

- Call 911
- Auraria Campus Police: 303-556-5000
- Anschutz Medical Campus Police: 303-724-4444

### Behavioral Medicine Clinic

We provide evidence-based psychotherapy via telehealth to those that have co-occurring emotional challenges (e.g., anxiety, depression) and health conditions that impact daily functioning and quality of life.

### **Behavioral Medicine Clinic Contact**

**[Email](mailto:behmedclinic@ucdenver.edu) \| 303-315-7080**

### CU Denver Mental Health First Aid Training

CU Denver Mental Health First Aid Training is a skills-based training on assisting those with mental health and substance-use issues.

### CU Denver: Request a training for your area, called Lynx Connected.

Crisis de-escalation training offered by the Counseling Center. Navigate to this form. For Presentation, Training, or Workshop select “Other (type below).” In the please specify box type, Lynx Connected. The Lynx Connected training is more information than the You Can Help a Student training and less intense than the Mental Health and First Aid training.

[FaST Form\\
\\
Faculty & Staff Threat Assessment and Response Team\\
\\
Concerns about faculty or staff behavior](https://cm.maxient.com/reportingform.php?UnivofColoradoDenver&layout_id=30)

**Faculty and Staff Threat Assessment and Response Team (FaST)**

- Phone: [**303-315-3278**](tel:3033153278)
- Email: [**DenverFAST@ucdenver.edu**](mailto:DenverFAST@ucdenver.edu)
- [**Policy**](https://www.ucdenver.edu/docs/librariesprovider284/default-document-library/4000-human-resources/4031---faculty-staff-assessment-team-(fast).pdf?sfvrsn=dee7f3ba_2)

[CARE Team\\
\\
Campus Assessment, Response, & Evaluation\\
\\
Concerns about student behavior](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student/health-wellness/care-team)

### Need resources to deal with a personal crisis

CSEAP Phone Number 844-493-8255

If you currently live out of the state of Colorado, please contact your insurance provider for information on services available to you. Unfortunately, due to Colorado state licensing requirements, CSEAP is unable to serve people who are not physically located in the State of Colorado at the time that services are to be rendered.

An additional resource available to employees is the Real Help Hotline. Please see the link below.

### CSEAP Webinars

Upcoming webinars about CSEAP and specialized topics.

### REAL Help Hotline

Need immediate crisis counseling?
REAL Help Hotline Phone Number: 833-533-2428

### REAL Help Hotline Flyer

### Phoenix Center at Auraria

For Interpersonal Violence resources and support

### Colorado Crisis Services

Colorado Crisis Services provides free, confidential, professional and immediate support for any mental health, substance use or emotional concern, 24 hours a day, 365 days per year.

Colorado Crisis Services
Call 1-844-493-TALK (8255) or text TALK to 38255 to speak to a trained professional.

### National Suicide Prevention Life Line

The Lifeline provides 24/7, free and confidential support for people in distress, prevention and distress resources for individuals and families.

### Helen and Arthur E. Johnson Depression Center

As COVID-19 affects a growing number of people in our communities, we are taking steps to ensure we’ll continue to be there for you and to meet your needs. We have fully transitioned our clinic to be remote and are currently seeing all patients via telehealth.

Current Patients:
(303) 724-3300

New Patients:
(303) 724-4975

### Colorado Wellness Recovery

Colorado Wellness Recovery is a mental wellness and addiction recovery guide. It is a free resource for Coloradans considering recovery.

### MyStrength

Available to Kaiser subscribers - virtual mental health and wellness platform.

# Employee Relations and Performance

The Employee Relations and Performance Department offers consulting services, advice, support and referrals to employees, supervisors, and managers who have questions or need assistance with the following:

- Policy and rule interpretation and application
- Performance management
- Education and development
- Management and organizational consulting
- Communication efforts


- Classified staff grievances
- Progressive discipline including corrective /disciplinary actions for classified staff
- At-will terminations
- Unemployment hearings
- Managing Family Medical Leave and Parental Leave

Supervisors should contact an Employee Relations and Performance consultant prior to beginning the progressive discipline process, responding to a classified staff grievance or when considering termination of an at-will employee.

![Leave Consulting Icon](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider188/employee-relations-performance/leave-consulting-icon3ac944e5302864d9a5bfff0a001ce385.png?sfvrsn=fce120b9_0)

## Exit Interviews

If you are ending your employment with CU Denver, we encourage you to consider providing feedback about your experience with us through the exit interview process. Exit interviews are confidential (except for information related to sexual misconduct, discrimination, fiscal misconduct, child endangerment, elder abuse which would be confidentially reported to the appropriate offices).

Information provided through the exit interview will be provided to campus, school/college/unit leaders through de identified and thematic feedback for improvements initiatives. This information may be used in conjunction with other survey and data that is collected.

If you would like to request an exit interview please email [**WorkplaceEngagement@ucdenver.edu**](mailto:WorkplaceEngagement@ucdenver.edu)

## Human Capital Management (HCM) Corrections

If you require a correction to any **HCM Data** and are unable to correct the row yourself, please submit an email to [**PeopleOperations@ucdenver.edu**](mailto:PeopleOperations@ucdenver.edu) with the following information:

- Reason for the Correction
- Employee ID
- Employee Record
- Position ID
- ePAR Number (if applicable)
- TBT transaction Number (if applicable)

This mailbox is monitored daily and we try to make corrections within the same business day. You can also utilize this email box for any questions concerning transactions in HCM.

The following email address should be used when you are having issues with an NPP or with CU Careers: [**hcm\_community@cu.edu**](mailto:hcm_community@cu.edu)

Agencies](https://www.cu.edu/psc/how-buy-temporary-services) | | | --- | | #### Types of Temporary Employees | | Temporary hire questionnaires are reviewed by Central Human Resources. Job codes to use are provided below and will be approved upon review.
**Classified (job code P1A1XX)** **-** Classified positions are governed by the State of Colorado and follow their classification and minimum qualifications procedures. Jobs in this series do not require a bachelor’s degree.
**Professional (job code 2582) -** In a professional position, the role involves the exercise of discretion, analytical skill, and judgment. Requires
a bachelor’s degree, but an educational equivalency statement may be used in substitution of a degree.
**Alternate Funding (job code 2702) -** Grant, gift, and/or auxiliary funding. Jobs in this series do not require a bachelor’s degree.
**Research (job code 1314) -** Professional role with duties that involve conducting research. In a professional position, the role involves the
exercise of discretion, analytical skill, and judgment. Requires a bachelor’s degree, but an educational equivalency statement may be used in substitution of a degree.
**Additional Considerations:**
- 9 month temporaries are required to have a 4 month break before continuing in the same or similar position. 
- A temporary position must be posted if it will exceed 9 months. 
- A formal search process must be conducted when changing the position to permanent.
- Any temporary hire hourly rate of $50 or more will be reviewed and approved by compensation to ensure we are maintaining internal equity with current employees in similar positions and are market competitive based on the job duties
and minimum qualifications of the position. Please allow extra time for this process.

[University of Colorado Denver \| Anschutz Medical Campus Employee Check-Out Notification](https://ucdenverdata.formstack.com/forms/employee_checkout_notification?sso=5e1cc77d2214f) #### Faculty & Staff Working Retirees ### Website Content Retirees may be hired into temporary staff or faculty retiree positions and are a suitable hiring solution for limited term hiring needs. Occasions in which hiring a working retiree may be optimal includes: - Fill a vacancy while a recruitment process takes place; - Train, cross-train, transfer knowledge or act in a short-term mentoring role for a new incumbent; - Provide specialized knowledge or fill a specialized role on a limited or project basis. Policy: [**Hiring Retirees to Work in Staff Positions - APS 5054**](https://www.cu.edu/ope/aps/5054) ### How to Hire a Retiree 1. As soon as you know you will be hiring a retiree, complete the [**Faculty and Staff Working Retiree Questionnaire**](https://app.smartsheet.com/b/form/a156360aac7d445cad8773e73eba91aa). 3. Central HR Operations (HR-Ops) will send you confirmation, identifying the correct job code and if a posting process is required. 1. If posting is required, HR-Ops will work with the department on posting. 2. HR Business Partner creates position in HCM and submits draft offer letter for review to Central HR. 3. HR will review the offer letters to ensure they contain the appropriate language for retirees. **_A few important things to keep in mind:_** - HR Business Partners should notify [**Valerie Vickers**](mailto:valerie.vickers@cuanschutz.edu) when transaction approval is needed in HCM for retiree job codes (e.g., position changes/TBTs and new hires). - If a department hires PERA retiree (CU or Non-CU), it is the retiree’s responsibility to notify the department and track hours worked to comply with PERA rules. - For Colorado Public Employee Retirement Association (PERA) retirees can work a maximum 110 days or 720 hours per calendar year.  When a retiree works more than 110 days or 720 hours, PERA will reduce the retiree’s monthly pension by a formula-set. ### Retirees at the Ready Program Periodically, job opportunities arise on a temporary basis for individuals that have unique university knowledge and skills. Central Human Resources maintains a list of working retirees that is distributed to hiring departments upon request. For departments wanting to use the retiree list: 1. Hiring department requests to view the working retiree list by completing the [**Retiree List Request Form**](https://app.smartsheet.com/b/form/e4c46c4f1e514cbeb1046b68af8ff11e) so HR-Ops can collect basic information about what the job entails. 3. HR-Ops will match the job with a list of potential retirees in the needed skillset and forward that list to the hiring department. 5. Hiring departments can then reach out to retirees to discuss the opportunity **For individuals that retired from CU who are interested in work:** If you are interested in being added to the list, please complete the [**Working Retiree List Form**](https://app.smartsheet.com/b/form/ade2fc8319b5408e9f36989f739b0c1a). ## Performance Management ### [Employee Relations & Performance](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/human-resources/employee-relations-performance) ### [Performance Management](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/human-resources/employee-relations-performance/performance-management-consulting) ## Leave ### [Leave Information & Consulting](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/human-resources/employee-relations-performance/leave-consulting) ## Personnel Matters Reporting ### [Personnel Matters Report](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/human-resources/hr-operations/personnel-matters-report) ### Human Resources Additional Resources - # Welcome to Human Resources

CU Denver created its dedicated Human Resources team after the university adopted its Strategic Plan in 2021. One of the university’s goals is to become a people-centered best place to work and that started with putting together a team that focuses exclusively on our employees. The first hire to lead the CU Denver Human Resources team was made in February 2022, and the most recent hire for the campus office was made in February 2023.

The goal of the team is to serve all of our employees by providing one place for all human resources-related needs. From assisting with recruiting for an open position in your department and training opportunities for career growth or questions about timecards and Family Leave, our team is here for you. The team also serves as a conduit to the CU System office, which is charged with Health Benefits administration. Training opportunities are offered by this team as well as the CU Anschutz Medical Campus, giving employees several choices.

We invite you to reach out to us with questions and check this website often for updates about our programs and resources.

## Human Resources Updates

### [Comprehensive Compensation Collaborative](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/human-resources/compensation-collaborative)

### [Time and Labor Project](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/human-resources/time-and-labor-project)

## Human Resources Units

### [Talent Acquisition & Compensation](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/human-resources/talent-acquisition-compensation)

Compensation policy, salary analysis, staff selection, Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA), and human capital management (HCM) use and security access.

### [Employee Relations & Performance](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/human-resources/employee-relations-performance)

Policy interpretation/application, performance management, organizational consulting, ADA compliance, leave information, and crisis/emergency resources

### [Learning & Development](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/human-resources/learning-development)

**New Employee Orientation & Required Training**

Designs and delivers professional development programs on topics including interpersonal skills, manager competencies, and university specific topics.

### [HR Operations](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/human-resources/hr-operations)

**Background checks & I-9 resources**

Open records requests, record retention policy, Public Service Loan Forgiveness (PLSF), and award recognition programs

## CU Denver Human Resources Team

### Meet Your CU Denver Human Resources Team

We are here to help you. If you need support or have a question, don’t hesitate to reach out to one of our team members. If you are in a college, school, or department, we recommend you start with your college HR representative if you have one. ## Human Resources Confidentiality Statement

> The University of Colorado Denver Human Resources will treat all information with appropriate discretion, given the nature of the information and the circumstances regarding its use. We care about our employees and their privacy and, whenever possible, will keep their information and inquiries confidential. State and federal law requires that certain information be kept confidential. In contrast, some information is considered an open record under the law and must be disclosed upon proper request.  In cases where University policies or the law require disclosure, the safety of our faculty, staff, students or others is compromised, or there is a compelling business reason, Human Resources will also use appropriate discretion in disclosing employee information to those who have a legitimate need to know. Employees with additional questions about confidentiality are encouraged to ask us for clarification on a case-by-case basis and to use the confidential services of the campus Ombuds Office.

## [Human Resources](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/human-resources)

# Job Description Library

See [CU Denver Salary Grades](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/human-resources/compensation-collaborative/salary-grades) for specific salary amounts and ranges by grade.

### Principal Professional

**Career Family:**Information Technology**Professional Level:**Information TechnologyPrincipal Professional

**Career Family Job Summary:** Jobs in this career family develop, maintain, and support computer systems, software and networks.  Functions include enterprise operations, distributed computing, academic computing, research computing, computer hardware and software management, computer networking, telecommunications, systems development, database administration, server administration, website management, programming, desktop support, and help desk operations.

**Professional Level Job Summary:** Principal Professionals are responsible for exercising discretion, analytical skill, personal accountability and responsibility in a wide range of areas including academic, administrative, managerial and student services functions. Work involves creating, integrating, applying and sharing knowledge directly related to a professional field. Work is performed at an advanced or expert level. Principal Professionals are either work leads over others or are considered a subject matter expert in a particular area.


**Minimum Qualifications:**

- A bachelor's degree in computer science, management/computer information systems, computer engineering, information technology, or a directly related field from an accredited institution.
- Three (3) years of related professional experience in System Administration,

Network Configuration, Software Installation, Troubleshooting, Customer Service, Technical Support.
- **Optional Substitution**: Related technical/paraprofessional experience may be substituted for the bachelor’s degree on a year for year basis.

**Preferred Qualifications:**

- A master's or terminal degree in computer science, management/computer information systems, computer engineering, information technology, or a directly related field from an accredited institution.
- Four (4) years  of related professional experience in System Administration, Network Configuration, Software Installation, Troubleshooting, Customer Service, Technical Support.
- Related professional experience as manager, supervisor, work leader, staff authority or project/program administrator

**Knowledge, Skills, Abilities:**

- Experience with any or all of the following Coding languages: (Python, HTML5, JavaScript, CSS, PHP, SQL, C++, Ruby, .NET)
- Ability to communicate effectively, both in writing and orally.
- Ability to establish and maintain effective working relationships with employees at all levels throughout the institution.
- Demonstrate a commitment to providing outstanding customer service skills.
- Demonstrate an exceptional understanding of access and engagement with a commitment to developing equitable practices.
- Ability to perform duties independently without guidance and direction from other professionals.
- Ability to articulate the University's mission and the importance of the strategic plan.

**Supervision Exercised:**
Can supervise students. No cap on # of students supervised (should not supervise permanent staff. Temporary staff supervision is rare and should be closely reviewed with justification).

Note: Since temporary appointments can be at many levels a "temp" SME should not report to a Principal Professional

**Salary:** Grade 8


**FLSA Status:** Exempt or Non-Exempt

# Learning and Development

The University of Colorado Denver \| Anschutz Medical Campus Learning & Development unit is responsible for the design and delivery of professional development programs for all university faculty and staff.  We provide courses on a wide variety of topics including professional development opportunities, interpersonal skills, manager competencies, and targeted topics specific to the University.

Our strategy is to provide professional and leadership development at all levels that is linked directly to supporting the **University’s Mission, Vision and Values**; as well as, the execution of the **strategic priorities** related to enhancing and promoting development programs at all career stages.

## Contact Information

- **Email:**[**HR Learning & Development**](mailto:megan.backstrom@ucdenver.edu)

## New Skillsoft Course CU: Total Worker Health® Leadership 101

### Overview

Renowned faculty from the Colorado School of Public Health's Center for Health, Work & Environment developed the Total Worker Health® (TWH) Leadership 101 course to help you assess your current leadership strategy and build a framework for evaluating Total Worker Health® (TWH) at your unit, in your organization. This training is asynchronous and can be completed online, at your own pace. At the end of this course, learners will be able to identify:

- The five practices of TWH leadership and your role in defining organizational culture
- The business case for TWH
- What makes a healthy and safe workplace
- The employee perspective of a healthy and safe workplace
- Ways to enhance and support your personal wellbeing

### Target Audience

This course is recommended for managers/supervisors interested in learning how to become leaders in their organization, who can drive organizational change to achieve Total Worker Health®. Professionals who may benefit from this training include: business owners, executives, senior managers, benefits and HR professionals, occupational health and safety specialists, and public health advocates.

### Estimated Completion Time

50 minutes

#### [Open TWH course in Skillsoft.](https://universityofcolorado.skillport.com/skillportfe/custom/login/saml/login.action?courseaction=Summary&assetid=_scorm12_cu_u00207_0001)

# New and Returning Employees

New and returning employees, please contact your [**Human Resources Business Partner**](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/human-resources/hr-staff-listings) in your school, college, or department. They are your first point of contact for all Human Resources related matters.

## Introduce yourself to life as a Lynx!

Thank you for your interest in the University of Colorado Denver. We offer an in-person campus tour to get you acclimated to life on our vibrant, downtown campus.

[**Schedule an In-person Campus Tour**](https://application.admissions.ucdenver.edu/portal/stafftour_vNew)

## [Office of Advancement](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/office-of-advancement)

## Celebrating the Power of Your Generosity

At the 2025 CU Denver Donor Celebration, students, leadership, and donors came together to celebrate the power of generosity.

[Donor Celebration Highlights](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/office-of-advancement/your-impact/impact-stories---donors-giving/advancement---donors-giving/donors-gather-to-celebrate-their-impact)

## Every gift to CU Denver touches someone's life.

Whether it's a scholarship that clears a path to graduation or funding for research that improves our communities, your support of CU Denver accelerates the benefits higher education provides, which extend throughout our great city, state, and region.

* * *

![Man in a dark polo smiling with his teeth, the CU Denver Business School is in the background.](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider200/content-blocks/jabez-brown-gates-scholarship-recipient.jpg?sfvrsn=4302c1b4_5)First-year marketing student Jabez Brown is the first recipient of the Gates Scholarship at CU Denver. Gates Corporation established the scholarship as a way of investing in future innovators and leaders.

### Gates is Empowering Students to Thrive

A new scholarship established by Gates Corporation helps students like Jabez Brown pursue their passions, stay connected to family, and thrive at CU Denver. This collaboration reflects the power of philanthropy to change lives and strengthen our community.

* * *

![Group of students engaging with CoorsTek representatives.](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider200/stories/group-of-students-engaging-with-coorstek-representatives.jpg?sfvrsn=60d4efb4_3)

### Where Curiosity Sparks Discovery

From robotic hands to cancer-fighting microbots, young scientists tackle big challenges at CU Denver’s annual CoorsTek Denver Metro Regional Science and Engineering Fair. With CoorsTek’s ongoing partnership, the fair inspires students to pursue careers in STEM and drives innovation for Colorado’s future.

* * *

## THANK YOU!

Thanks to your generosity, CU Denver students gained greater access to higher education and the support that helps them stay on track. Together, [**donors contributed $7.2 million for scholarships last year**](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/office-of-advancement/your-impact/impact-stories---donors-giving/advancement---donors-giving/thank-you!-you-came-through-for-our-students-like-never-before), helping ease financial pressures and empower students to reach their goals.

* * *

## Why Give to the University of Colorado Denver?

50%

of first-year students are also first-generation students.

70%

of CU Denver students receive financial aid.

Less than 15%

of CU Denver's budget comes from the State of Colorado.

We're the most diverse

research university in Colorado, providing a talent pipeline like no other.

* * *

## DID YOU KNOW?

CU Denver is the only public university in Colorado that is considered highly accessible whose graduates earn on average more than $2 million in lifetime income. This is according to data from the [Carnegie's Student Access and Earnings Classification](https://carnegieclassifications.acenet.edu/carnegie-classification/classification-methodology/2025-student-access-and-earnings-classification/) and from the [Georgetown University Center on Education and the Workforce](https://cew.georgetown.edu/cew-reports/roi2025/).

* * *

## CU Denver Top News

- [**Our Favorite Stories from CU Denver in 2025** ![](https://news.ucdenver.edu/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/S0A8513-1-1288x726.jpg)12/29/2025\\
\\
It’s been a big year. We welcomed our new chancellor, engaged in cutting-edge research on topics that impact … \\
\\
* * *\\
\\
Read More](https://news.ucdenver.edu/our-favorite-stories-from-cu-denver-in-2025/)
- [**Who Has the Best Idea to Improve Denver? CU Denver Business School Launches Sustainability Competition to Find Out.** ![](https://news.ucdenver.edu/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/DSC03586-1288x726.jpg)12/17/2025\\
\\
The new challenge, funded by Dr. Blair Gifford, calls on students and local partners to deliver real-world … \\
\\
* * *\\
\\
Read More](https://news.ucdenver.edu/who-has-the-best-idea-to-improve-denver-cu-denver-business-school-launches-sustainability-competition-to-find-out/)
- [**Festive Activities On and Near Campus** ![](https://news.ucdenver.edu/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/S0A5824-1288x726.jpg)12/15/2025\\
\\
Whether you want to enjoy a warm cider or take in some beautiful holiday lights, there is no shortage of ways to … \\
\\
* * *\\
\\
Read More](https://news.ucdenver.edu/festive-activities-on-and-near-campus/)

* * *

THANK YOU

for supporting the University of Colorado Denver!

## [Office of the Provost](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/provost)

# Karen Marrongelle

## Provost and Executive Vice Chancellor for Academic Affairs

![Karen Marrongelle headshot](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider216/default-album/karen-marrongelle-headshot.jpeg?sfvrsn=d3a0e4b4_1)

Karen Marrongelle, PhD, is the Provost and Executive Vice Chancellor for Academic Affairs and a Professor of Mathematics and Statistics. As Provost, she provides leadership for academic planning and research advancement, working in collaboration with the deans of CU Denver's schools and colleges to ensure academic excellence, promote robust student outcomes, and support CU Denver's mission as a leading public urban research university.

Prior to her appointment as Provost, Karen was the Chief Science Officer and Chief Operating Officer at the U.S. National Science Foundation, where she oversaw scientific director and operations of the federal agency whose mission includes support for all fields of fundamental science and engineering. She also served as Assistant Director of the NSF for Education and Human Resources (EHR), supporting research that enhances learning and teaching to achieve excellence in U.S. science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) education.

Prior to joining NSF, Karen was Dean of the College of Liberal Arts and Sciences at Portland State University, and a Professor of Mathematics and Statistics. At Portland State, she oversaw departments and programs across the humanities, social sciences and natural sciences. She also held positions as the Vice Chancellor for Academic Strategies and as Assistant Vice Chancellor for Academic Standards and Collaboration with the Oregon University System. From 2007-09, Karen researched undergraduate mathematics learning and teaching. She served on a rotation as a program officer at NSF and led numerous grants, collaborating with researchers nationally and internationally to improve undergraduate mathematics education and K-12 mathematics professional development.

Karen has a bachelor's degree in mathematics and philosophy from Albright College, a master's degree in mathematics from Lehigh University, and a doctorate in mathematics education from the University of New Hampshire.

## [Office of the Provost](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/provost)

# Office of Academic Planning

​The Office of Academic Planning oversees and assists in the development of new academic programs, centers and institutes, as well as the periodic review of all departments, schools and colleges, and centers and institutes. The Office also incorporates
the [Office of Institutional Research and Effectiveness](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/institutional-research-and-effectiveness/office-of-institutional-research-and-effectiveness) (OIRE). The Office oversees the processes involved in preparing and maintaining institutional accreditation and serves as liaison with the institutional accrediting agency, the [Higher Learning\\
Commission](https://www.hlcommission.org/) (HLC).

## The Office of Academic Planning oversees:

- [Academic Planning Process](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/provost/academic-planning/academic-planning-process)
- [Academic Program Review](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/provost/academic-planning/academic-program-review)
- [Assessment](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/assessmentoffice/assessment-office)

- [Centers and Institutes](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/provost/academic-planning/academic-program-review/centers-and-institutes)
- [New Degree Program Development](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/provost/academic-planning/new-academic-program-development)
- [Institutional Research and Effectiveness](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/institutional-research-and-effectiveness/office-of-institutional-research-and-effectiveness) (OIRE)
- [Core Curriculum Oversight Committee](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/provost/academic-planning/ccoc) (CCOC)
- [University Curriculum Committee](https://www.ucdenver.edu/faculty-staff/faculty-assembly/denver-campus/denver-campus-committees/faculty-campus-committees/university-curriculum-committee) (UCC)
- [Data Governance and Management](https://www.cu.edu/it-gov/projects/data-governance-management-project)
- [Institutional Accreditation](https://www.ucdenver.edu/university-accreditation)
- CU Denver participation in [GT Pathways](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/provost/academic-planning/gt-pathways)
- [Policy Coordination](https://www.ucdenver.edu/policies/home)

## [University Communications](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/ucomm)

## Getting the Message Out

# CU Denver Media Relations

## Reporter Resources: Experts, Requests, Assets, and More

The CU Denver Communications team is here to help journalists connect with leading experts on a variety of topics, as well as provide story ideas regarding the cutting-edge research and exciting events happening at the university every day.

Our specialists include former print and TV media personnel. We know what it takes to put together a story in today’s news cycle which means we:

- Connect reporters with faculty experts
- Assist crews with filming on campus
- Coordinate open-press events and news conferences
- Provide advisories, photos, and news releases

Below is a sampling of some of CU Denver’s expertise that is timely, topical, and provides solutions to complex local and global issues.

## CU Denver Experts by Topic

### Climate, Disaster Preparedness, and Wildfires COMING SOON

### Engineering & AI COMING SOON

### National Security & Cybersecurity COMING SOON

### Policy & Civic Engagement COMING SOON

### Psychedelics

### Transportation & Infrastructure COMING SOON

## CU Denver Media Assets and Naming Conventions

* * *

#### CU Denver Name Guidelines

**Institutional Naming Guidelines**

In print publications and on the web, the appropriate first reference for the institution is the University of Colorado Denver. On the second reference, CU Denver is the preferred name. After first mention of University of Colorado Denver, titles can
utilize the preferred name.

**For Example:**

- Dr. Robert Hobbins, a professor at the University of Colorado Denver (CU Denver)

* * *

**Referencing Schools, Colleges, Centers, and Institutes**

When referencing a school, college, center, or institute on the campus, the appropriate format is to name the school at the University of Colorado Denver or CU Denver.

**For example:**

- College of Arts & Media at the University of Colorado Denver
- Business School at CU Denver
- Dr. Robert Hobbins, a Business School professor at CU Denver.

* * *

**TV Broadcast Guidelines**

For TV broadcast, the preferred first use is CU Denver on-air and the University of Colorado Denver included in the web story.

#### Key Facts and Stats about CU Denver

**CU Denver Facts**

Download the latest [CU Denver fact sheet](https://photos.ucdenver.edu/s/cfqkwtjh4p9qtjb6k62x78cg) for full details.

- **Founded:** 1973

- **Mascot:** Milo the Lynx
- **Programs:**
  - Seven schools and colleges
  - 150 degrees, 40+ fully online
  - 160+ certificates and microcredentials
  - 15+ research centers
  - 100+ student clubs and organizations
- **Student statistics:**
  - 13,800 total
  - 50% students of color
  - 28% Hispanic
  - 41% undergraduates are transfer students
- **Honors and designations:**
  - #1 in Colorado for social mobility, best professors, most diverse, and best location
  - Asian American Native American Pacific Islander-Serving
  - First Generation-Serving
  - Career-Connected
  - Hispanic-Serving
  - Military Friendly
  - Age Friendly
- **Innovation, creativity, and prosperity**
  - $27.5 million in annual research grant awards
  - 68% of alumni stay in Colorado to power our workforce
  - Faculty and alumni have won Grammy, Oscar, Emmy, and Guggenheim Create Arts awards

* * *

**Boilerplate**

Millions of moments start at CU Denver, a place where innovation, research, and learning meet in the heart of a global city. We’re the state’s premier public urban research university with more than 100 in-demand, top-ranked bachelor’s, master’s, and doctoral degree programs. We partner with more than 13,000 diverse learners—at any stage of their life and career—for transformative educational experiences. Across seven schools and colleges, our leading faculty inspires and works alongside students to solve complex challenges and produce impactful creative work. As part of the state’s largest university system, CU Denver is a major contributor to the Colorado economy, with 2,000 employees and an annual economic impact of [$665 million](https://www.ucdenver.edu/about-cu-denver/our-impact). To learn more about how CU Denver helps learners meet their moment, visit [ucdenver.edu](https://ucdenver.edu/).

#### CU Denver Logos, Campus Images, and Footage

**Public/Media Use Photo Collection**

- Download and use any CU Denver assets that you need. Please include attribution based on what is listed for each. [View Photo/Media Collection](https://photos.ucdenver.edu/university-of-colorado-denver/public-media-use-collection)

* * *

**Request Information**

- Reporters may request interviews, photos, or video assets directly from the [media relations team](mailto:mediatips@ucdenver.edu).

#### Media Filming on Campus



Filming by news media outlets does not require a permit; however please contact the [media relations team](mailto:mediatips@ucdenver.edu) for awareness.


## Navigating CU Denver

* * *

#### Location and Interactive Campus Map



CU Denver is located in the heart of downtown Denver—Colorado's capital and biggest city. In addition to our presence in downtown Denver, CU Denver shares the Auraria Campus with two other separate and distinct institutions of higher learning, Community College of Denver (CCD) and Metropolitan State University at Denver (MSU Denver). For more information about this tri-institutional academic environment, please visit the [Auraria Campus](https://www.ucdenver.edu/life/living-on-around-campus/the-auraria-campus) page.

Explore the [interactive campus map](https://www.ucdenver.edu/about-cu-denver/visit-cu-denver/location-maps) to figure out where you want to go.


#### Where and How to Park at CU Denver



CU Denver is not able to issue free parking passes on the Auraria Campus or near CU Denver buildings within downtown Denver. Almost all parking requires some type of payment or permit. More information on those parking options is provided on the [Auraria Parking and Transportation](https://www.ahec.edu/services-departments/parking) webpage.


#### How to Connect to Campus Wi-Fi

CU Denver has **two Wi-Fi options** depending on where you are located.

- If on the Auraria Campus to the west of Speer Blvd., you can connect to [AurariaNet](https://library.auraria.edu/services/wireless-access#auraria_net).
- If you are in CU Denver buildings in downtown Denver on the east side of Speer Blvd., you can connect to CU Denver Guest.

## Reporting and Records Request

* * *

#### How to Make a Public Records Request



CU’s [policy](https://www.cu.edu/ope/aps/2022) on open records and the [request form](https://www.cu.edu/records/submit-request) are intended to facilitate requests and get you to the right place for the information you seek. Please visit the [University of Colorado Open Records Request](https://www.cu.edu/records) webpage to learn more about the process and the types of information that can be requested.


#### Key University Data and Reporting



The CU Denver [Office of Institutional Research and Effectiveness](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/institutional-research-and-effectiveness) collaborates with campus partners to maintain key campus statistics and provide aggregate-level data and official reporting for the university. Find the latest data, including admissions, student demographics and outcomes, instruction, faculty and staff, finance, and more.


#### How to Confirm Student or Alumni Status

- To confirm student or alumni status, please contact the [media relations team](mailto:mediatips@ucdenver.edu) and include the following information:


  - Full legal name and, if possible, include any previous names used during their time at the institution.
  - Date of birth to help distinguish individuals with similar names.
  - Any academic details you may have such as degree title and major/specialization, dates of attendance, graduation date, or expected graduation date.


- Under FERPA and current CU Denver policy, only items designated by CU Denver as directory information, and only a limited amount of this information, are routinely disclosed by CU Denver university officials. The university retains the discretion
to refuse disclosure of directory information if it believes such disclosure would be an infringement on student privacy rights. Students may ask the university not to publicly disclose directory information.



[Learn more about FERPA](https://www.ucdenver.edu/registrar/student-resources/ferpa#ac-what-is-a-third-party-0) on the CU Denver Registrar’s webpage.

## Meet the CU Denver Media Relations Team

Get story ideas on key issues and topics, plus contact information for CU Denver experts. For help scheduling interviews, please contact the [media relations team](mailto:mediatips@ucdenver.edu).

![Zenaida Gonzalez Kotala](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/default-source/offices-ucomm-images/zenaida-gonzalez-kotala-800-800.jpg?sfvrsn=397dd0bb_2)

### Zenaida Gonzalez Kotala

PIO and Assistant Vice Chancellor for Strategic Communications

![Jennifer Woodruff](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/default-source/offices-ucomm-images/jennifer.jpg?sfvrsn=53105abb_0)

### Jennifer Woodruff

Director of Public Relations and Integrated Media

## Resources for CU Denver Faculty and Staff Public Engagement

Have an interesting and newsworthy story to share with the local, national, or international media? New research about to be published? How about a unique or compelling student or class? Please [email the media relations team](mailto:mediatips@ucdenver.edu).

Have you been approached by a member of the media for an interview?  Want to learn how PR and media relations can enhance your work and the university? We offer media training for faculty, staff, and students. The [media relations team](mailto:mediatips@ucdenver.edu) can help you prepare. You can also use this [quick tip sheet](https://olucdenver.sharepoint.com/sites/ucomm/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2Fucomm%2FShared%20Documents%2FPublic%20Relations%2FMedia%20Training%2FCU%20Denver%20Media%20Training%5FTip%20Sheet%2Epdf&parent=%2Fsites%2Fucomm%2FShared%20Documents%2FPublic%20Relations%2FMedia%20Training&p=true&ga=1) to get you started.

Faculty, staff, and students are always welcome to speak with the media to share their scholarly expertise or personal perspective. If you choose to engage, it is important to clarify whether you are speaking in an academic capacity or from personal experience—not on behalf of the university.

If a reporter is looking for the university’s official position on a topic, please refer them to the CU Denver [media relations team](mailto:mediatips@ucdenver.edu).

#### Op-ed Language

For op-eds or other written materials, one of the following disclaimers is also recommended:

- “The views expressed here are the author’s and do not represent the position, nor speak on behalf of, the University of Colorado Denver and the University of Colorado System.​”

- “The views expressed here are my own and do not represent, nor speak on behalf of, ​the University of Colorado Denver and ​the University of Colorado System.​”

#### Research Communications



If you are working on research which has been accepted for publication in a peer-reviewed journal, please email the [media relations team](mailto:mediatips@ucdenver.edu) as soon as the article is accepted. Do not wait until it is published. Our team will need time to assess and prepare the best form of communication to raise awareness about the impact of your work. Our office follows all federal agency requirements and journal embargoes.


#### Filming on Campus

- Filming by news media outlets does not require a permit; however please contact the [media relations team](mailto:mediatips@ucdenver.edu) for awareness.

- All other filming on university grounds for internal and external purposes is evaluated on a case-by-case basis and requires varying levels of approval and permitting. A location and lease agreement will need to be put in place. **Please reach out at least 3 weeks prior to the intended film date to start the process.**

- Visit the [Filming with a 3rd Party Vendor – Non-University Entity](https://www.cuanschutz.edu/offices/facilities-management/services/film-and-video-requests/film-3rd-party) webpage for more details and to start the process.

- Visit the [Working with CU Denver Photography and Video Services](https://www.ucdenver.edu/offices/ucomm/resources/photography-video-services) webpage for internal production support.

## [Parents and Families](https://www.ucdenver.edu/parents-and-families)

[Register Today!](https://cvent.me/eA4mR0)

## Welcome to the Lynx Family!

The CU Denver Family Engagement office recognizes the important role that parents and families play in supporting our students. We strive to provide you with engagement opportunities and invite you to our community.

We are committed to forming partnerships and providing resources for CU Denver parents, families, and support persons and offer a variety of ways for you to become familiar with current happenings and campus resources. We want to empower you to understand the CU Denver college experience to best support your student.

If you can't find what you're looking for or need additional support, please don't hesitate to contact us!

## Call Us

Reach out to us to speak directly with our faculty

[303-315-5969](tel:3033155969)

## Email Us

Send us your queries and we'll get back to you with all the information you need from our faculty

[Family@ucdenver.edu](mailto:family@ucdenver.edu)

## Visit Us

Student Commons Building

1201 Larimer St.

Suite 1107

Denver, CO 80204

## **Stay Involved**

![Exterior image of the Tivoli Student Union at CU Denver](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider235/default-album/tivoli_web35a648e7302864d9a5bfff0a001ce385.jpeg?sfvrsn=fa8e2cbb_0)

### Newsletter Mailing List

Join our mailing list and stay connected. You’ll receive our monthly newsletter and other university communications. Just drop us an email with your first and last name, and preferred email address.

![Outdoors image of a CU Denver student with their family and Milo](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider235/default-album/family-web.jpeg?sfvrsn=448e2cbb_0)

### Family Advisory Board (FAB)

Want to be part of the conversation and work with us to support your student? Join our monthly Zoom FAB meetings. Each month connect with various departments and get questions answered to help you best support your student!

[Email to Join the Advisory Board](mailto:family@ucdenver.edu)

![Image of a laptop, smart phone, and a cup of coffee on a desk](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider235/default-album/facebook_weba7a648e7302864d9a5bfff0a001ce385.jpeg?sfvrsn=688e2cbb_0)

### Facebook Group

Be sure to follow our Facebook page to learn more about CU Denver. Then, ask to be added to our Parent & Family group. The discussion board is a place for families to ask questions and connect.

[Find Parent & Family on Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/groups/cudenverfamilies)

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

#### What is the Lynx Family?

Lynx Family is the CU Denver Family Engagement office. That is committed to forming partnerships and providing resources for CU Denver parents, families, and support persons and offers a variety of ways for you to become familiar with current happenings and campus resources.

#### How can I get engaged with the Lynx Family?

You can get involve with the activities of Lynx Family by subscribing to our monthly newsletters in English [(Click here)](https://cu.tfaforms.net/321) or Spanish [(Haz clicaquí)](https://cu.tfaforms.net/319), or [email](mailto:family@ucdenver.edu%20[undefined:family@ucdenver.edu]) Family Advisory Board (FAB)  to help direct our mission, plan and volunteer with event and connected with other parents and families, or follow our [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/groups/cudenverfamilies) page and ask to join our Parent and Family group and ask questions in our discussion board and get connected with others.

#### How can I contact Lynx Family?

You can contact us by calling 303-315-3503 or email us at [family@ucdenver.edu](mailto:family@ucdenver.edu), or visit us in person at

Learning Commons

1191 Larimer St.

Suite 2206

Denver, CO 80204

## Partner Offices

![Iconography of a student in a grad cap](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/default-source/orientation-family-engament/fye.jpg?sfvrsn=2fcf23bb_2)

### First-Year Experiences

Courses with a supportive environment for new students and transfer students.

![Iconography of the circular process from school to college](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/default-source/orientation-family-engament/pre-college.jpg?sfvrsn=91c123bb_0)

### Pre-Collegiate

Taught at CU to high-achieving, first-generation students who are planning to attend college.

![Iconography of a map and other location pins.](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/default-source/orientation-family-engament/orientation.jpg?sfvrsn=f9c423bb_0)

### New Student Orientation

Welcoming new students and transfer students to campus with important information.

![Iconography of students in a classroom setting](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/default-source/orientation-family-engament/trio.jpg?sfvrsn=ccc123bb_4)

### TRIO Programs

Helping first-generation, low-income students, and students with disabilities reach their full potential.

## [Parents and Families](https://www.ucdenver.edu/parents-and-families)

## Family Orientation

## **Family & Guest Orientation**

We welcome and strongly encourage family members and guests to participate in Family & Guest Orientation. **Data shows that students are more successful when their support networks are familiar with resources available to them.**

Participating in the Family & Guest Orientation is a way for you as someone who supports and cares for a CU Denver student to get questions answered and learn how to best support your student as they transition to the university. As a support person of a CU Denver student, we rely on you to encourage students to engage academically and socially so they have the best possible chance of making CU Denver their home for the next few years and succeeding in college!

When you attend the virtual orientation, you can expect to hear from a variety of our CU Denver campus partners as they speak to the following content areas and have time for general Q&A:

- Academic Success Tips and Resources

- Important Academic Dates

- Billing Process

- And more!


We offer two Zoom Family & Guest Orientation sessions over the summer. This year Family & Guest Orientation dates are on the dates below

- Monday, June 9th from 12:00 pm - 1:30 pm MT: [RSVP here](https://application.admissions.ucdenver.edu/register/?id=277fa8fa-394f-4e1e-9686-8207d874d063)

- Wednesday, July 16th from 5:30 pm - 7:00 pm MT: [RSVP here](https://application.admissions.ucdenver.edu/register/?id=c3bf4c72-df58-4cb3-bad0-e8d2658aa0b8)


If you are unable to join us on Zoom for these dates, never fear! We record the sessions for your convenience and the recordings will be available below:

#### Family & Guest Orientation Recording

**2025 Family & Guest Orientation Recording**

[https://youtu.be/BC61eu\_sfAY](https://youtu.be/BC61eu_sfAY)

## **CU Denver Spotlight Sessions**

These sessions are designed to address more specific concerns that you may have regarding Housing & Dining, Financial Aid, and the Bursar (Billing Office)

- _March 26, 2025 (5:30 - 6:30 MT), [click here to sign up](https://application.admissions.ucdenver.edu/register/?id=87337d00-dc91-4156-a782-218f41bb2d7f)_
- _May 21, 2025 (5:30 - 6:30 MT)_ _, [click here to sign up](https://application.admissions.ucdenver.edu/register/?id=69e401f5-27ab-4169-901b-cd6752c856a8)_
- _August 4, 2025 (5:30 - 6:30 MT)_ _, [click here to sign up](https://application.admissions.ucdenver.edu/register/?id=3523c4d8-110d-454f-b520-f90ab167b7f7)_

If you miss these sessions, don’t worry! We record the sessions for your convenience and the recordings will be available below:

“Can I Afford to Live on Campus?”:

Are you unsure of the benefits of living on campus? Do you have questions about how financial aid, loans, grants, and scholarships all impact how much your student pays for college? How can I know if I can afford to live on campus? If any of these questions interest you, join us as our Financial Aid, Housing, and Billing office colleagues present to answer these questions and more!

- Wednesday, February 5, 2025: **Watch the recorded session below**


#### On-Campus Housing Spotlight Recordings

**Recording from** **Tuesday, February 5, 2025**!

### [Astrophysics Minor](https://clas.ucdenver.edu/physics/academics/undergraduate-programs/astrophysics-minor)

Astrophysics is an important and well-represented sub discipline in physics. It includes the study of the solar system, galactic and extragalactic astrophysics, as well as cosmology. A minor in this...

Astrophysics is an important and well-represented sub discipline in physics. It includes the study of the solar system, galactic and extragalactic astrophysics, as well as cosmology. A minor in this field combines a theoretical approach with observational analysis. There is opportunity to do research in this field as well. The physics department offers minors in physics, biophysics, and astrophysics. However, it is not possible to receive a minor in more than one of these fields. Also, physics majors may elect to receive the astrophysics or biophysics minor from the department, but not the physics minor.

### [Astrophysics Minor](https://clas.ucdenver.edu/physics/academics/undergraduate-programs/astrophysics-minor)

Astrophysics is an important and well-represented sub discipline in physics. It includes the study of the solar system, galactic and extragalactic astrophysics, as well as cosmology. A minor in this...

Astrophysics is an important and well-represented sub discipline in physics. It includes the study of the solar system, galactic and extragalactic astrophysics, as well as cosmology. A minor in this field combines a theoretical approach with observational analysis. There is opportunity to do research in this field as well. The physics department offers minors in physics, biophysics, and astrophysics. However, it is not possible to receive a minor in more than one of these fields. Also, physics majors may elect to receive the astrophysics or biophysics minor from the department, but not the physics minor.
