## [Student Finances](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances)

### [Financial Aid](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid)

# Your Rights and Responsibilities

## What You Need to Know

### Student Rights

As a student consumer, you have the right to know:

- What [types of financial aid are available](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/types) from all federal, state, and institutional financial assistance programs;
- The deadlines, selection criteria, eligibility requirements, terms, policies, application procedures, and regulations for each program
- The types of financial aid available for study abroad programs
- How your financial need is determined
- [How the estimated cost of attendance is determined for your program of study](https://www.ucdenver.edu/docs/librariesprovider22/financial-aid/view-cost-of-attendance8d2b80e5302864d9a5bfff0a001ce385.pdf?sfvrsn=b003e4b9_0), including tuition and fees, books and supplies, room and board, and any additional costs
- How to maintain eligibility for financial aid, including satisfactory academic progress (SAP) standards required to receive financial aid and the criteria for re-establishing aid eligibility if you fail to meet SAP
- How and when financial aid is disbursed and refunded to students
- How the university provides for Pell eligible students to obtain or purchase required books and supplies by the seventh day of a payment period and how the students may opt out
- [The terms of any student loans you receive as part of your financial aid package](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/types/loans), including the interest rate, total amount that must be repaid, the procedures for paying, the length of time you have to repay, when repayment begins, cancellation provisions, deferment and forbearance possibilities, and any special consolidation or refinancing options
- [The terms and conditions applicable to student employment (work-study)​](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/types/work-study) provided as part of a financial aid package
- [CU Denver's withdrawal policy](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/eligible/withdrawing). The portion, if any, of tuition and fees that are refunded to you if you withdraw by the designated date, and the portion, if any, of the financial aid you must pay back immediately if you withdraw before the end of the term
- You can accept, decline or reduce any or all types of aid
  - If you reduce or decline aid, we may not be able to reverse this action as some funds are limited and distributed on a first-come, first-served basis
  - Accepting more financial aid than necessary is not advised. We will not offer more aid than needed to attend (see how aid is determined); however, you may not need all that is offered. It is in your best interest to only accept what you need
  - Please contact the [Financial Aid and Scholarships Office](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid) if you decide to reduce a loan that has already been disbursed. We will return funds to your loan servicer within 45 days of disbursement. If you reduce a loan more than 45 days after disbursement, you will need to return the funds directly to your loan servicer

### Student Responsibilities

It is your responsibility to:

- Understand and comply with the terms and conditions of aid that you receive
- Use financial aid funds only for education expenses (tuition, fees, room, board, books, supplies and other living costs)
- Respond promptly to all requests for supplemental information or documentation
- Know and meet the deadlines to apply and re-apply for financial aid
- Complete all financial assistance applications and forms with thorough and accurate information
- Read, understand and keep copies of all forms that you are asked to submit or sign
- Notify the Financial Aid and Scholarships Office:
  - If there is any change to information you reported on the FAFSA
  - If there are any changes to your enrollment status or level
  - If you receive any scholarships, grants, or other financial assistance from sources outside the university
- Know and comply with university refund and withdrawal policies
- Maintain [satisfactory academic progress​](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/eligible/sap) to continue to be eligible for financial aid
- Complete [loan requirements](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/apply/loans) if you receive a loan
- Notify your lender of any changes in your name, address, or school status while you are attending school and after you leave school
- Repay any student loans you receive regardless of the fact that you successfully completed your program;
- Perform in a satisfactory manner work agreed upon for Federal Work-study jobs
- Understand that by signing the FAFSA, you agree that the information provided is accurate and complete and that if you purposely give false or misleading information, you may be fined up to $20,000, sent to prison, or both
- Individuals willfully misrepresenting information provided in applying for Colorado student aid funds are subject to penalty of Colorado law

There are four primary forms of financial aid: grants, scholarships, work-study, and loans. Grants come from your higher education institution or the state and federal governments. Grants do not need to be repaid and are usually based on financial need.

Specific to the University of Colorado System, the CU Promise offers eligible students a financial aid award package that includes a combination of grants, scholarships, and work-study that will fund the student’s tuition, fees, and book expenses. Aid is also available for students enrolled in summer school.

Scholarships also do not need to be repaid and can come from government entities, higher education institutions, and private companies. They can be awarded based on need, academic and athletic achievement, community involvement and service, and many other factors.

Work-study allows a student to earn money for college costs while enrolled and working in a part-time job. Loans are money that you or your parents borrow from a bank, private lender, or government. Loans do need to be repaid and will come with interest that sometimes accrues from the day you take out the loan, and sometimes accrues after you receive your degree. Loans from the government usually have lower interest rates and give you a longer amount of time to pay back.

### Grants

Grants are a type of financial aid award that you don’t need to repay. Students must be enrolled at least half-time to receive the grant unless specified.

[Learn More About Grants](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/types/grants)

### The CU Promise

For low-income Colorado residents, CU Denver offers a special financial aid package to fund the student share of tuition, fees, and book expenses.

[See If You're Eligible](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/types/grants/cu-promise)

### Work-Study

Work-study is a financial aid award that allows undergraduate and graduate students to work part-time to earn money to pay for educational expenses.

[Find the Right Fit for You](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/types/work-study)

### Financial Aid vs Scholarships

Both of these options can help you pay for school. Let's look at the differences between them and how they work.

[Learn the Differences](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/types/compare)

### Student Loans

Read about Federal Student Loans and Private Educational Loans. Discover the differences and get to know more about which is better for you.

[Discover Loan Options](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/types/loans)

### Private Loans

If you apply for financial aid, you will likely need to borrow federal and or private student loans as part of your financial aid package. We strongly encourage students to exhaust all federal loan eligibility before borrowing a private student loan as federal loans generally have better terms and conditions.

[Learn about Private Loans](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/types/loans/private)

## [Student Finances](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances)

### [Financial Aid](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid)

# Financial Aid vs. Scholarships

## Financial Aid

Financial aid is available for eligible students to use toward paying educational costs while enrolled in a degree-seeking program. Using the [Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA)](https://fafsa.gov/ "Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA)"),
you will be considered for three types of financial aid:

- Grants
- Work-study
- Student Loans

Separate application forms are not required to be considered for these types of aid. Apply early and apply annually to be considered.

Need-based financial aid eligibility is determined by the FAFSA and the
availability of funding. You must qualify each year to receive need-based awards. Need-based awards include:

- Grants
- Work-study
- Federal Subsidized Direct Loan

For the most part, a grant or gift aid does not need to be repaid. You earn the work-study award through on-campus or off-campus employment. A Subsidized Direct Loan means that the federal government subsidizes (pays) the interest while you are in school.

According to federal financial aid regulations, the total financial aid award cannot exceed the Cost of Attendance.
[The Financial Aid Summary in the UCDAccess is where you can view the Cost of Attendance](https://passport.ucdenver.edu/login.php?page=ucdaccess.php "UCD Access Portal").

## Scholarships

Scholarships are a form of _gift aid_—money that helps pay for college and does not need to be repaid. Institutions and many private organizations offer scholarship opportunities. Each scholarship has its own eligibility criteria and application process. Awards may be based on a combination of factors such as academic achievement, financial circumstances, area of study, community engagement, or other information provided by the donor or program. Students are encouraged to explore all available options and apply for scholarships that best match their interests and experiences.

# Grants

Grants are a type of financial aid award that you don’t need to repay. Students must be enrolled at least half-time to receive the grant unless specified.

**Note**: Each semester, grant aid is "locked" at the main session census date. Grant aid is adjusted based on enrollment at the time of census. If students add classes _after_ themain census date - including “five-week” or “eight-week” sessions - grant aid will not be recalculated.

## Federal Grants

- [Federal Pell Grant](https://studentaid.gov/understand-aid/types/grants/pell)

- [Federal Supplemental Educational Opportunity Grant (FSEOG)](https://studentaid.gov/understand-aid/types/grants/fseog)

- [Iraq and Afghanistan Service Grant](https://studentaid.gov/understand-aid/types/grants/iraq-afghanistan-service)
- [Children of Fallen Heroes Scholarship](https://www.nasfaa.org/news-item/16809/11-19_Children_of_Fallen_Heroes_Scholarship_Act)
- [Teacher Education Assistance for College and Higher Education (TEACH) Grant](https://studentaid.gov/understand-aid/types/grants/teach)
  - In order for CU Denver to determine your eligibility for the TEACH Grant, [complete the Federal TEACH Grant Interest form located on our forms page under _miscellaneous forms_](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/forms).

#### More information about TEACH Grant

The Teacher Education Assistance for College and Higher Education (TEACH) Grant Program provides $4,000 per year in grant funding for qualified students who intend to teach full-time in high-need subject areas for at least four years at schools that serve
students who are from low-income families.

TEACH Grants first disbursed on or after October 1, 2020 and before October 1, 2026 are subject to a 5.7% reduction of the student's calculated award.

The award amounts reflecting the 5.7% reduction are listed below.

- Undergraduate Study: Up to $4,000 per year for first baccalaureate degree to a maximum of $16,000; the award amount with a

5.7% reduction is $3,772.00.
- Graduate Study: Up to $4,000 per year for first graduate degree up to a maximum of $8,000; the award amount with a 5.7%

reduction is $3,772.00.

If students fail to complete the four-year teaching requirement within eight years of completing or ceasing their program of study, the grant reverts to an Unsubsidized Stafford Loan, and will need to be repaid with interest. The interest rate that will
apply to a TEACH Grant that has been converted to a Direct Unsubsidized Loan will be the rate that was in effect for Direct Unsubsidized Loans on the date the TEACH Grant was first disbursed, with the interest charged from the date of each TEACH Grant
disbursement.

**Student Eligibility Requirements:**

To receive a TEACH Grant students must:

- Complete the FAFSA and meet basic eligibility criteria for federal student aid programs, although they do not have to demonstrate financial need
- Be a U.S. Citizen or eligible non-citizen
- Meet one of the following academic achievement requirements:
  - Score above the 75% percentile on a college admissions test (ACT or SAT) OR
  - Graduate from high school with a cumulative GPA of at least 3.25 as a freshman OR
  - Have an earned transfer cumulative GPA of at least 3.25 as a transfer student OR
  - Have a cumulative GPA of at least a 3.25 through the most recent payment period of your college coursework to receive a grant for each subsequent term
- Be enrolled in a program of study designated as TEACH Grant eligible. Eligible programs are those that prepare a student to teach in a high-need area such as Special Education
- Sign a TEACH Grant [Agreement to Serve](https://studentaid.gov/teach-agreement/) and respond to requests by the U.S. Department of Education confirming your continuing intention to meet the teaching obligation by performing the teaching service at a low-income school for a total of at least
four of the eight years following academic program completion
- Complete a TEACH Grant [counseling](https://studentaid.gov/teach-initial-and-subsequent-counseling/) session.

**How to apply:**

You may contact the Financial Aid & Scholarship office and ask for a review of your academic record based on the minimum requirements listed above. If you meet these requirements and are interested in applying for a TEACH Grant you must complete the following steps:

1. Complete and submit a [Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA)](https://studentaid.gov/h/apply-for-aid/fafsa)
2. Complete the [TEACH Grant Interest form](https://dm-unityform.prod.cu.edu/UnityForms/UnityForm.aspx?d1=AdWkiHhYcpNPdjGXuclJ%2b9ZQxtWE2S78QZVnr5oxn9y%2bz%2bJl0HA1SpryqfAbXVzEswn9S8FXBvyzbEdAI8%2fas64r8Oq8y1%2bqPDz6K81XM%2fmbNLJQ8iQDBofjVvsL2A2GYrtG5GjnyCvlfVYxAunUoiYgn1cej2dv1S7DOGAXsX2oSmaQmyFvtiXjn%2fG70J0MVJ6rdFzDWmDDyBmwvKMH1WOIozkMxBI%2fyC2LqW4xfPlsZ2Ch94W%2bSqFr9gapz%2fQwbg%3d%3d).
3. Complete the [Initial Counseling](https://studentaid.gov/teach-initial-and-subsequent-counseling/) for TEACH Grant
4. Sign a TEACH Grant [Agreement to Serve](https://studentaid.gov/teach-agreement/)(ATS)

## State Grants

- Colorado Student Grant - Awarded to eligible undergraduate students who are Colorado residents with documented financial need as demonstrated through the FAFSA, awarded by CU Denver and funded by the Colorado General Assembly. Students must be enrolled in a minimum of six (6) credit hours to receive this award. Students awards are limited to $5,000 annually and dependent on enrollment status.

- Colorado Graduate Grant - Awarded to eligible graduate/professional students who are Colorado residents with documented financial need as demonstrated through the FAFSA, awarded by CU Denver and funded by the Colorado General Assembly. Students must be enrolled in a minimum of four (4) credit hours to receive this award. Student awards are dependent on enrollment status and limited by funding allocation.

## Institutional Grants

- University Need ​Grant - Awarded to eligible Colorado Resident CU Denver undergraduate students with documented financial need as demonstrated through the FAFSA.​ Students must be enrolled in a minimum of six (6) credit hours to receive the University Need Grant.

- Lynx Grant - Awarded to eligible Colorado Resident CU Denver undergraduate students with documented financial need as demonstrated through the FAFSA. Students must be enrolled in a minimum of six (6) credit hours to receive the Lynx Grant.

**Please note:** The Federal Supplemental Education Opportunity Grant, Colorado Graduate Grant, Colorado Student Grant, University Need Grant, and Lynx Grant are offered based on funding availability and are not guaranteed. Complete your financial aid application and any requested documentation as early as possible to be considered for the best funding available.

# The CU Promise

## Putting a College Degree within Reach

The CU Promise began in the fall semester of 2005. The University of Colorado Denver (CU Denver) guarantees that eligible Colorado residents from low-income families will be able to afford the academic costs of a college education without going into debt.

If you are eligible, CU Denver offers a financial aid award package that includes a combination of grants, scholarships, and work-study sufficient to fund the student share of tuition and fees.

Grants and scholarships are awarded on the basis of financial need and/or merit and do not have to be repaid. Work-study is offered as a part of the student's financial aid award. The student is responsible for earning the work-study funds through an on-campus department or approved off-campus agency. [Contact student employment if you have any questions.](https://www.ucdenver.edu/lynxconnect/student-resources/student-employment)

### Eligibility

To be eligible for the CU Promise, the student must:

- Be a Colorado resident,
- Complete their financial aid application process, including the [Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA)](https://fafsa.gov/) by April 1
- Be eligible to receive a Federal Pell Grant award
- Be enrolled each term for 12 credit hours or more

CU Denver is committed to assisting students in their pursuit of a college education. If the student qualifies for The CU Promise, they may still choose to use student loans to cover some of their other educational costs such as room and board. If they don't qualify for The CU Promise, they may still be eligible for enough financial aid to cover the majority of her academic costs.​​​​

[Read more about the Direct Loan Program.](https://studentaid.gov/understand-aid/types/loans) [Read more about Interest Rates and Loan Fees.](https://studentaid.gov/understand-aid/types/loans/interest-rates) **Interest Rates for Direct Unsubsidized Loans\*** | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | Loan Type | **Interest Rate**
**First disbursed on or after July 1, 2025 through June 30, 2026** | **Interest Rate**
**First disbursed on or after July 1, 2024 through June 30, 2025** | | Graduate Unsubsidized Loans | 7.94% | 8.08% | \\* The rate for each loan is a fixed interest rate for the life of the loan. * * * **Loan Origination Fees for Direct Subsidized and Unsubsidized Loans** An origination fee is a percentage of your loan amount charged by the lender for the processing of your loan. Federal student loans have an origination fee; therefore, the amount you may receive as a disbursement may be slightly lower than the amount you accept. | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | Loan Type | **Origination Fee**
**First disbursed on or after October 1, 2024 - September 30, 2026** | **Origination Fee**
**First disbursed on or after October 1, 2023 - September 30, 2024** | | Unsubsidized Loans | 1.057% | 1.057% | **Graduate Student Direct Loan Annual and Lifetime Aggregate Limits** **Annual Unsubsidized Limit:** $20,500 **Lifetime Limit:** $138,500, of which, no more than $57,500 may be from undergraduate Direct Loan limits. ## Federal Graduate PLUS Loan The Federal Graduate PLUS loan is a federal loan that graduate or professional degree students can use to help pay education expenses. The US Department of Education makes Direct PLUS Loans to eligible borrowers through CU Denver's participation in the Federal Direct Loan Program. [Find more information about the Federal Graduate PLUS loan](https://studentaid.gov/understand-aid/types/loans/plus/grad). **Interest Rates for Direct Graduate PLUS Loans\*** | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | Loan Type | **Interest Rate**
**First disbursed on or after July 1, 2025 through June 30, 2026** | **Interest Rate**
**First disbursed on or after July 1, 2024 through June 30, 2025** | | Graduate PLUS Loan | 8.94% | 9.08% | \\* The rate for each loan is a fixed interest rate for the life of the loan. * * * **Loan Origination Fees for Direct Graduate PLUS Loans** An origination fee is a percentage of your loan amount charged by the lender for the processing of your loan. Federal student loans have an origination fee; therefore, the amount you may receive as a disbursement may be slightly lower than the amount you accept. | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | Loan Type | **Origination Fee**
**First disbursed on or after October 1, 2024 - September 30, 2026** | **Origination Fee**
**First disbursed on or after October 1, 2023 - September 30, 2024** | | Graduate PLUS Loan | 4.228% | 4.228% | ## Complete the Loan Process If you accept a Federal Direct Unsubsidized Loans or apply and are approved for a credit-based Graduate PLUS Loan, you will have steps to complete for each loan borrowed. All loan requirements must be completed for the university to apply a loan to your CU Denver tuition. ## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) #### Does a Federal Direct Unsubsidized Loan required a credit check? No. The Unsubsidized Loan does not require a credit chec. [Visit our Complete Loan Process](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/apply/loans/graduate) page for graduate students for the steps to complete the loan process. #### Who is eligible to apply for a Graduate PLUS Loan? A graduate or professional student enrolled at least half-time (3 credit hours) in an aid eligible program leading to a graduate or professional degree or certificate. #### Why can’t I accept the PLUS Loan in my UCDAccess Portal? Graduate PLUS Loans are credit-based loans. To be considered for the loan, graduate students are required to [apply for the loan](https://studentaid.gov/plus-app/grad/landing). Because the credit check is required, the loan is not accepted in the UCDAccess portal.  If you are eligible for the loan based on the credit check, the status will change to accepted in your UCDAccess portal. #### How do I apply for a Graduate PLUS Loan? 1. You may apply for the credit-based loan at [StudentAid.gov](https://studentaid.gov/plus-app/grad/landing). You will use the same FSA ID you use for your FAFSA. 2. Be sure to pick the correct aid year and the correct Loan Period 1. For a Fall – Spring Loan pick Academic Year Loan Period 2. For a summer loan select Summer Only Loan Period 3. If approved for the loan, complete [required loan paperwork](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/apply/loans) 4. The application for each aid year generally opens mid-June the summer before the Fall term. #### What happens after I apply for a Graduate PLUS Loan? - You cannot accept the PLUS loan in your UCDAccess portal. - We will receive notification within 2-3 business days after you have completed a PLUS Loan application. - If you are eligible for the loan, the status will change to accepted in your UCDAccess portal. #### Can I borrow a federal student loan for summer term enrollment? Please visit our [Summer Financial Aid page](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/apply/summer-aid) to learn about summer aid. #### Where can I get more information about Federal Direct Student Loans? Visit [https://studentaid.gov](https://studentaid.gov/) for more information on federal student loans. #### Is there a loan I can borrow if I am not eligible for federal student loans? If you are not eligible due to citizenship status you may want to pursue a credit-based [private student loan](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/types/loans/private). In This Section ### Financial Aid & Scholarships #### Located in Lynx Central CU Denver Student Commons Building 1201 Larimer Street #1107 Denver,CO 80204 * * * [**FinancialAid@ucdenver.edu**](mailto:FinancialAid@ucdenver.edu) **303-315-5969** Top CMS Login SearchSubmit Opens in a new windowOpens document in a new window Chat iconChat with us!

More videos ## More videos You're signed out Videos you watch may be added to the TV's watch history and influence TV recommendations. To avoid this, cancel and sign in to YouTube on your computer. CancelConfirm Share Include playlist An error occurred while retrieving sharing information. Please try again later. The Federal Parent PLUS loan is a federal loan that parents of dependent undergraduate students can use to help pay education expenses. The U.S. Department of Education makes PLUS Loans to eligible borrowers through the CU Denver's participation in the Federal Direct Loan Program. [Find more information about the Federal Parent PLUS Loan](https://studentaid.gov/understand-aid/types/loans/plus/parent). [Read more about Interest Rates and Loan Fees.](https://studentaid.gov/understand-aid/types/loans/interest-rates) **Interest Rate for Parent PLUS Loans\*** | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | Loan Type | **Interest Rate**
**First disbursed on or after July 1, 2024 through June 30, 2025** | Interest Rate
First disbursed on or after July 1, 2024 through June 30, 2025 | | Parent PLUS Loan | 8.94% | 9.08% | \\* The rate for each loan is a fixed interest rate for the life of the loan. * * * **Loan Origination Fees for Direct Parent PLUS Loans** An origination fee is a percentage of your loan amount charged by the lender for the processing of your loan. Federal student loans have an origination fee; therefore, the amount you may receive as a disbursement may be slightly lower than the amount you accept. | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | Loan Type | **Origination Fee**
**First disbursed on or after October 1, 2024 - September 30, 2026** | **Origination Fee**
**F** **irst disbursed on or after October 1, 2023 - September 30, 2024** | | Parent PLUS Loan | 4.228% | 4.228% | ## Complete the Loan Process Parents who choose to borrow a Federal Direct Parent PLUS Loan to assist with educational expenses will have steps to complete for each loan borrowed.  All loan requirements must be completed for the university to apply Parent PLUS Loan funds to your student’s CU Denver tuition. ## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) #### What is a Parent PLUS Loan? - Direct PLUS Loans are loans made to parents of dependent undergraduate students to help pay for education expenses not covered by other financial aid. - Eligibility is not based on financial need, but a credit check is required. #### Who is eligible to apply for a Parent PLUS Loan? - A biological, adoptive parent, or a stepparent that is included on the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) of a dependent undergraduate student enrolled at least half time (6 credit hours). - Grandparents (unless they have legally adopted the dependent student) and legal guardians are not eligible to apply for a Parent PLUS, even if they have had primary responsibility for raising the student. #### How do I apply for a Parent PLUS Loan? - You may [apply for the credit-based loan at StudentAid.gov](https://studentaid.gov/plus-app/parent/landing). - Be sure to pick the correct aid year and the correct Loan Period - For a Fall – Spring Loan pick Academic Year Loan Period - For a summer loan select Summer Only Loan Period - If approved for the loan, complete [required loan paperwork](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/apply/loans) - The application for each aid year generally opens mid-June the summer before the Fall term. #### How do I determine how much to borrow? - Parents can borrow up to the entire cost of attendance for an academic year, minus any financial aid the student accepted or plans to accept. Visit the [Cost of Attendance Estimator](https://www.ucdenver.edu/tuition-cost/cost-of-attendance-calculator) and review all expenses included in your student's cost of attendance.  You student can view their estimated [Cost of Attendance](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/tuition-fees/cost-of-attendance) used for the aid offer in their UCDAccess Portal, and can visit our [How To Page](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/help-resources/guides) for instructions. - Determine which expenses you would like to fund with a Parent PLUS loan. - Review student aid offer for the amount CU Denver charges covered by the student's scholarships. grants and Federal Direct Student Loans. If the student aid does not cover all the CU Denver charges we recommend you add that amount to the additional costs you would like to cover with the Parent PLUS loan. #### What happens after I apply for a Parent PLUS Loan? - We will receive notification within 2-3 business days after you have completed a PLUS Loan application. - If you are eligible for the loan based on the credit check, the Parent PLUS loan will be added to the student your student's UCDAccess portal. #### Where can I get more information about Federal Direct Student Loans? Visit [https://studentaid.gov/understand-aid/types/loans/plus](https://studentaid.gov/understand-aid/types/loans/plus) for more information on Federal Parent PLUS Loans. #### Are there non-federal loan options for my student? - Credit-based [private education loans](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/types/loans/private) are available to student borrowers if federal loan options are either not available or families feel they need additional financing. These loans typically require a student borrower to secure a creditworthy co-signer. We encourage families to exhaust all federal loan options before utilizing this type of loan to assist with educational expenses. ## [Student Finances](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances)

### [Financial Aid](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid)

# Private Student Loans

![Picture4](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider22/finacial-aid/picture4.jpg?sfvrsn=578aa9ba_4)

If you apply for financial aid, you will likely need to borrow federal and or private student loans as part of your financial aid package. We strongly encourage students to exhaust all federal loan eligibility before borrowing a private student loan as federal loans generally have better terms and conditions. Below is some general information about each type of loan. To learn more, [visit our Student Loans page](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/types/loans).

- **Federal student loans** are made by the government, with terms and conditions that are set by law, and include many benefits (such as [fixed interest rates](https://studentaid.gov/understand-aid/types/loans/interest-rates#rates) and [income-driven repayment plans](https://studentaid.gov/manage-loans/repayment/plans/income-driven)) not typically offered with private loans.

- **Private student loans** are non-federal loans, made by a lender such as a bank, credit union, state agency, or a school. These are credit-based loans and the applicant and cosigner will be required to complete a credit application. Private loans cannot be consolidated with federal loans. If you elect to borrow a private student loan, the Financial Aid & Scholarships Office is able to certify the loan up to your [Cost of Attendance](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/tuition-fees/cost-of-attendance) minus any other aid you have received.

* * *

## Before Applying for Private Student Loans

**Determine Eligibility for Federal, State and Institutional Aid**

[Complete the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA)](https://studentaid.gov/h/apply-for-aid/fafsa) to determine your eligibility for federal, state, and institutional aid. Review your Student Aid Report (SAR) and respond promptly to any requests for additional information from the Financial Aid & Scholarships Office. We strongly encourage students and families to [learn about the differences in Federal vs. Private student loans](https://studentaid.gov/understand-aid/types/loans/federal-vs-private) in order to make an informed decision.

Eligible undocumented students who do not meet the [basic eligibility criteria](https://studentaid.gov/understand-aid/eligibility/requirements) for federal student aid, but who qualify for [Colorado ASSET (Advancing Students for a Stronger Economy Tomorrow)](https://cdhe.colorado.gov/sites/highered/files/Revised%20ASSET%20Leg%20General%205.28.pdf) are encouraged to [complete the Colorado Application for State Financial Aid (CASFA)](https://cdhe.colorado.gov/students/preparing-for-college/colorado-application-for-state-financial-aid) to determine your eligibility for aid through the State of Colorado.

**Research and Apply for Scholarships**

Scholarships are gifts, and in most instances, do not have to be repaid. [Visit our Scholarships page for information on applying for scholarships through CU Denver](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/scholarships).

**Determine How Much To Borrow**

- [Review your financial aid offer in UCDAccess](https://www.ucdenver.edu/redirects/f5-redirects/ucdaccess)(if applicable).
- [Compare your financial aid offer to your Estimated Cost of Attendance (COA)](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/tuition-fees/cost-of-attendance).
- Remember that the total amount of aid you can receive from all sources, including scholarships, grants, work-study, and loans, cannot exceed the COA.


We strongly encourage you to exhaust all federal, state, and institutional aid sources before applying for a private student loan. Students and families may [learn more about the differences in Federal vs. Private student loans](https://studentaid.gov/understand-aid/types/loans/federal-vs-private)in order to make an informed decision.

* * *

## Ready to choose a lender and apply for private loans?

Students may apply for private or alternative loan programs through their preferred lenders. Private loans will not be certified for
more than the student's cost of attendance minus other financial resources that the student is receiving. Further, students are
subject to the criteria as determined by the individual lender.

It is recommended for students to apply for federal aid before applying for private or alternative loans to be informed on all
available educational financing options. Other restrictions or requirements may apply in order for a private or alternative loan to
be disbursed. Students are encouraged contact Financial Aid & Scholarships Office for more information regarding the
requirements to obtain a private educational loan.

You may review and compare lender offerings using these links:

- [Undergraduate Private Loan Review & Comparison Tool](https://www.elmselect.com/link/query?schoolId=967&programTypeId=1)
- [Graduate Private Loan Review & Comparison Tool](https://www.elmselect.com/link/query?schoolId=967&programTypeId=2)
- [Parent Private Loan Review & Comparison Tool](https://www.elmselect.com/link/query?schoolId=967&programTypeId=11)
- [MBA Program Private Loan Review & Comparison Tool](https://www.elmselect.com/link/query?schoolId=967&programTypeId=7)
- [International Student Private Loan Review & Comparison Tool](https://www.elmselect.com/link/query?schoolId=967&programTypeId=5)

### Ready to Apply?

Have you already selected a lender and are now ready to apply for your private student loan?

[Learn About Applying](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/types/loans/private/applying)

[Skip to content](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/types/loans/private/applying#CUMain)

## [Student Finances](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances)

### [Financial Aid](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid)

# Applying for Private Loans

![Picture3](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider22/finacial-aid/picture3.jpg?sfvrsn=648aa9ba_4)

Students may apply for private or alternative loan programs through their preferred lenders. Private loans will not be certified for more than the student's cost of attendance minus other financial resources that the student is receiving. Additionally, private loans certification requests may not be completed until a student is registered for courses in the term. Further, students are subject to the
criteria as determined by the individual lender.

It is recommended for students to apply for federal aid before applying for private or alternative loans t be informed on all available
educational financing options. Other restrictions or requirements may apply in order for a private or alternative loan to be disbursed.
Students are encouraged contact Financial Aid & Scholarships Office for more information regarding the requirements to obtain a
private educational loan.

Use school code **004508**.

Use the Loan Term Dates listed below to apply for the appropriate loan period.

You may review and compare lender offerings using these links:

- [Undergraduate Private Loan Review & Comparison Tool](https://www.elmselect.com/link/query?schoolId=967&programTypeId=1)
- [Graduate Private Loan Review & Comparison Tool](https://www.elmselect.com/link/query?schoolId=967&programTypeId=2)
- [Parent Private Loan Review & Comparison Tool](https://www.elmselect.com/link/query?schoolId=967&programTypeId=11)
- [MBA Program Private Loan Review & Comparison Tool](https://www.elmselect.com/link/query?schoolId=967&programTypeId=7)
- [International Student Private Loan Review & Comparison Tool](https://www.elmselect.com/link/query?schoolId=967&programTypeId=5)

## Loan Period Dates

![colorful graphic design of a person using a computer with other technology devices around them](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider22/finacial-aid/winter.png?sfvrsn=d089a9ba_4)

### Fall & Spring

August 1 - May 30

![colorful graphic design of a person using a computer with financial instruments around them](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider22/finacial-aid/fall.png?sfvrsn=4e89a9ba_4)

### Fall Only

August 1 - December 20

![colorful graphic design of a person using a computer with presentation materials around them](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider22/finacial-aid/spring.png?sfvrsn=c88aa9ba_4)

### Spring Only

January 15 - May 20

![colorful graphic design of a person on the phone using a computer](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider22/finacial-aid/summer.png?sfvrsn=3a89a9ba_4)

### Summer Only

June 1 - July 30

**Borrower Rights**

The Higher Education Opportunity Act of 2008 (HEOA) added section 128(e)(3) to the Truth in Lending Act (TILA) which requires private loan lenders to obtain a completed and signed Self-Certification Form from the applicant. In addition, the private loan lender must provide disclosures to borrowers at different stages of the application process. Please contact us for a copy of the Self-Certification Form if your lender does not provide one.

[**Know Your Rights!**](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/policies/rights-responsibilities) Read and understand your rights and responsibilities before you borrow a loan.

**Applying for a Loan to Pay a Prior Term (Past Due) Balance**

Certain private loan lenders offer loans to cover a past due balance from a prior term or academic year. Many lenders will allow you to apply up to 6 months after the past due balance is created. However, you should check with your lender as the time-frame to apply ranges from one month to one year.

CU Denver must receive the lender’s certification request for prior term balance loan no later than September 15th of the following academic year. CU Denver will only certify prior term balance loans to cover an outstanding balance on your CU Denver student account.

* * *

Next Steps

### After Applying for Your Private Loan

The Financial Aid & Scholarships
Office will notify you by email once we have received and reviewed the
private loan certification request from your lender. The loan amount
processed by the Financial Aid & Scholarships Office may be less
than the amount you requested if you have not accepted or declined your
other loans, or if the requested amount exceeds your COA. You may
contact our office to discuss the loan amount before the loan
certification process is completed.

You will be required to complete [Private Loan Entrance Counseling](https://www.ucdenver.edu/docs/librariesprovider22/financial-aid/private-student-loans/2021-2022-private-loan-entrance-counseling.pdf?sfvrsn=818aa2ba_2) before the certification request is processed.

Loan
funds will be sent to the university and will be applied to any
outstanding balance due on your student account. Any remaining funds
will be sent to you by the Bursar’s Office via direct deposit or mail.

**Loan Increases**

Once
our office has certified an approved private loan, we will rarely be
able to increase the loan amount. In most cases, you will need to apply
for a new loan.

**Loan Reductions and Cancellations**

- If
we have not notified you that we received the private loan
certification request from your lender, please work with your lender to
reduce or cancel the loan.

- If we have notified you that we received the private loan certification request, you will need to [complete a](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/forms) [Loan Adjustment Form](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/forms).

- If
you receive additional aid after your private loan is certified, our
office may need to adjust the amount of your private loan. We will
notify you if your loan needs to be adjusted.

* * *

What now?

### Apply for Scholarships

Investing the time to apply for scholarships can really pay off. See what's available.

[Search Scholarships](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/scholarships)

### Stay Up-To-Date

Learn more about important dates, deadlines, disbursement, and refund schedules.

[View Important Dates](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/help-resources/important-dates)

### Additional Resources

View instructional guides, checklists, and video demonstrations to keep you on track.

[Find More Resources](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/help-resources/guides)

# Choosing a Lender

![Picture2](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider22/finacial-aid/picture2.jpg?sfvrsn=e88aa9ba_4)

Top 10 Things to Consider When Evaluating a Lender


1


### Does the lender offer a fixed or variable interest rate?


2

3


### Can I get a better interest rate by applying with a cosigner?


4


### Does the lender offer a cosigner release option?


5


### Can I reach a live representative during hours convenient for me?


6


### What is the lender’s rating with the Better Business Bureau?


7


### When am I required to start making payments?


8


### What are the repayment plans for the loan?


9


### What options are available if I have trouble making payments?


10


### Is there a penalty for paying the loan off early?

**Credit Check**

Students and cosigner must meet the lender’s established minimum credit criteria. Lenders may consider a number of factors when determining loan eligibility such as credit-worthiness and debt-to-income ratio. Students and cosigners are encouraged to [obtain a free copy of their credit report annually from AnnualCreditReport.com](https://www.annualcreditreport.com/cra/index.jsp).

**Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP)**

Some private lenders require that students meet the [Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP) Standards](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/eligible/sap) to be eligible for a private loan. The purpose of SAP is to ensure academic success and graduation. Students who do not meet the SAP standards may not be eligible for their private loan. To learn more, review our SAP Policies:

[Skip to content](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/types/loans/proration#CUMain)

## [Student Finances](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances)

### [Financial Aid](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid)

# Student Loan Proration

When an undergraduate student’s final
period of study before graduation is less than an academic year, the
annual Federal Direct Subsidized and Unsubsidized Loan amounts must be
prorated. Proration may result in reduced or increase loan eligibility
based on enrollment. Typically, this rule impacts seniors who are
graduating in December.

**Three Final Periods of study less than an academic year:**

- Student will graduate at the end of the Fall semester
- Student does not attend the Fall semester, and will graduate at the end of the Spring semester
- Student does not attend Fall or Spring semesters, and will graduate at the end of Summer term

**How is the prorated loan amount calculated?**

The formula provided by the federal government is below.

![credit hours enrolled, divided by 24, equals the prorated percentage, multiplied by the annual loan limit](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider22/finacial-aid/proration-table.png?sfvrsn=342e9aba_16)

In some cases, this proration may result in reduced loan eligibility,
while enrollment could also result in increased loan eligibility.

# Undergraduate Student Loan Proration Calculator

Based on your FAFSA are you dependent or independent? DependentIndependent

How many credits are you required to take to complete for graduation?6789101112131415161718192021222324

Calculate

**What other factors could affect the loan amount offered?**

- The actual loan amount may be less if you have reached your annual or lifetime loan limits prior to your graduation semester.
- The amount could also be less if your remaining Cost of Attendance (COA), after all other aid is subtracted, is less than the prorated loan amount.

**What to do if you will no longer be graduating:**

Your
academic advisor must email FASO to let us know you have not met
graduation requirements for your current degree(s) and must tell us what
courses are required for you to graduate.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ):

#### I am graduating in the Fall. Can I have all my loan funds in the Fall?

If you are an undergraduate, your
Federal Direct Loans must be prorated based on the number of credit
hours for which you are enrolled.

#### Who has loans prorated?

Graduating
undergraduate students who are only attending one term of the academic
year will have their Direct Loans prorated based on the number of credit
hours they are enrolled.

**Note:** Graduate and professional students are excluded from the loan proration requirement

#### When is proration required?

See Three Final Periods of study less than an academic year above

#### Which loans are prorated?

Federal Direct Subsidized and Unsubsidized Loans. Note: Parent PLUS Loans are not prorated.

#### What should I do if I am no longer graduating that term?

Your academic advisor must email FASO to
let us know you have not met graduation requirements for your current
degree(s) and must tell us what courses are required for you to
graduate.

Your loan funding may be impacted in Spring semesters if loans were prorated in the Fall with Fall graduation anticipated.

#### When does loan proration occur?

Adjustments to
loan amounts may also occur after the term census date if you have
changed enrollment or if you are enrolled in more than 12 credits. If
your enrollment changes before census and you are enrolled in fewer
credits, you may owe money back to the university.

markdown:
[Skip to content](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/types/loans/undergraduate#CUMain) ## [Student Finances](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances) ### [Financial Aid](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid) # Undergraduate Student Loan Information CU Denver participates in the Federal William D. Ford Direct Loan program, and the U.S. Department of Education is the lender. A Federal Direct Loan may be Subsidized or Unsu​bsidized. Subsidized Loans are available to undergraduate students with financial need. Unsubsidized Loans are available to undergraduate students regardless of need. The amount and loan type you may be offered is based on your grade level, your previous borrowing, your cost of attendance and other financial aid you receive. [Read more about the Direct Loan Program.](https://studentaid.gov/understand-aid/types/loans) [Read more about Interest Rates and Loan Origination Fees.](https://studentaid.gov/understand-aid/types/loans/interest-rates) **Interest Rates for Direct Subsidized and Unsubsidized Loans\*** | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | Loan Type | **Interest Rate**
**First disbursed on or after July 1, 2025 through June 30, 2026** | **Interest Rate**
**First disbursed on or after July 1, 2024 through June 30, 2025** | | Undergrad Subsidized and Unsubsidized Loans | 6.39% | 6.53% | \\* The rate for each loan is a fixed interest rate for the life of the loan. * * * **Loan Origination Fees for Direct Subsidized and Unsubsidized Loans** An origination fee is a percentage of your loan amount charged by the lender for the processing of your loan. Federal student loans have an origination fee; therefore, the amount you may receive as a disbursement may be slightly lower than the amount you accept. | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | Loan Type | **Origination Fee**
**First disbursed on or after October 1, 2020 - September 30, 2026** | **Origination Fee**
**First disbursed on or after** **October 1, 2022 - September 30, 2023** | | Undergrad Subsidized and Unsubsidized Loans | 1.057% | 1.057% | **Direct Loan Borrower Annual and Lifetime Aggregate Loan Limits** **Dependent Student Direct Loan Annual and Lifetime Aggregate Limits** | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Year | Academic Credits Passed | Annual Subsidized Limit | Annual Unsubsidized Limit | | Freshman | 0 - 29 | $3,500 | $2,000 | | Sophomore | 30 - 59 | $4,500 | $2,000 | | Junior+ | 60+ | $5,500 | $2,000 | **Lifetime Limit:** $31,000, of which, no more than $23,000 may be subsidized. * * * **Independent Student Direct Loan Annual and Lifetime Aggregate Limits** | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Year | Academic Credits Passed | Annual Subsidized Limit | Annual Unsubsidized Limit | | Freshman | 0 - 29 | $3,500 | $6,000 | | Sophomore | 30 - 59 | $4,500 | $6,000 | | Junior+ | 60+ | $5,500 | $7,000 | **Lifetime Limit:** $57,500, of which, no more than $23,000 may be subsidized * * * ## Complete the Loan Process One of the many benefits of Federal Direct Student Loans offered to undergraduates is that the loans are not credit-based. If you accept Federal Direct Subsidized or Unsubsidized Loans, you will have steps to complete each year. All loan requirements must be completed for the university to apply a loan to your CU Denver student account. ## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) #### How do I apply for a federal student loan? - You must first complete a Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) for the academic year in which you wish to borrow.  For example, if you complete the 2022-2023 FAFSA you will have completed the correct FAFSA for Fall 2022, Spring 2023, and Summer 2023. - If you are offered a federal student loan as part of your financial aid offer, visit our [Complete the Loan Process page](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/apply/loans/undergraduate) for further guidance #### What is the difference between a subsidized and unsubsidized loan? - A **Subsidized Loan** is only available to undergraduate students and the interest is subsidized. This means that the government pays all the interest that accrues while the student remains enrolled at least half time in school, during the grace period, and during any authorized deferment periods. Eligibility is based on need. - An **Unsubsidized Loan** is not subsidized and the borrower is responsible for all of the loan's interest from the moment the school applies the funds to your tuition account.  This type of loan is not based on need. #### Can I borrow a loan for Summer? Please visit our [Summer Financial Aid page](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/apply/summer-aid) to learn about summer aid. #### What can I do if the aid I was offered does not cover my costs? Parents of dependent undergraduate students may apply for [Parent PLUS Loan](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/apply/loans/parents) to help pay for education expenses not covered by other financial aid. Independent students and students whose parents are unable to borrow a Parent PLUS Loan, may pursue a credit-based [private student loan](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/types/loans/private).  These loans typically require a student borrower to secure a creditworthy co-signer. We encourage families to exhaust all federal loan options before utilizing this type of loan to assist with educational expenses. #### Is there a loan I can borrow if I am not eligible for federal student loans? If you are not eligible due to citizenship status you may want to pursue a credit-based [private student loan](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/types/loans/private). #### Where can I get more information about Federal Direct Student Loans? Visit [https://studentaid.gov](https://studentaid.gov/) for more information on federal student loans. [Photo image of University Of Colorado Financial Aid & Scholarships](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCJ0Zcpw7Dl4TU_3QQ1xi7FQ?embeds_referring_euri=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.ucdenver.edu%2Fstudent-finances%2Ffinancial-aid%2Ftypes%2Fwork-study)

Work-study is a financial aid award that allows undergraduate and graduate students to work part-time to earn money to pay for educational expenses. The program encourages community service work and work related to the recipient's course of study. Students earning work-study funds are paid bi-weekly. Students may search open positions on the [Handshake Job Board](https://ucdenver.joinhandshake.com/login). In order to be awarded work study, students must submit a Work Study Request form and a Letter of Intent to hire. Funding may be limited.

Students can be awarded Federal or Colorado Work-study.

## Federal Work-Study

Federal work-study is need-based financial aid awarded with Federal funding. To be eligible a student may be a graduate or undergraduate student, and must demonstrate financial need as determined by the FAFSA. Students employed in off-campus positions are only eligible for Federal work study, if they meet all other eligibility requirements.

## Colorado State Work-Study

Colorado State Need based Work-study – Awarded with state funding. To be eligible a
student must be an undergraduate with no prior degree, and a Colorado
resident. The student must demonstrate financial need as determined by
the FAFSA.

Colorado State Non-need based Work-study – Awarded without regard to need and funded by the state.
To be eligible a student must be an undergraduate with no prior degree,
and a Colorado resident. To be eligible the student must complete the
FAFSA.

Colorado work-study is funded by the Colorado General Assembly.

* * *

## How to Apply for Work-Study

1.  Complete your [Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA)](https://studentaid.gov/h/apply-for-aid/fafsa)
2. Search open positions on [Handshake Job Board](https://ucdenver.joinhandshake.com/explore)
1. Mention your interest in work study
2. Eligible students are sent an email from the Financial Aid and Scholarships office confirming potential eligibility. This may be shared with employers to establish work study positions.
3. Complete and submit a [Work Study Authorization Form](https://www.ucdenver.edu/docs/librariesprovider22/financial-aid/student-employment/2025-2026/work-study-authorization-form_2025f80f6ce8302864d9a5bfff0b001ce385.pdf?sfvrsn=fed236b4_6) once you have been offered a position.  You or your supervisor can submit this form to the Student Employment Office. Students are only awarded work study funds once this form is complete and received by the Financial Aid and Scholarships Office.
4. Watch your [UCDAccess student portal](https://www.ucdenver.edu/redirects/f5-redirects/ucdaccess) and email for updates on your award.
5. Once awarded, start working!

## [Click here for a download of our "How-To" guide!](https://www.ucdenver.edu/docs/librariesprovider22/financial-aid/student-employment/how-to-apply-for-work-study.pdf?sfvrsn=b8e977b4_3)

* * *

## How to Complete the Work Study Authorization Form

Once you have been offered a position, work with your employer to complete the [Work Study Authorization form](https://www.ucdenver.edu/docs/librariesprovider22/financial-aid/student-employment/2025-2026/work-study-authorization-form_2025f80f6ce8302864d9a5bfff0b001ce385.pdf?sfvrsn=fed236b4_6). This is needed to review your work study eligibility and offer an award.

Please reference our [Work Study Authorization Form Help Guide](https://www.ucdenver.edu/docs/librariesprovider22/financial-aid/student-employment/2025-2026/work-study-authorization-form_2025_markupversion.pdf?sfvrsn=5d23fcb4_1) for more details and clarification on how to complete this form prior to sending it to Student Employment.

* * *

## Work Study Forms

## 2025-2026 Work Study Authorization Form

## 2025-2026 Work Study Increase Request form

* * *

## Work Study FAQs

#### Am I Eligible for Work Study?

Students are generally eligible for work study by:

- Completing a [Free Application for Federal Student Aid](https://studentaid.gov/h/apply-for-aid/fafsa) (FAFSA) and has demonstrated [financial need](https://studentaid.gov/help-center/answers/article/what-does-financial-need-mean)\*
- Enrolled at least half time per semester
- Maintain good academic standing

  - Undergraduate: 2.0 GPA
  - Graduate: 3.0 GPA

_\*income changes, year in school, household size, etc can impact your need._

CU Denver students who are eligible for work study are automatically sent an email detailing their eligibility. This email can be shared with prospective employer indicating initial eligibility.

#### I was denied work study, why?

There are many reasons a student may be denied work study and may be unique to your situation. Here are some of the following reasons a student may be denied a work study award:

- You have not been employed or been offered employment with an on-campus employer or an eligible off-campus employer
- You have not completed a FAFSA or required additional documentation
- You have not demonstrated financial need
- You do not have room in your financial aid package, better known as your [Cost of Attendance](https://studentaid.gov/help-center/answers/article/what-does-cost-of-attendance-mean)
  - additionally, you may have **not** selected the checkbox on the Work Study Authorization form allowing the Student Employment office to reduce or cancel loan offerings to make room in your financial aid package.
- You did not meet GPA requirements
- You are not enrolled a minimum of half time

Please note, we do not share the reason for denial with your employer.

If you have any specific questions on why you may have been denied that is not answered in the list above, please reach out to [Student Employment](mailto:studentemployment@ucdenver.edu).

#### Do I need a job before I apply for Work Study?

Yes, it is recommended to apply and be offered a position before applying for a work study award. Students are notified of their work study eligibility via email prior to a job being secured.

You may search for jobs on [Handshake](https://app.joinhandshake.com/login). Once a job is secured, you can work with your employer to complete a [Work Study Authorization Form](https://www.ucdenver.edu/docs/librariesprovider22/financial-aid/student-employment/2025-2026/work-study-authorization-form_2025.pdf?sfvrsn=4f2708b4_1) to finalize a work study award as part of your financial aid package.

#### What can I spend my Work Study on?

Work study is wages earned through work. You will be paid directly through payroll for the hours you have worked. You can use these funds to help cover education expenses, housing and food, etc.

Students are paid according their the Payroll Calendar which can be accessed [here](https://www.ucdenver.edu/docs/librariesprovider22/financial-aid/student-employment/2025-2026/2025-2026-student-employment-bi-weekly-payroll-calendar.pdf?sfvrsn=92348b4_1)!

#### Can I still work on-campus if I don't have a work study award?



Yes! There are several on-campus (and off-campus) jobs that do not require work study.


#### How do I search for a job? What is Handshake?

[Handshake](https://ucdenver.joinhandshake.com/) is CU Denver's platform for searching and posting student employment jobs. Students can create a profile and begin finding all sorts of jobs!

Handshake is managed by the [Career Center at Lynx Connect](https://www.ucdenver.edu/lynxconnect/career-center). Please reach out to their office for additional help with Handshake.

#### Can I request additional work study funds?

Generally yes, students who have already been awarded work study can request additional funds. However, work study award increases are only possible if work study allocations remain for the university. There is no guarantee that if you request additional funds that you will receive more.

To request additional funds, please complete a Work Study Increase request form which can be found on this page.

#### How much does Work Study cover for wages? (For Employers)

For the 2025-2026 year, work study will cover 100% of earned wages. Departments do **not** need to account for 25.5% at this time. _This is subject to change in future fiscal years._

100% of wages earned **after** a student's work study award is exhausted is the responsibility of the employing department.

![Illustration of a planner with a printer and envelop representing the forms process](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider22/finacial-aid/forms-card.png?sfvrsn=ace6f3b9_4)

Jump on Handshake and search job openings. Check back often!

# Scholarships

Investing the time to apply for scholarships can really pay off. There are opportunities based on merit, talent, personal stories, creativity, and more. Apply now before deadlines start to hit.

[Apply for Scholarships](https://ucdenver.scholarshipuniverse.com/)

![Illustration of a dollar sign standing on top of books](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider22/finacial-aid/am-i-eligible-for-aid-card.png?sfvrsn=72e5f3b9_2)

### What are scholarships?

Learn more about the difference between scholarships, grants, and loans and other financial aid. Scholarships are free money – money given to you for your education by a higher education institution, state or federal government, or a private company or organization that you don’t have to pay back

[Compare Financial Aid Types](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid/types/compare)

![Illustration of a submission scren](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider22/finacial-aid/apply-for-aid-card.png?sfvrsn=f3e5f3b9_2)

### Search and Apply for Scholarships

At CU Denver, searching for scholarships has never been easier. We have one tool to support you in searching and applying for scholarships. Our Scholarship Universe site allows you to apply for scholarships offered by CU Denver and private donors.

[Go to Scholarship Universe](https://ucdenver.scholarshipuniverse.com/)

![Illustration of a coin bouncing into a mason jar full of coins and dollars](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider22/billing-and-payments/student-debt?sfvrsn=ca52f5b9_4&download=true)

Get tips on how to apply and become an outstanding scholarship applicant.

[Get tips and tricks!](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/scholarships/scholarship-application-tips)

![Milo's paw holding a phone showing a bill](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider22/billing-and-payments/pay-your-bill?sfvrsn=7251f5b9_4&download=true)

### Upcoming Events!

Search collaboration and connection events with upcoming workshops and webinars!

[See Upcoming Events](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/scholarships/scholarship-events)

![Illustration of a calendar with money in the foreground](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider22/billing-and-payments/billing-calendars?sfvrsn=1a51f5b9_4&download=true)

### Schedule an Appointment

Use this appointment link to speak to a Scholarship Advisor!

[Get tips and tricks!](http://ucdenver.navigate.eab.com/)

![Types-of-Aid-Card](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider22/finacial-aid/types-of-aid-card88cc97e5302864d9a5bfff0a001ce385.png?sfvrsn=47e4f3b9_0)

### Complete a FAFSA

Even if you aren't sure what you qualify for, it is recommended to complete a FAFSA to find other scholarship and grant opportunities!

[Complete a FAFSA](https://studentaid.gov/apply-for-aid/fafsa/filling-out)

![Scholarships Graphic](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider22/finacial-aid/global-education-vector-275955-vector-art-at-vecteezy.png?sfvrsn=434ed5b4_0)

### Nationally Competitive Scholarships

Nationally competitive scholarships are prestigious awards that recognize students' academic and leadership potential. These scholarships are typically merit-based and open to students nationwide. Many of these scholarship programs offer the opportunity to study abroad. Examples include: Fulbright, Rhodes, and Marshall Scholarships.

![Coins in front of a dollar](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider22/finacial-aid/types-of-aid-cardeacc97e5302864d9a5bfff0a001ce385.png?sfvrsn=25e4f3b9_2)

Each year, CU Denver undergraduate students are awarded over $30 million in scholarships from institutional, local, state and national sources. Most of these funds are institutional provided through our schools and colleges. Local, state, and national undergraduate sources can be found at My Colorado Journey.

## [Student Finances](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances)

### [Scholarships](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/scholarships)

# Scholarship Application Tips

## We're here to help you maximize your award!

- You should apply for scholarships
early and often. You don’t have wait until you’ve been accepted by a
college or university to apply for scholarships. In fact, you can start
applying for scholarships sometimes as early as a year before you start
your academic journey in higher education

- As with any
deadline, pay close attention to scholarship application deadlines. You
will be disqualified for eligibility if you miss the submission
deadline

- If you find you’ve missed a deadline for a scholarship
you had your eye on, keep looking – there are hundreds of scholarships
out there, all with different deadlines

- Don’t discount the
smaller scholarships – a few hundred dollars can offset costs, and being
awarded more than one smaller scholarship adds up quickly

- You
don’t have to be an incoming freshman to qualify for scholarships. Be
sure to investigate scholarships each year you’re in college. Nearly 50
percent of available scholarships are for students already in college

- Many
scholarships are based on financial need and academic achievement, but
there are many others that have different eligibility requirements such
as community service, first-generation, race and ethnic identity,
community involvement, major or minor degree program, age, special
talents and hobbies, or memberships in organizations


## Submitting Applications

- Submit your scholarship application on time


- Make sure you are qualified for ALL scholarship criteria
  - Are you in the field of study for which the scholarship is intended, if applicable?
  - Do you meet the minimum test scores or grade point average, if applicable?
- Assemble ALL requested information in the order in which it was requested, if one is specified

- Include at least the minimum number of recommendations

- Make your application and essays as neat as possible

- Make your application and essays clear. Need some help? Check out the [CU Denver Writing Center](https://clas.ucdenver.edu/writing-center)
- If requested, reference all community service work and community affiliations

- [If required, complete the FAFSA for financial aid](https://studentaid.gov/apply-for-aid/fafsa/filling-out)

## Stay Organized

- When retrieving letters of recommendation, obtain multiple
copies and have each signed in ink. Letters of recommendation may not be
considered valid if the signature is copied and not signed

- Always save copies of your essays for future reference

- Make an ongoing list of activities and affiliations that you are involved in to refer to when completing applications

- Taking time on your application conveys that you will take time and do well in the endeavor you are pursuing

## Ready to apply?

## [Student Finances](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances)

### Tuition & Fees

When a student begins researching higher education institutions, tuition
is often the first stop. Undergraduate tuition costs differ for
in-state students – students with Colorado residency – and
out-of-state students – those attending CU Denver from outside Colorado. Tuition and fees are based largely on the classes you take, which school or college offers your classes, and your
resident status. You must understand [your residency status](https://www.ucdenver.edu/registrar/residency) because it impacts more than
just tuition rates.

Tuition is the amount a student pays for instruction per course at a college or university. Tuition costs are often listed along with additional fees that all students must pay. These fees cover anything from the student wellness center and AHEC facility operation costs to student support services.

Tuition
and fees are approved by the University of Colorado Board of Regents,
the governing body of the University of Colorado, and may change from
those shown here without prior notice. Where appropriate, the rates reflect the [College Opportunity Fund](https://www.ucdenver.edu/registrar/student-resources/college-opportunity-fund) discounted rate.

The College Opportunity Fund (COF) is a stipend offered to eligible Colorado residents attending a public college/university or a participating private institution in Colorado. It isn’t considered financial aid and doesn’t figure into any financial aid packages offered by the college or university. The stipend pays a portion of your total in-state tuition and is paid directly to your institution on a per-credit-hour basis. This means that you will not receive a check in the amount of the COF, rather, you will see a deduction on your tuition bill. The credit-hour amount is set annually by the Colorado General Assembly. [Please check the official State College Opportunity Fund website for the current amount.](https://cof.college-assist.org/) ​​​​​​​​​

### Undergraduate Student Tuition

Your main cost of attending college is tuition and fees. Our tuition rates are calculated by credit hour and your cost per credit hour is based on what kind of student you are; undergraduate or graduate student, which school or college you attend, and your residency status.

[Check Undergraduate Rates and Fees](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/tuition-fees/undergraduate)

### Tuition Appeals

Sometimes a student will need to withdraw from classes due to circumstances behind their control, including health conditions or a death in the family. When that happens, students can file for a tuition appeal. Be sure to know the guidelines, process, and deadlines for applying.

[Learn About Tuition Appeals](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/tuition-fees/tuition-appeals)

### Graduate Student Tuition

Graduate students, like undergraduate students, are charged both tuition and fees. Tuition is charged per credit hour and is calculated based on the rate per credit hour determined by each school or college. Tuition rates are also based on residency status.

[See Graduate Tuition and Fees](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/tuition-fees/graduate)

### WUE

If you’re an out-of-state student who is a resident of a state that participates in the Western Interstate Commission for Higher Education (WUE), you’re in luck! WUE students receive a tuition discount of up to $11,000. This includes all undergraduate programs but only applies to on-campus classes.

[See If You Qualify for WUE](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/tuition-fees/wue)

### Cost of Attendance (COA)

So, what will it cost you to attend CU Denver? We can help you figure out an estimate for a semester or the nine-month academic year. Check the Cost of Attendance Calculator to calculate direct costs, like tuition, and indirect costs like books and personal expenses.

[Add Up Your Costs](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/tuition-fees/cost-of-attendance)

### Billing Calendars

Bills and payments, tuition and fees. It's all part of attending college. The key is staying on top of deadlines and understanding the billing cycle. The semester billing calendar outlines them all, starting before the semester begins and continuing until the final payment due date.

[Check Billing Dates and Deadlines](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student/student-calendars/billing)

## Additional Tuition and Fee Information

#### Non-Degree Undergraduate Student Tuition



Tuition is charged by course at the resident rate for non-degree
undergraduate students who are Colorado residents, and at the standard
non-resident undergraduate rate for non-degree undergraduate students
who have not established in-state residency. Tuition is assessed by
course at the international non-resident rate for non-degree
undergraduate students based on VISA permit status.​


#### Non-Degree Graduate Student Tuition



Tuition is charged by course at the resident rate for non-degree
graduate students who are Colorado residents, and at the standard
non-resident graduate rate for non-degree graduate students who have not
established in-state residency. Tuition is assessed by course at the
international non-resident rate for non-degree graduate students based
on VISA permit status. Please note that non-degree graduate students enrolled
in undergraduate courses (Not COF Eligible) are subject to
undergraduate rates based on the school administering the course.


#### Tuition for Courses Taken for No Credit

Tuition for courses taken for no credit (NC) is the same as for courses taken for credit.


#### Fractional Credit



Fractional credit is regarded as 1 hour in assessing tuition and fee charges.


#### Mandatory Student Fees

Students
are subject to **full** student fees when enrolled in a mix of online or remote courses, and one or more in-person or hybrid course.

Students enrolled in a mix of online and remote courses are assessed the Student Services Fee and the Mental Health and Wellbeing Fee.

Extended Studies online and remote courses are assessed Student Services Fee, Mental Health and Wellbeing Fee, Student Information System (SIS), and Information Technology fees.

All new degree-seeking students will be subject to the one-time **New Student Fee**. The International Student fee will be assessed to students dependent on their visa status.

Students
enrolled in Continuing Education courses off-campus are only
assessed the Information Technology and Student Information System (SIS) Fee(s). However, students enrolled in courses taught on campus or
concurrently enrolled in both Continuing Education and Denver main
campus courses located on campus are subject to full student fees.​​

#### Non-Resident Online Tuition

Beginning in Fall 2021, the “Out-of-State, Online-Only (OOSOO)” tuition rate is being eliminated for new students and replaced by the "Nonresident Online" tuition rate, as described below. Students enrolled before Fall 2021 assigned the “out-of-state online only” residency exception as their most recent tuition residency will continue to receive this discounted tuition until completion of the degree provided that all criteria for that residency continue to be met:

- Enrollment in a qualifying fully-online degree
- Out-of-state address for the following:
  - Mailing
  - Home
  - Local
- Enrollment in only online course

**"Non-Resident Online" Tuition Rate:**

If you are not a resident of the state of Colorado and are only taking online courses, you may be eligible for the Non-resident, online tuition rate offered through the University of Colorado Denver. The rate of 120% applied to main campus courses only and excludes non-degree-seeking programs and extended study courses.

Audits will run regularly to ensure that students with the Nonresident Online tuition exception continue to meet these criteria. Students who no longer meet the eligibility criteria will receive an email informing them that they no longer qualify for the Non-resident online tuition rate. If this happens during an academic term, the change in residency will come into effect the following term. Students who have lost this tuition exception will be ineligible to reapply for the exception through completion of their current degree level (bachelors, masters, etc.).

**Billing Process:**

If registered for an online-only program and you live outside of Colorado, please, note the following:

- Tuition will be billed as "Nonresident".
- All online registered students will have their registration reviewed by the Registrar’s Office
  - The review can take up to 30 business days.
- Registrars will determine that all criteria above are met.
- The next billing statement will reflect the change in tuition to the non-resident online tuition rate.

**NOTE:**  Upon registration, you will be charged at the "non-resident" rate. Your account will be reviewed by the Office of the Registrar, and the correct rate will be applied to your account before the first due date of any given semester.

For more information about the Non-Resident online tuition rate please, contact the Office of the Registrar via phone at: 303-315-5969 or via email: [residency@ucdenver.edu](mailto:residency@ucdenver.edu)

For additional questions please visit: [Tuition and Financial Aid \| CU Online - University of Colorado](https://online.cu.edu/tuition)

### Bursar's Office

#### Student Billing
