# Scholarships

A college degree is an investment, and there are generous organizations and donors who are ready to help invest in your future, too. Each year, CU Denver undergraduate students are awarded more than $30 million in scholarships and grant packages from
institutional, private, local, state, and national sources. Overall, about 72% of full-time undergraduate students with financial need who apply for aid receive a need-based scholarship or grant award every year.

Even if you or your family members have done it before, earning a scholarship can seem like a complicated and confusing process at times. But at CU Denver, we are here to support you in finding the right assistance to fund your education. Remember, a
bill should not be a barrier to success.

For information about International Students Financial Aid & Scholarships, please visit the [International Admissions](https://www.ucdenver.edu/international-admissions/costs-financial-aid) website.

## Apply for Scholarships

Though you may be automatically considered for certain scholarships based on your admissions application, admitted students can also search for additional scholarships at Scholarship Universe, which includes information about many CU Denver scholarships and their
requirements.

## Displaced Aurarian Scholarship

The Displaced Aurarian Scholarship Program provides former Auraria residents and their descendants scholarships to all programs offered on the Auraria campus.

[Scholarship Details & Eligibility](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/scholarships/displaced-aurarian)

## By the Numbers

$88K+

Average accepted aid by undergraduate full-time students.

$13K+

Average yearly total of financial aid received by undergraduate students.

## Contact the Financial Aid & Scholarships Office

The Financial Aid & Scholarships Office helps students achieve their educational goals by administering federal, state, and institutional aid, which includes scholarships, grants, work-study, and student loans.

[Visit the Financial Aid & Scholarship Office](https://www.ucdenver.edu/student-finances/financial-aid)

## [Undergraduate Admissions](https://www.ucdenver.edu/undergraduate-admissions)

### First-Year Students

​​​​Your college experience is more than
just four years and a piece of paper. You'll be applying to CU Denver as a first-year college student, and you know what you want. You want a rich educational
experience that lets you learn, grow, and make an impact. You want
classes that challenge you, a campus that embraces you, a city that
empowers you. And some mountains wouldn't hurt.

## What Defines a First-Year Student?

Anyone applying for admission to a college or university for the first time after graduating from high school, or earning a General Educational Diploma (GED), is a first-year student for admission purposes. Students who completed college courses while still in high
school may have credits that will be considered for transfer toward their college degree, but they still apply as first-year students.

## What First-Year Students Need to Know

![ACT and SAT scores next to a diploma](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/librariesprovider207/default-album/admission-requirements5a3994e5302864d9a5bfff0a001ce385.jpg?sfvrsn=9511f0b9_0)

Start planning. Schedule orientation, apply for scholarships, explore student housing, get to know Lynx Central.

## [Undergraduate Admissions](https://www.ucdenver.edu/undergraduate-admissions)

### [First-Year Students](https://www.ucdenver.edu/undergraduate-admissions/first-year)

# First-Year Admission Requirements

[Apply Now](https://login-slate.ucdenver.edu/slate?sr=dec2d4b1-c538-49c6-b662-c6f6eac9d837)

As one of the most academically accomplished student bodies in the state, CU Denver offers an environment where ambition thrives. ​Our students are among the best and brightest - motivated individuals who are driven to pursue their passion. The middle 50% of our admitted freshmen had between a 3.23 and 3.93 GPA and scored between a 1070 and 1260 on the SAT, 21-27
on the ACT. CU Denver students are some of the most academically talented in the state. [Learn more about what it will take to join their ranks as a \\
CU Denver student](https://www.ucdenver.edu/docs/librariesprovider207/default-document-library/gpa-admission-test-scores.pdf?sfvrsn=7f04eab9_2).

**Please note:** _Submitting test scores is optional, and won't negatively impact your application. [Review our FAQ](https://www.ucdenver.edu/undergraduate-admissions/first-year/help-and-resources) for more information._

Not sure if you should apply as a **first-year** or [transfer student](https://www.ucdenver.edu/undergraduate-admissions/transfer)?
Let's start with this. Have you previously taken college classes? You may have heard the term concurrent enrollment. If your only college coursework was taken concurrently, which means while you were still enrolled in high school before you graduated,
you're in the right place. Your application will be evaluated for admission as a first-year student.

## General Admission

General admission applies
to programs in the College of Liberal Arts and Sciences (CLAS), the School of
Public Affairs (SPA), and the College of Arts & Media (CAM), except for music.

**Several criteria indicate you're a strong candidate for general admission.**

- Your GPA and test scores fall into or above our middle 50%
- [You've completed all HEAR requirements](https://www.ucdenver.edu/undergraduate-admissions/first-year/requirements#HEAR)
- [You have no more than two MAPS unit deficiencies](https://www.ucdenver.edu/undergraduate-admissions/first-year/requirements#MAPS)

## Higher Education Admission Requirements (HEAR)

The
state of Colorado Higher Education Admission Requirements (HEAR) apply
to students graduating from high school in 2008 or later. The HEAR
requirements are specific courses or units that applicants for admission
should have completed by the time they graduate from high school. College courses taken
while still in high school may be considered toward fulfilling these
requirements.

**High school course or unit requirements are different depending on graduation year.**

Class of 2008 or 2009

- Four years of English
- Three years of mathematics (Algebra I or higher)
- Three years of social studies (including one year of U.S or World History)
- Three years of natural science (two of which are lab-based)
- Two years of academic elective

Class of 2010 or Later

- Four years of English
- Four years of mathematics (three of which must be Algebra I or higher)
- Three years of social studies (including one year of U.S. or World History)
- Three years of natural science (two of which are lab-based)
- One year of a single foreign language
- Two years of academic elective

## General Educational Development (GED)

If you did not graduate from high school, request official GED test scores be sent directly from the certifying agency to the CU Denver Office of Admissions. **CU Denver's minimum GED consideration threshold is a 145 total score and a 145 in each of the four content areas.**

## ​Minimum Academic Preparation Standards (MAPS)

The
University of Colorado Minimum Academic Preparation Standards (MAPS)
apply to students who graduated from high school in 1988 or later.
Though completion of most MAPS requirements is expected by high school
graduation, units not completed may be made up by taking specific CU
Denver courses.

- Four years of English (at least two years of composition)
- Three years of mathematics (four years for business and engineering students)
- Three years of natural science
- Two years of social science
- Two years of foreign language (same language)
- One year of academic elective​

## Still have questions? Admissions counselors have answers.

**Appointments** are strongly encouraged to ensure guaranteed time with an admission counselor. **Walk-Ins** are available during business hours, subject to admission counselor availability.

Monday through Friday, 8:00am – 5:00pm

14,947 Students Strong

- Average Entering ACT Score: 23.2 Composite
- Average Entering SAT Score: 554 Math, 559 Verbal
- Average High School GPA: 3.5


Estimate your cost of attendance.

Tuition and fees are based largely on the classes you take, and which school or college is offering your classes, as well as your resident status.

Your journey starts with the first step.

**1\. Start Planning**

Complete the online application.

**2\. Apply Online**

Include all requested information and complete all the steps.

**3\. Send Official Documents**

Send your official documents with your transcript.

**4\. File your FAFSA**

The first step in applying for financial aid is to complete the FAFSA.

[**How to Apply for First-Year Admission**](https://www.ucdenver.edu/undergraduate-admissions/first-year/apply)

[**How to Apply for Transfer Admission**](https://www.ucdenver.edu/undergraduate-admissions/transfer/apply)

The things you'll do.

Make connections.

Want to enhance your college experience? Students who participate in activities tell us they’re more successful, get better grades, feel a greater sense of belonging, and are more likely to graduate.

Discover new passions.

Try something new. You can join more than 100 student organizations at CU Denver or create your own. Meet people, network, grow as a leader, improve your time management skills, and just have fun.

Make wellness a priority.

Your wellness is our priority at CU Denver. It’s not just fitness, the pool, rock climbing, or club sports. Our 7 Dimensions of Wellness support your body and your mind. And, wait till you see our wellness center!

The places you'll see.

One visit. That's all it takes.

Our education corridor takes you straight into the heart of Denver with all of the professional opportunities that await. This is the urban campus you’re looking for with the Colorado Rocky Mountains right in our backyard.

Adventure is calling.

This is Colorado. You want to get out there and we have the experiences you’re looking for. Outdoor Adventures include hiking, biking, paddle boarding, even week-long excursions, and everything in between.

Grab your passport.

Take time to expand your world, make international connections, and discover the benefits of global education. You can study abroad or do an international internship and apply those credits toward your degree.

And the friends you'll make along the way.

Welcome home.

Live where you learn.

We call our residence hall Lynx Crossing and it puts you in perfect position to get the most from your academic and social life, and explore all that the City of Denver has to offer.

We've got your six.

Successful transitions for our veteran and military students are a priority. Need a tutor, professional career mentor, or a community you can count on? Veteran & Military Student Services has your back.

You are welcome here.

International students make up 7% of our student population. You enrich our community and overcome unique challenges. We welcome you to CU Denver and we’re here for you.

You worked hard to get here.

## [Undergraduate Admissions](https://www.ucdenver.edu/undergraduate-admissions)

# Fresh Start Program

The University of Colorado Denver believes that sometimes it’s nice to have a fresh start. We designed our Fresh Start program to provide previous CU Denver students who left the university with a cumulative GPA below 2.0 or on academic probation or suspension with the opportunity to return to CU Denver to complete their degree. Students re-admitted under this program will start a new CU Denver GPA. This program is only applicable to CU Denver courses and only on the CU Denver campus.

## Am I eligible?

Previously enrolled undergraduate degree-seeking CU Denver students who left the university with lower than a 2.0 cumulative GPA and who have been away from the university for three consecutive academic terms (e.g., spring, summer, and fall) are eligible to apply for the Fresh Start program.

## How does it work?

1. You take at least one full academic year (three terms—e.g., spring, summer, and fall) away from CU Denver in order to gain perspective and reflect upon your previous experience.  During your time away, you will have readied yourself to
    return to CU Denver with a fuller understanding of the challenges you faced and strengthened priorities to move you forward.

2. Submit a degree-seeking application for admission.

3. Your application will be reviewed and you will receive communication from the Office of Admissions that will direct you to your academic advising office.

4. You will then discuss your next steps to Fresh Start and select courses for your intended major with your advisor. In the meantime, please review the Fresh Start and Financial Aid (link above).

5. After meeting with your advisor and reviewing the Fresh Start and Financial Aid document, you will opt into the Fresh Start program by submitting the Fresh Start Statement of Understanding Form, which you will receive from your advisor. If you anticipate
    needing financial aid support, you are strongly encouraged to meet with a financial aid advisor before opting into the program.

## Deadlines

University application deadlines are listed below. All application materials must be submitted within a week of the deadline to be considered for that academic start term.

- Summer 2025: May 23, 2025
- Fall 2025: July 31, 2025
- Spring 2026: January 2, 2026

# University Accreditation

* * *

Smart Badge

[![Verify hlc Membership](https://cdn.yoshki.com/hlc/validation/150/0/_logo.png)![Verify hlc Membership](https://cdn.yoshki.com/hlc/validation/150/0/-logo.png?a=121*012801280128@55012601$01090=@=901270=@123@550=@1090126)![Verify hlc Membership](https://cdn.yoshki.com/hlc/validation/150/0/_logo.png)](https://yoshki.com/validate.aspx?104108099+118097108105100097116105111110+108111103111046112110103+104116116112115058047047119119119046117099100101110118101114046101100117047117110105118101114115105116121045097099099114101100105116097116105111110)

[Close Popup](https://cdn.yoshki.com/iframe/54732.html)

[Close Popup](https://cdn.yoshki.com/iframe/54732.html)

Loading...

The University of Colorado Denver (CU Denver) and the University of Colorado Anschutz Medical Campus (CU Anschutz) is a two-campus consolidated entity [accredited by the Higher Learning Commission (HLC)](https://www.hlcommission.org/component/directory/?Action=ShowBasic&Itemid=&instid=1040) and is known by its authorized name, the University of Colorado Denver (the “University”), and by its working name, the University of Colorado Denver \| Anschutz Medical Campus.

## History of University Consolidation and HLC Accreditation

In 2004 the University of Colorado Board of Regents approved a consolidation of the University of Colorado Health Sciences Center (abbreviated as UCHSC) and the University of Colorado at Denver (abbreviated as UCD). This single entity became known as
the University of Colorado at Denver and Health Sciences Center (abbreviated as UCDHSC). The schools and college previously referred to as UCHSC now comprise the Anschutz Medical Campus.

Prior to their consolidation, UCHSC and UCD were accredited as independent institutions by the HLC. Prior to 1980 the University of Colorado Health Sciences Center was accredited as part of the University of Colorado system. The Health Sciences Center
underwent comprehensive reviews by the HLC in 1980, in 1988, and in 1998. In December 2004, the consolidated university submitted a report to the HLC requesting that the Commission consider UCDHSC as one accredited institution. The HLC team that conducted
a focused visit in January 2005, recommended approval of this change and the HLC subsequently approved this change.

In October 2007 the consolidated institution became known as the University of Colorado Denver. This was the authorized name not only with the HLC but with other entities also. We currently also use University of Colorado Denver \| Anschutz Medical Campus
as a working name for the university.

In April 2011, the University of Colorado Denver submitted a self-study encompassing both the Denver and Anschutz campuses and subsequently underwent a single and successful comprehensive visit to both campuses. Its accreditation was continued for a further
ten years.

In 2021, the HCL reaffirmed the accreditation of the University of Colorado Denver for another ten-year period. The next comprehensive evaluation is scheduled for 2030-31.

For complete information on University of Colorado Denver accreditation, see the [Statement of Accreditation Status](https://www.hlcommission.org/component/directory/?Action=ShowBasic&Itemid=&instid=1040) on
HLC’s website.

## [Wellness & Recreation](https://www.ucdenver.edu/wellness)

## Our Facilities

The Lola & Rob Salazar Student Wellness Center features three levels of unique and flexible spaces. From a spa to study rooms, gym courts to a patio, this 85,000-square-foot center gives you places to exercise, socialize, study or relax.

Boost your strength and stamina with our climbing wall and bouldering cave. Kick back in our lounge and catch up on homework. Then rest your mind and body in the Wellness Suite, which includes reflection rooms, a food pantry and more.

![Photo of 38-Foot Climbing Wall on the second floor of the wellness center](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/default-source/wellnesscenter-images/38-foot-climbing-walle136f8e7302864d9a5bfff0a001ce385.png?sfvrsn=2e1e9cbb_0)

### 38-Foot Climbing Wall

![Week6_facility-2137](https://www.ucdenver.edu/images/default-source/wellnesscenter-images/week6_facility-2137.jpg?sfvrsn=5f08adb9_0)

### The Wellness Suite

# Milo the Lynx Mascot

Named for our "Mile High City" and "LoDo" neighborhood, Milo the Lynx not only cheers on our [Club Sports](https://www.ucdenver.edu/wellness/programs/sports/club-sports) teams but also brings energy to student and alumni events and joins in university celebrations. He's even been known to bust out some impressive dance moves.

You'll see Milo on campus and around the city of Denver. You can't miss his big, fluffy ear tufts. But, like all lynx, he moves quickly – so be on the lookout!

## Invite Milo

Infuse your event with school spirit. Milo's not just for sports!

[Request Milo](https://forms.ucdenver.edu/secure/request_milo)

## Become Milo

Ever wondered who Milo is behind the scenes? It could be you!

[Become Milo Today](https://ucdenver.edu/wellness/about/employment#competitivesports)

### Questions?

**Contact us at:**

milo@ucdenver.edu

_Milo requests cannot be made through email, all requests must be made through the [Milo Request Form](https://ucdenverdata.formstack.com/forms/request_milo)_
